## TASKS A GENERER -- SCOPES DETAILLES

### Task 4.1.1 -- Architecture Base + Decision-015 + Design Doc

**Densite cible** : 100-140 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : Sprint 13 closure | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.2 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task est le **gateway architectural** du Sprint 14 : avant toute implementation, la decision-015 doit etre formellement ratifiee + un design doc detaille 30+ pages doit etre redige + peer-review obligatoire Saad CTO + Abla CEO. Sans cette validation upfront, les 11 tasks suivantes risquent de partir dans une mauvaise direction (Sprint 14 est l'inflection point architectural critique de la plateforme Skalean - une erreur ici impacte toutes Phases 3-9). La task rationalise le choix architectural X-Road inspired versus alternatives (API Gateway centralized Kong/Tyk/Apigee, message bus pub/sub Kafka all-to-all, service mesh Istio/Linkerd) avec analyse trade-offs explicite + benchmarks production X-Road Estonie (23 ans operation), Finlande (Suomi.fi), Islande (Ísland.is), Japon (NHS-RJ), Argentina (eX-Road).

Livrables principaux : fichier `decisions/015-skalean-exchange-layer-xroad.md` ratification formelle 8-12 pages avec sections (titre + date 30 mai 2026 + status Accepte + contexte business + decision + alternatives evaluees detaillees + consequences positives/negatives + revision schedule), fichier `docs/architecture/skalean-exchange-layer-design.md` design doc 30+ pages comprenant diagrams architecture haute niveau (4 composants), deployment topology Atlas Cloud DC1/DC2, security model PKI mTLS + JWS signing flow detaille avec sequence diagrams, audit log model hash chain cryptographic, migration plan Sprint 12 adapters etape par etape, roadmap evolution Phase 7+ (chained CA, service mesh production-grade), risk analysis matrix, fichier `00-pilotage/reports/phase-3-kickoff-report.md` rapport ouverture Phase 3 documentant cloture Phase 2 5 sprints transverses + objectifs Phase 3 (3 sprints SEL + Resolution + Sinistre Foundation) + risques majeurs + jalons critique, peer-review obligatoire Saad CTO + Abla CEO avec signatures dates cote document avant impl.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Decision-015 trop vague : "pattern X-Road" sans details architecturaux precis. Solution : detailer 4 composants explicit + interfaces + protocols + flows avec sequence diagrams (Mermaid markdown), peer-review obligatoire avant 4.1.2.
- Alternatives mal evaluees : presenter X-Road comme seul choix valide sans analyser alternatives. Solution : tableau Markdown 4+ alternatives avec colonnes (pattern, pros, cons, scaling cost, time-to-implement) + justification choix explicite.
- Design doc trop technique inacessible non-tech : Abla CEO doit comprendre pour ratifier. Solution : section "Executive Summary" 2 pages debut doc + diagrams clairs + glossaire termes techniques en annexe.
- Peer-review skip pour aller vite : "on commencera impl et on documente apres". Solution : audit trail dedie peer-review obligatoire dans 4.1.1 closure, audit log signature Saad+Abla avant commit 4.1.2.
- Diagrams ASCII non scalable : difficile maintenir + lire. Solution : Mermaid markdown diagrams (rendus natif GitHub + GitLab + tools tiers), sources versionnees.
- Glossaire absent : SEL/PKI/mTLS/JWS/X-Road termes confusing newcomers. Solution : annexe glossaire 1 page debut doc avec definitions claires + references docs externes.
- Migration plan Sprint 12 superficiel : "on migre les adapters" sans plan etape par etape. Solution : plan detaille avec 4 phases (preparation + adaption + cutover + cleanup), durations + responsables + risks per phase.
- Roadmap Phase 7+ absent : decision-015 sans vision long terme. Solution : section "Future Evolution" 3-4 pages documenter chained CA (Tanmia/Globalsign), service mesh production-grade Istio future, blockchain real audit log Phase 8+, integrations BAM/CNIE/ANRT.

### Task 4.1.2 -- Skalean Exchange Hub Deployment + Infrastructure

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 8h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.1 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.3 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task deploie le composant Exchange Hub central sur Atlas Cloud Benguerir DC1 Tier III avec replication async DC2 Tier IV. Le Hub est le composant central qui maintient le Service Registry, route les messages inter-organismes, applique rate limits per partenaire, gere circuit breaker, et publie events Kafka pour audit log centralise. Hub utilise NestJS server port 9443 mTLS-only (pas de plaintext HTTP authorise), Postgres pour registry persistence avec RLS active, Redis pour rate limit counter + circuit breaker state, Kafka pour events publishing. Deployment via Terraform IaC pour reproducibilite + disaster recovery.

Livrables principaux : creation package `@insurtech/skalean-exchange-hub` avec NestJS skeleton port 9443 mTLS-only configure (refuse plain HTTP connections, valide cert client X.509 sur handshake), Dockerfile multi-stage build optimise (base node:20-alpine, layer caching, image finale < 200 MB), extension `docker-compose.yml` Sprint 1 avec service `exchange-hub` ports 9443:9443 + volumes persistents Postgres/Redis/Kafka + healthcheck command, migration `CreateExchangeHubEntities` avec 4 tables (`partners` avec colonnes id/name/type enum gov/insurance/internal_tenant/banking/etc/is_internal_tenant/status/created_at, `services` avec colonnes id/partner_id/service_name/version/description/auth_method enum mtls_only/mtls_oauth2/mtls_jws_signed/etc/is_active, `service_endpoints` avec colonnes id/service_id/url/weight/health_status enum healthy/degraded/unhealthy/maintenance/last_health_check_at/circuit_breaker_state, `service_rate_limits` avec colonnes id/service_id/limit_per_minute/limit_per_hour/limit_per_day/is_active), indexes performance (tenant_id partout, partner_id+service_name composite, health_status pour queries health-check), RLS Postgres active sur tables tenant-scoped (cross-tenant queries internes Skalean uniquement super-admin), seed initial 6 partenaires (DGI gov+is_internal=false, ACAPS gov, AMC gov, AssurMaghreb insurance fictional, Atlas Insurance fictional, MediAssur fictional) avec endpoints initiaux placeholders (URLs remplis par tasks suivantes 4.1.8 fictional + 4.1.7 migration governmental), NestJS health-check endpoint `/health` retourne 200 si Postgres + Redis + Kafka up, configuration deployment Atlas Cloud Benguerir DC1 via Terraform script `infra/terraform/exchange-hub/main.tf` provisioning VM + Postgres managed + Redis managed + Kafka managed, replication async DC2 configuree via Postgres logical replication + Redis Sentinel + Kafka MirrorMaker2 pour failover automatique < 30s, telemetry Prometheus exporter `/metrics` exposing routing_requests_total/error_rate/latency_seconds, 12+ tests integration deployment + health-check + failover DR.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Hub mTLS-only refuse plain HTTP : developpeurs locaux confus si HTTP non-secure pas autorise. Solution : env dev mode `EXCHANGE_HUB_DEV_MODE=true` accepte plain HTTP localhost only (avec warning log), production strict mTLS-only enforced.
- Postgres RLS active sur Hub partners : super-admin Skalean cross-tenant queries necessaires. Solution : permission `exchange_layer.admin.read.cross_tenant` distinct du tenant-scoped, controllers admin utilise pattern bypass RLS via `SET LOCAL ROLE postgres_admin` audit log strict.
- Replication async DC2 lag : si DC1 crash, DC2 peut perdre ~1-30s donnees. Solution : commit Postgres synchrone-replication DC2 acceptable performance (sync_commit=remote_apply pour critical tables registry), Redis Sentinel auto-failover < 5s, Kafka MirrorMaker2 lag tracking.
- Terraform state files multi-environnement : dev/staging/prod separation. Solution : workspaces Terraform isoles state per env, backend S3 Atlas Cloud per workspace, lock dynamoDB equivalent.
- Hub port 9443 conflict avec autres services : verifier port disponible. Solution : env var `EXCHANGE_HUB_PORT` configurable default 9443, conflicts detection at startup.
- Dockerfile node:20-alpine missing native deps : node-forge ou crypto packages echec build. Solution : `RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++` pour compilation native deps, multi-stage final image clean.
- Health-check trop simple : `/health` retourne 200 mais Postgres down silencieux. Solution : health-check actif test queries (`SELECT 1 FROM partners LIMIT 1`), Redis PING, Kafka topic list, failure si l'un fail.
- Seed initial idempotent : re-run seed casse data existante. Solution : `INSERT INTO partners VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name, updated_at=NOW()`, audit log changes.

### Task 4.1.3 -- Skalean CA + Certificate Issuance

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE SECURITE | **Effort** : 7h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.2 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.4 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente la PKI Skalean CA infrastructure qui emit certificats X.509 mTLS aux tenants et partenaires connectes. La CA root est self-signed initial Sprint 14 (acceptable pour deploiement initial avec partenaires manuellement onboarded), evolution Phase 7+ vers chained CA reconnu autorite tierce marocaine (Tanmia, Globalsign Maroc). La criticite securite est maximale : si CA root private key fuite, attaquant peut emit certs falsifies et impersonate tenants/partners, compromission complete plateforme. Storage Vault avec audit log access strict + co-signature multi-personne required pour CA root setup + rotation manuelle annuelle.

Livrables principaux : creation package `@insurtech/skalean-ca` complet avec services + utils + tests, service `CaRootService.initialize()` execute one-time setup (genere CA root self-signed RSA 4096 bits + Common Name "Skalean Root CA 2026" + validity 10 ans + storage Vault path `secret/skalean/ca-root-key` chiffre AES-256-GCM + audit log critique requires co-signature Saad CTO + Abla CEO + DSI + CISO via 4 signatures Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow avant procede), service `CertificateIssuanceService.issueCert(targetType: 'tenant' | 'partner', targetId: string, validity_days: number = 365): IssuedCertificate` emit cert client X.509 signe par CA root + private key returned ONCE to caller (jamais stocke cote CA pour confidentialite caller), service `CertificateRevocationService.revoke(certSerial: string, reason: RevocationReason)` revocation via CRL (Certificate Revocation List) publiee endpoint public `https://ca.skalean.internal/crl.pem` consultable verifiers, service `CertificateRenewalService` CRON job daily 02h UTC scan certs expirant dans 30j + auto-renouvelle (emit nouveau cert + notification owner + ancien cert revoque apres new active), storage Vault paths organises (`secret/skalean/ca-root-key` CA root private + cert public, `secret/skalean/certs-issued/{cert_serial}` metadata cert emis sans private key), audit log emission/revocation/renewal critique super-admin Skalean only access (`ca.cert.issue`, `ca.cert.revoke`, `ca.cert.renew` permissions Sprint 7.5a), telemetry Prometheus `ca_certs_issued_total{target_type}`, `ca_certs_revoked_total{reason}`, `ca_certs_renewed_total`, `ca_root_setup_signature_count`, helper `OpenSSLWrapper` pour operations crypto natives (forge JS lib alternative si OpenSSL pas disponible container), 14+ tests integration emission + revocation + renewal + CRL + co-signature workflow.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 10** :

- CA root private key compromise = catastrophe absolue. Solution : storage Vault dedicated path avec audit log access strict (every access logged with caller_id), rotation manuelle annuelle obligatoire (CA root re-issued + tous certs re-emit transparente via batch process), HSM hardware Phase 7+ pour key protection physique (yubico HSM 2 ou alternative), separation duties (super-admin Skalean DSI + super-admin CISO must co-sign rotation via Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow).
- Co-signature 4 personnes setup CA root : process complexe. Solution : Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow reuse pattern, 4 signataires sequentiels (Saad CTO -> Abla CEO -> DSI -> CISO), timeout 7j par signataire, audit trail dedicated.
- Cert validity duree default 365j : trop court ou trop long ? Solution : default 365j (1 an), configurable 90-730j per request, renewal auto 30j avant expiration evite outage.
- Private key returned ONCE jamais stockee : caller doit secure storage. Solution : documentation exhaustive consumers (`secret/{tenant_id}/tls-key` Vault path standard), tests verifie response contient private key + cert public, post-response audit log marks "private key delivered" sans contenir le key value.
- CRL publication latency : revocation must propagate. Solution : CRL publie endpoint `https://ca.skalean.internal/crl.pem` mise a jour immediate apres revocation, verifiers cache CRL TTL 5 min max, hard refresh sur 401/403.
- OpenSSL command-line vs JS native crypto : platform-specific. Solution : Node.js native `crypto` module + lib `node-forge` cross-platform pure JS pour compatibility, OpenSSL fallback si available, tests parametrized 2 implementations.
- CA root self-signed acceptance partenaires externes : DGI/ACAPS peuvent rejeter cert self-signed. Solution : Sprint 14 acceptable initial (governmental partners pre-onboarding manual + cert pinning), Phase 7+ migration chained CA reconnu autorite tierce (Tanmia ou Globalsign Maroc).
- Vault path access : permission Sprint 5 Vault. Solution : Vault policy stricte super-admin Skalean only pour CA root, `secret/skalean/ca-root-key` policy `path "secret/skalean/ca-root-key" { capabilities = ["read"] }` avec role binding strict.
- Cert serial number unique : collisions possible si random sans tracking. Solution : table BDD `ca_serial_counter` sequence atomic INCREMENT generated par insert, audit log serial allocations.
- Renewal CRON job execution missed : Skalean down 02h UTC. Solution : recovery job recup missed renewals jusqu'a 7j apres scheduled time, alerte ops si > 7j retard.

### Task 4.1.4 -- Service Registry + Routing Engine

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 7h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.3 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.5 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente le Service Registry catalog + routing engine intelligent. Le Service Registry est la "source de verite" pour quels partenaires sont connectes, quels services ils exposent, quels endpoints sont disponibles, quels rate limits s'appliquent, quels auth methods sont configures. Le routing engine consume Service Registry pour decider : (1) quel endpoint utiliser parmi les redondants (load balancing), (2) si circuit breaker triggered pour skip endpoint unhealthy, (3) si rate limit respected, (4) retry strategy en cas erreur 5xx. Pattern Hystrix-inspired pour circuit breaker (state machine open/half-open/closed) avec thresholds configurables per service.

Livrables principaux : entites TypeORM complete `Partner` (relations OneToMany Service), `Service` (relations OneToMany ServiceEndpoint + ServiceRateLimit + ServiceAuthMethod), `ServiceEndpoint`, `ServiceRateLimit`, `ServiceAuthMethod` selon migration 4.1.2, service `ServiceRegistryService` CRUD complet (createPartner, updatePartner, deactivatePartner, addService, addEndpoint, updateEndpointHealth, listServices, findEndpointsForService, etc), service `RoutingEngine.routeRequest(serviceName: string, payload: SignedPayload, sourceTenantId: string): Promise<RouteResult>` orchestre flow complet (lookup service + endpoints sains via health-check tracker Redis, selection load balancing strategy `LoadBalancer.selectEndpoint(endpoints, 'round_robin')`, verification rate limit `RateLimitChecker.canProceed(serviceName, sourceTenantId)`, verification circuit breaker `CircuitBreaker.canCall(endpointId)`, execute call avec timeout 30s + retry exponential backoff sur 5xx, mise a jour metrics latency + error rate, retour result avec audit trail), service `LoadBalancer.selectEndpoint(endpoints: ServiceEndpoint[], strategy: 'round_robin'): ServiceEndpoint` round-robin initial via counter per service in Redis, evolution `weighted` future weighted load balancing selon weight column, evolution `least_latency` future based on P50 metrics, service `CircuitBreaker.protectCall(endpointId: string, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>` state machine Hystrix pattern (closed = normal operation, open = failures > threshold 5 within 60s -> skip endpoint reject immediate, half_open = after timeout 30s allow 1 test call -> success -> closed, fail -> reopen), service `HealthCheck` CRON 30s heartbeat per endpoint actif via HTTP GET `/health` (timeout 5s, status 200 = healthy, status 503 = degraded, timeout/5xx = unhealthy), state tracker Redis `service_endpoint_health:{endpoint_id}` TTL 90s, retry logic exponential backoff `[1s, 2s, 4s, 8s]` max 4 retries sur 5xx ou network errors, telemetry per endpoint (`service_endpoint_state{endpoint_id,state}`, `service_endpoint_latency_seconds{endpoint_id,quantile=0.95}`, `service_endpoint_errors_total{endpoint_id,error_type}`), publication Kafka events `skalean.events.exchange.endpoint_health_changed`, cache Redis service registry TTL 5 min pour acces frequent + invalidation via Kafka event listener, 16+ tests unitaires + integration couvrant routing happy + circuit breaker triggered + rate limit + load balancing + health-check.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Service Registry stale data : tenant change credentials, cache 5 min stale. Solution : event-driven invalidation via Kafka `skalean.events.exchange.registry_updated` publie sur UPDATE registry tables, consumers Security Servers invalident cache local immediat.
- Circuit breaker false positives : 5 failures transitoires (DGI maintenance temp) declenche open inutile. Solution : threshold configurable per service (DGI 5 failures, AMC 10 failures car moins critical), reset_timeout adaptatif (DGI 30s, fictional 5s pour Demo Day reactivity).
- Health-check overhead : 50 endpoints * heartbeat 30s = 100 req/min overhead constant. Solution : aggregation Health-check batch (1 batch query per partner all endpoints), staggered execution different intervals per partner pour eviter spikes.
- Rate limit cross-tenant unfairness : tenant A frequente service epuise rate limit, tenant B bloque. Solution : rate limits scoped (tenant_id, service_name) couple, pas globale per service, fair sharing strict.
- Round-robin counter Redis race condition : 2 instances Hub simultane. Solution : `INCR` atomic Redis + modulo endpoints.length, counter strict serializable.
- Retry exponential backoff infinite : si endpoint permanently dead, retries inutiles. Solution : max 4 retries (1s + 2s + 4s + 8s = 15s max), apres echec final fallback alternative endpoint si available, sinon throw NoHealthyEndpointError.
- Half-open state contention : multiple test calls concurrent. Solution : Redis lock `circuit_breaker_test_call:{endpoint_id}` TTL 30s, premier acquerit + execute, autres reject immediate.
- Cache Redis registry invalidation broadcast : 100+ Security Servers writers cache. Solution : Kafka topic `skalean.events.exchange.registry_updated` partitioned per partner_id, consumers Security Servers consume + invalident cache local atomic.

### Task 4.1.5 -- Skalean Security Server (Sidecar Tenant)

**Densite cible** : 150-200 KB | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE | **Effort** : 8h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.4 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.6 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente le composant Security Server qui est le **point d'entree principal pour tenants** vers le SEL. Le Security Server est packageable docker image deployable per tenant en sidecar de leur API (pattern microservice sidecar standard). Le Security Server intercepte tous les calls sortants depuis tenant API : tenant API code utilise `SkaleanExchangeLayerClient.routeRequest('dgi.e-facture.submit', payload)` qui sous le capot fait HTTP POST vers Security Server local port 8443, Security Server signe le payload avec tenant private key + chiffre mTLS + forward vers Exchange Hub central, Hub route vers DGI endpoint avec auth credentials, retourne response signed par DGI, Security Server verifie signature + decrypt + retourne tenant API. Cette architecture decouple totalement tenant code des details d'auth/crypto/audit (responsabilite Security Server) et facilite changements sans impacter applications.

Livrables principaux : creation package `@insurtech/skalean-security-server` complet avec Express server port 8443 mTLS-only + workers signing, Dockerfile `skalean/security-server:1.0.0` multi-stage build optimise + image finale < 250 MB avec Alpine + native deps cryptographic, service `MtlsHandshakeService` configure cert chain client (tenant cert + CA root chain) + verify Hub cert sur handshake via mutual TLS (server validate client cert + client validate server cert), service `CertificateLoaderService` load tenant cert from local mount `/etc/skalean/tenant-cert/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (mount Kubernetes Phase 7+, env vars + file local dev), service `MessageSignerService.sign(payload: any, tenantPrivateKey: string): JwsSignedMessage` signature JWS RS256 algorithme conforme RFC 7515 (header alg=RS256 + kid=tenant_id, body = payload JSON, signature = RSA-PKCS1-v1_5 SHA-256 avec tenant private key), service `MessageVerifierService.verify(jwsMessage: JwsSignedMessage, partnerPublicKey: string): VerifiedPayload` validation signature + retourne payload typed, service `ExchangeHubClientService.forwardRequest(serviceName: string, signedPayload: JwsSignedMessage, correlationId: string): Promise<SignedResponse>` HTTP POST mTLS vers Hub URL `https://exchange-hub.skalean.internal:9443/exchange/route` avec Bearer token + correlation header, service `AuditLogPublisherService.publish(auditEntry: AuditLogEntry): Promise<void>` via Kafka topic `skalean.events.exchange.audit_logged` avec entry contenant {timestamp, tenant_id, partner_id, service_name, payload_hash SHA-256, correlation_id, http_status, latency_ms}, controller `ProxyController.intercept('/exchange/route', body, headers)` endpoint principal local accepte tenant API calls et orchestrate (verify caller auth via internal token Sprint 5, sign payload, forward Hub, audit log, return response), configuration tenant-specific via env vars `TENANT_ID`, `EXCHANGE_HUB_URL`, `KAFKA_BROKERS`, `TENANT_CERT_PATH`, `TENANT_KEY_PATH`, performance optimization (signature operations CPU-bound via worker thread async pour eviter block event loop, audit log batch publish max 100 entries / 1s flush interval, connection pool reuse mTLS handshake limit 50 concurrent), telemetry Prometheus `security_server_requests_total{tenant_id,service_name,outcome}`, `security_server_signing_duration_seconds{quantile=0.95}`, `security_server_hub_latency_seconds`, audit log emission strict every routed request, tests integration 18+ couvrant scenarios mTLS handshake happy + invalid cert + signing + verification + forwarding + audit + concurrent load.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 10** :

- Performance overhead Security Server : chaque call sortant tenant ajoute latence (signature ~10ms + mTLS handshake ~30ms + audit log ~5ms = ~45ms P95). Solution : connection pool reuse mTLS sessions (handshake une fois reutilisee), worker thread async signing, audit batch flush, target P95 < 100ms overhead acceptable.
- Tenant cert loading at startup : si cert manquant container crash. Solution : startup check `/etc/skalean/tenant-cert/cert.pem` + `key.pem` exist + valid X.509, throw `MissingTenantCertError` fatal, helm chart future ensure mount.
- JWS RS256 algorithme strict : autres algorithmes (HS256, ES256) refuses pour conformite RFC 7515 + securite (asymmetric key required). Solution : `MessageSignerService` hardcoded RS256 + jose lib enforce, tests verifie tampering algorithme rejected.
- Hub failover transparency : si Hub DC1 down, Security Server failover DC2. Solution : env var `EXCHANGE_HUB_URLS=https://exchange-hub-dc1.skalean.internal:9443,https://exchange-hub-dc2.skalean.internal:9443` array, retry next URL on failure, timeout 5s per URL, health-check Hub regulier 60s.
- Audit log Kafka publish failure silent : event lost. Solution : transactional outbox pattern -- INSERT audit_log_outbox table dans meme transaction request + publisher Kafka worker async lit outbox + retry sur fail.
- mTLS handshake CPU-intensive : 100 concurrent requests = CPU saturation. Solution : connection pool reuse + warmup pre-connect partners frequents au startup, max concurrent handshakes 10 limit semaphore.
- Cert expiration not monitored : cert expire sans warning + outage. Solution : startup check expiration > 30j sinon warning telemetry `cert_near_expiration{days_remaining}`, expire < 7j critical alert ops, expire = communication fail with clear error msg.
- Correlation ID propagation : tenant API correlation_id must flow end-to-end. Solution : header `X-Correlation-Id` extract from tenant API request, propagate in JWS payload extracted + log + audit + forward to Hub same header.
- Sidecar pattern resource contention : Security Server consumes tenant pod resources. Solution : resource limits CPU 200m + memory 256Mi per Security Server (sufficient for 100 req/min), monitoring resource usage + alert > 80% sustained.
- Health-check endpoint exposed : `/security-server/health` accessible tenant API but not external. Solution : bind localhost only `127.0.0.1:8443/health` not external IP, mTLS bind external IP `0.0.0.0:8443` only for forwarded calls.

### Task 4.1.6 -- Audit Log Hub Append-Only + Hash Chain

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 6h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.5 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.7 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente l'audit log centralise blockchain-style append-only avec cryptographic hash chain pour immutability. Le pattern est inspire blockchain Bitcoin/Ethereum (chain de hashes immutable) mais simplified (pas de proof-of-work, pas de consensus distribue, single writer trusted Skalean). L'objectif : prouver mathematiquement qu'aucune entry audit log n'a ete modifie retrospectivement (tampering detection garantie). Chaque entry inclut hash entry precedente + hash courant = chain immutable. Verification quotidienne CRON recalcule chain depuis genesis et detecte tampering eventuel + alerte critique compliance officers. Storage Atlas Cloud bucket WORM (Write Once Read Many) pour double protection (hash chain logique + WORM physique), retention 10 ans + 1 jour alignement decision-008 fiscal MA.

Livrables principaux : creation package `@insurtech/skalean-audit-hub` NestJS server port 9444 mTLS-only, migration `CreateAuditLogEntries` avec table `audit_log_entries` (id BIGINT identity, sequence_number BIGINT UNIQUE strict ordering, timestamp_utc TIMESTAMP, tenant_id UUID, partner_id UUID, service_name VARCHAR(255), payload_hash CHAR(64), signature_chain_prev_hash CHAR(64), signature_chain_current_hash CHAR(64), correlation_id UUID, http_status INT, latency_ms INT, source_security_server_id VARCHAR(64), routed_endpoint_id UUID, created_at), partition mensuelle audit_log_entries sur timestamp_utc pour gestion volume + drop partitions anciennes apres 10 ans + 1 jour retention, migration `CreateAuditChainTip` table `audit_log_chain_tip` singleton row (id PK constant=1, current_hash CHAR(64), last_updated_at, sequence_number BIGINT), RLS Postgres pour tenant_id isolation queries admin (super-admin Skalean cross-tenant via permission special), service `HashChainService.computeNextHash(prevHash: string, entry: AuditLogEntry): string` SHA-256(`${prevHash}|${entry.sequence_number}|${entry.timestamp_utc}|${entry.tenant_id}|${entry.partner_id}|${entry.service_name}|${entry.payload_hash}|${entry.correlation_id}|${entry.http_status}|${entry.latency_ms}`), service `AuditPersistenceService.appendEntry(entry: AuditLogEntryInput): Promise<AuditLogEntry>` execute en transaction Postgres : `SELECT FOR UPDATE` sur `audit_log_chain_tip` row id=1, compute next_hash via HashChainService avec current_hash + new entry, INSERT entry avec sequence_number incremental + signature_chain_prev_hash = current_hash + signature_chain_current_hash = next_hash, UPDATE chain_tip current_hash=next_hash + sequence_number+1 + last_updated_at=NOW, COMMIT, throw `ConcurrentAuditAppendError` si conflict, service `AuditQueryService.queryEntries(filters: AuditQueryFilters): PaginatedResult<AuditLogEntry>` pour admin retrieval avec filters tenant_id/partner_id/service_name/period/correlation_id + pagination cursor-based + max 100 entries per request, service `ChainIntegrityVerifier.verifyChain(startSequence?: number, endSequence?: number): VerificationResult` recalcule chain depuis genesis (sequence=0) ou depuis startSequence custom jusqu'a tip current, compare chaque hash recalcule vs persisted, throw `ChainTamperedError` si mismatch detected + identifier first tampered entry, CRON job `chain-verification.job.ts` quotidien 04h UTC execute verifyChain complete (peut prendre 30-60min pour 1M+ entries), telemetry `audit_chain_verification_status{result=ok|tampered}`, `audit_chain_verification_duration_seconds`, replication Atlas Cloud bucket WORM via Sprint 10 DocumentService pattern reuse (chaque audit entry exporte en file JSON + uploaded WORM bucket avec retention 10 ans + 1 jour automatique), Kafka consumer `audit-log.consumer.ts` souscrit topic `skalean.events.exchange.audit_logged` -> persist entry via AuditPersistenceService (idempotent via correlation_id UNIQUE), 14+ tests integrity + concurrency + tampering detection.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Concurrent appends race conditions : 100 entries simultane casser chain ordering. Solution : `SELECT FOR UPDATE` strict sur chain_tip row + serialised appends via single writer thread (audit-hub worker dedicated), throughput limit ~1000 entries/sec acceptable Sprint 14, optimisation Phase 7+ via sharding chain per tenant.
- Hash chain genesis entry : premier entry n'a pas prev_hash. Solution : genesis prev_hash convention = SHA-256("Skalean Audit Log Genesis 2026"), sequence_number = 0 entry initiale created at audit hub startup, audit log emission.
- Chain verification cost : recalcule 1M+ entries quotidien expensive CPU. Solution : verification incremental depuis last_verified_sequence (track in `chain_tip` row), full verification weekly (Sunday 04h UTC) + incremental daily, alerte si full > 60min.
- WORM bucket cost : 10 ans retention + 1M entries/jour = 3.6 milliards entries fin retention. Solution : compression gzip JSON entries (90% size reduction), bucket lifecycle policy auto-archive Glacier-equivalent apres 1 an, retrieval delay acceptable controles legals.
- Kafka consumer idempotency : duplicate events same correlation_id. Solution : INSERT INTO audit_log_entries WITH UNIQUE (correlation_id) constraint, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, telemetry duplicate rate.
- ChainTamperedError critical alert : compliance officers must be notified immediate. Solution : Sprint 9 NotificationRouter priorite max (Email + Push + SMS to compliance officers + super-admin Skalean), incident workflow auto-trigger.
- Audit log query performance large dataset : 100M entries scan slow. Solution : indexes obligatoires (tenant_id+timestamp_utc DESC composite, correlation_id, partner_id+service_name composite), partition pruning enforced filters period.
- Retention 10 ans + 1 jour automation : drop partitions sans archive perdu donnees. Solution : drop partition seulement apres verifie archive WORM bucket existe + checksum match, audit trail dedicated partition drops.

---

**[Suite dans G-14-part2 : Tasks 4.1.7 a 4.1.12 + contraintes universelles + verification finale]**

## TASKS A GENERER -- SCOPES DETAILLES (SUITE)

### Task 4.1.7 -- Migration Sprint 12 Adapters vers SEL

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE NON-BREAKING | **Effort** : 7h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.6 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.8 a 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task est l'**operation chirurgicale critique** du Sprint 14 : retirer les 3 adapters Sprint 12 (DGI e-facturation, ACAPS portal, AMC declarations) et les remplacer par wrappers SEL Client meme signature methodes pour preservation non-breaking. Les consumers Sprint 12 (Tasks 3.5.5 invoices DGI, 3.5.7 ACAPS reports, 3.5.10 declarations AMC suspicions) ne doivent PAS necessiter modifications de code applicatif -- seul change l'implementation underlying. Tests E2E Sprint 12 doivent PASSER apres refactor (regression test critique obligatoire). Cette task est la preuve de l'investissement architectural SEL : si migration casse Sprint 12, l'argument "SEL evite duplication adapters" devient vide.

Livrables principaux : retrait fichiers Sprint 12 adapters (`repo/packages/books-compliance/src/exports/adapters/dgi-efacturation-api.adapter.ts`, `acaps-portal-api.adapter.ts`, `amc-declaration-api.adapter.ts`) avec git tag `pre-sel-migration-{date}` sauvegarde avant suppression pour rollback emergency, creation 3 wrappers SEL replacement memes signatures methodes dans `repo/packages/books-compliance/src/exports/services/` (`dgi-via-sel.service.ts` injecte `SkaleanExchangeLayerClient` + expose methodes identiques DGI adapter retire avec body interne appelant `client.routeRequest('dgi.e-facture.submit', payload)` ou `'dgi.e-facture.status'` selon operation + handling response signed JWS unwrap + return typed format identique adapter ancien, `acaps-via-sel.service.ts` idem pour ACAPS avec services `acaps.reports.submit` + `acaps.reports.status`, `amc-via-sel.service.ts` idem pour AMC avec services `amc.declaration.submit` + `amc.declaration.status`), update `InvoiceService` Sprint 12 Task 3.5.5 imports DgiViaSelService au lieu de DgiAdapter retire (depend injection NestJS module update), update `AcapsReportService` Sprint 12 Task 3.5.7 idem, update `DeclarationSoupconService` Sprint 12 Task 3.5.10 idem, validation strict tests E2E Sprint 12 doivent continuer passer (regression test obligatoire CRITIQUE -- aucun test PASS perdu apres refactor), telemetry tracking transitions adapters retires (compteurs `adapter_migrated_to_sel_total{adapter_name}` pour traçabilite + audit trail Sprint 7), update documentation `docs/books-compliance/integrations.md` cross-reference SEL et retire mentions adapters legacy, audit log dedie migration Sprint 7 (super-admin action), 14+ tests integration regression + non-breaking + telemetry verifie + Sprint 12 E2E suite execution complete.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Tests Sprint 12 fail post-migration : breaking change detecte. Solution : execution mandatory tests Sprint 12 E2E suite COMPLETE apres chaque adapter migration, abort migration suivante si fail, rollback git tag `pre-sel-migration-{date}` strict.
- Response format diff JWS signed vs raw adapter : wrappers doivent unwrap JWS et retourner format identique adapter. Solution : tests unitaires verifie response shape identique (typed interfaces), wrapper extrait payload du JWS verified + cast format adapter legacy.
- Auth methods diff DGI OAuth2 vs SEL mTLS : Sprint 12 DGI adapter utilisait OAuth2 client_credentials, SEL utilise mTLS + JWS. Solution : credentials migration Vault paths (DGI client_id/secret retire, tenant cert + private key path same Sprint 14 4.1.3), Hub Service Registry config DGI service `auth_method=mtls_oauth2` Hub fait OAuth2 cote DGI.
- Endpoint URLs hard-coded adapter retire : adapter contenait URL DGI. Solution : Service Registry Hub stocke URLs partenaires (Sprint 14 4.1.2 seed), wrapper SEL Client lookup service_name pas URL.
- Rate limits Sprint 12 adapter custom : DGI rate 100 req/min hard-coded. Solution : Service Registry Hub stocke rate limits per service (Sprint 14 4.1.4 ServiceRateLimit), wrapper inherit Hub rate limit handling.
- Retry logic adapter custom : DGI adapter retry 3 fois. Solution : Hub routing engine 4.1.4 inclus retry exponential backoff standard, wrapper delegate, retire retry custom code adapter.
- Error types specific adapter : adapter throw `DgiSpecificError` vs SEL throw `SelRoutingError`. Solution : wrapper convert errors SEL en errors specifiques Sprint 12 (try-catch + re-throw typed), tests Sprint 12 continuent catch `DgiSpecificError`.
- Timeline migration strict : si 4.1.7 prend > 7h, retard sprint. Solution : migration faite per adapter sequentiel (DGI -> ACAPS -> AMC), commit + test apres chaque adapter, possibilite stop midway si fail (Sprint 12 partiellement migre acceptable transient).

### Task 4.1.8 -- Insurance Companies Fictional Endpoints

**Densite cible** : 120-160 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 6h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.7 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.9 a 4.1.12, Sprint 17 Sinistre

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente les 3 endpoints mock pour insurance companies fictional (AssurMaghreb, Atlas Insurance, MediAssur) avec donnees realistic pour Demo Day developpement + Sprint 17 Sinistre Core integration. Les 3 brands fictifs evitent risques juridiques mentionnant vraies compagnies marocaines (Wafa, Saham, AtlantaSanad, etc) sans partenariats formalises. Mock servers exposent 3 services principaux : policy lookup (recuperer details police), claim declare (declarer sinistre cote insurer), claim payout approve (decision payout). Lors Sprint 32 post Demo Day, remplacement par vraies compagnies via update Service Registry (changement endpoints + credentials, code application non impacte grace pattern SEL).

Livrables principaux : creation package `@insurtech/skalean-fictional-insurers` avec mock servers NestJS ports distincts (9999 AssurMaghreb, 10000 Atlas Insurance, 10001 MediAssur), Dockerfile fictional-insurer:1.0.0 reutilise avec env vars `INSURER_NAME` + `INSURER_PORT` + `INSURER_FIXTURES_PATH`, generation fixtures realistic 10000 polices repartis 3 insurers (3500 AssurMaghreb avec specialite auto + dommages, 3500 Atlas Insurance avec specialite multirisques + RC + entreprise, 3000 MediAssur avec specialite health + medecine + hospitalier) + 5000 sinistres divers branches + 1500 payouts approved/rejected variete realistic, services exposes par chaque insurer dans Service Registry Hub via seed migration update 4.1.4 : `insurer.policy.lookup(policyNumber: string): PolicyDetails` retourne (policy_number, holder_name, branch enum auto/dommages/multirisques/health/etc, status enum active/expired/suspended, premium_amount_mad, coverage_amount_mad, start_date, end_date, indemnities_breakdown JSON), `insurer.claim.declare(claimData: ClaimDeclaration): ClaimAcknowledgement` retourne (claim_id assigned by insurer, status enum received/under_review, estimated_processing_days, contact_person, sla_response_at), `insurer.claim.payout_approve(claimId: string): PayoutDecision` retourne (decision enum approved/rejected/partial, payout_amount_mad nullable, rejection_reason nullable, approval_conditions JSON nullable, approved_at, valid_until_date), endpoints REST mock realistic responses + delays simulant latency reseau partenaire externe (50-200ms random gaussian distribution autour 100ms moyenne), realistic edge cases dans mock data (policy not found 404 ~5% lookups random, policy expired ~3% random, claim already exists conflict 409 ~2% random, payout rejected with realistic reason 25% claims, partial payout ~10% claims), registration dans Service Registry Hub via migration seed UPDATE partner endpoints (AssurMaghreb partner_id existing depuis 4.1.2 seed initial + URLs endpoints fictional now active), disclaimers UI Sprint 26 affichant badge visible "Brand fictif developpement - integration Sprint 32" sur toutes pages mentionnant AssurMaghreb / Atlas / MediAssur (audience Demo Day clarte), documentation interne `docs/fictional-insurers-readme.md` separation claire real vs mock + plan transition Sprint 32, tests E2E 12+ couvrant scenarios end-to-end via SEL routing (tenant -> Security Server -> Hub -> fictional insurer -> response signed -> verifie -> retour tenant).

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Demo Day audience confusion fictional vs real : journalistes peuvent reprendre noms fictifs comme real. Solution : disclaimers visibles partout (badge UI "Fictif developpement" + footer page + slide Demo Day annonce explicite), documentation publique stricte separation.
- Fixtures realistic plausibility : police numero format reel marocain. Solution : format respecte conventions reelles `{insurer_code}-{branch}-{year}-{sequence}` (`AM-AUTO-2026-001234` AssurMaghreb auto), respecting taille typiques 12-15 chars.
- Mock latency realistic : pas 0ms response triche performance tests. Solution : gaussian distribution 100ms +/- 50ms simulating reseau partenaire externe, configurable env var `MOCK_LATENCY_MEAN_MS` + `MOCK_LATENCY_STDDEV_MS`.
- Edge cases coverage : tests SEL only happy path miss real failures. Solution : 25% mock responses ont edge cases (errors 4xx, retries needed, partial decisions), tests E2E cover these.
- Insurer responses signing : mock insurers doivent signer responses JWS pour pattern SEL complete. Solution : chaque mock insurer a son private key cert emis Skalean CA Sprint 14 4.1.3 (treated comme partner external), signs responses JWS verifie Security Server.
- Service Registry seed conflict update : initial 4.1.2 seed inserts partners avec URLs placeholder, Sprint 14 4.1.8 update URLs reels mock. Solution : migration `UpdateFictionalInsurersEndpoints` UPDATE service_endpoints SET url=... WHERE service_id IN (SELECT id FROM services WHERE partner_id IN (AssurMaghreb, Atlas, MediAssur)).
- 3 instances mock ports distincts : conflits potential local dev. Solution : env vars configurables ports `ASSURMAGHREB_PORT`, `ATLAS_INSURANCE_PORT`, `MEDIASSUR_PORT` defaults 9999/10000/10001, docker-compose service distinct per insurer.
- Data persistence mock : restart container perd state. Solution : fixtures hard-coded JSON files mountes volume, mutations in-memory (claim declared retourne ID + stocke memory 24h TTL pour subsequent lookups), acceptable pour Demo Day reset acceptable.

### Task 4.1.9 -- Cross-Tenant Handoffs Preparation Sprint 21-22

**Densite cible** : 110-150 KB | **Priorite** : P1 | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.8 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.10 a 4.1.12, Sprint 21-22 Dispatch

**Scope precis** :

Cette task prepare l'infrastructure cross-tenant handoffs (broker tenant -> garage tenant ou broker tenant -> expert tenant) qui sera utilisee Sprint 21-22 Dispatch Platform. SEL support deja inter-organismes via Hub central pour partenaires externes (insurers, gov), mais cross-tenant Skalean ecosystem requires registry interne specifique avec auto-registration tenant lifecycle. Chaque tenant nouveau cree devient automatiquement un "partner internal" registre dans Service Registry pour permettre cross-tenant communication via meme infrastructure SEL.

Livrables principaux : extension Service Registry avec service type `cross_tenant_handoff` enum value dans table `services.service_type` (ajout enum values `external_integration` defaut existing + `cross_tenant_handoff` nouveau), registration auto chaque tenant comme partner Skalean ecosystem via lifecycle hooks Sprint 7 (subscription Kafka event `tenant.created` consume + INSERT partner avec type=`internal_tenant` + is_internal_tenant=true flag + auto-generate cert via Skalean CA 4.1.3 + register services tenant default), services exposes par tenant via Security Server pattern (`tenant.sinistre.handoff_to_garage(sinistreData: SinistreHandoffPayload): HandoffAcknowledgement` pour preparation Sprint 22 Dispatch broker->garage, `tenant.sinistre.handoff_to_expert(sinistreData)` pour preparation Sprint 24 Sinistre Apps broker->expert, `tenant.sinistre.handoff_back_to_broker(updatedSinistreData)` pour retour expert/garage -> broker), workflow handoff async avec correlation_id end-to-end pour tracking complete chain (sinistre cree tenant_a -> handoff tenant_b expert -> handoff tenant_c garage -> handoff back tenant_a broker, audit log Hub track complete chain via correlation_id query), audit log strict cross-tenant communications pour traçabilite Skalean ecosystem (decision-008 CNDP cross-tenant requires audit), documentation patterns `docs/cross-tenant-handoffs-patterns.md` avec examples Sprint 22 + 24 + diagrams sequence Mermaid, permissions cross-tenant Sprint 7.5a : `sel.cross_tenant.handoff.send` + `sel.cross_tenant.handoff.receive` per service exposed, 8+ tests integration cross-tenant handoff scenarios (broker A -> garage B -> retour broker A complet, broker -> expert avec timeout escalation, cross-tenant authentication via mTLS verify CA Skalean).

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Auto-registration tenant lifecycle race : tenant created mais SEL Security Server pas ready, partner registry inconsistent. Solution : Kafka event `tenant.created` consume async avec retry exponential backoff + idempotent (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), tenant operationnel uniquement apres Security Server provisioned (Sprint 7 lifecycle includes SEL setup).
- Internal tenant cert emission cost : 1000 tenants = 1000 certs CA. Solution : CA load acceptable (Sprint 14 CA root signs ~10/min, 1000 tenants 100min batch initial migration acceptable), monitoring CA throughput + alerting > 80% capacity.
- Cross-tenant permission Sprint 7.5a granularity : tenant A peut handoff vers tenant B mais pas tenant C. Solution : permissions scoped `sel.cross_tenant.handoff.send.tenant_b` per target tenant, default deny strict, explicit grant per relationship.
- Cross-tenant data sovereignty : tenant A donnees envoyees tenant B doivent etre filtrees (no PII unnecessary). Solution : payload schemas SinistreHandoffPayload strict avec champs limits (sinistre_id, customer_name uniquement, plate_number, branch_info, NOT full customer PII), validation Zod strict.
- Correlation ID flow multi-hop : 3+ tenants chain perd correlation. Solution : header `X-Correlation-Id` propage stricte every Security Server hop, audit log includes `correlation_chain_position` (1, 2, 3, ...) pour reconstruction trace.
- Handoff async response timeout : tenant A handoff vers tenant B mais B retard reply. Solution : timeout configurable per service type (sinistre handoff 5min standard, escalation N+1 si > 5min, manual intervention > 1h), notification Sprint 9 tenant A si timeout.
- Cross-tenant audit log volume : 100 tenants * 10 handoffs/jour * cross-tenant = 1000+ entries audit/jour. Solution : retention 10 ans acceptable storage WORM, query indexes optimises pour reconstruction chains.
- Tenant deletion lifecycle : tenant supprime mais certs encore valides. Solution : Kafka event `tenant.deleted` consume + revoque cert via CA `revoke()` + deregister partner Service Registry + audit log critical.

### Task 4.1.10 -- Monitoring Observability SEL

**Densite cible** : 110-150 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.9 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.11, 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente monitoring observability complet SEL avec dashboards Sprint 13 analytics enrichis. Sans monitoring proper, SEL devient une "black box" et les operations Skalean ne peuvent pas detecter degradations partenaires + capacite planning + incidents. Les metrics critiques exposees : per partner success rate (% requetes succes / total), P95/P99 latency per service, circuit breaker state currents, audit log volume trend (capacite planning), certificate expiration tracking (eviter outages renouvellement oublies), error rate per partner (detection partenaires degrades).

Livrables principaux : extension Sprint 13 AnalyticsService avec 4 nouvelles methodes specifiques SEL : `getSelPartnerHealth(period: TimePeriod): SelPartnerHealthResult` retourne per partner (success_rate %, total_requests, average_latency_ms, uptime_percentage_period), `getSelLatencyByService(period): SelLatencyResult` P95/P99 latency per service + endpoint distribution, `getSelCircuitBreakerStates(): SelCircuitBreakerResult` etat current circuit breakers all endpoints (state enum open/half_open/closed, failures_count_current_window, last_state_change_at), `getSelCertificateExpirations(): SelCertExpirationResult` certificates expirent < 30j classifier severity (critical < 7j, warning 7-30j, info 30-60j), schemas ClickHouse SEL events ajout dans `sel-events.schema.sql` (audit_log_events + routing_events tables ClickHouse partitioned mois), pipeline ETL Sprint 13 enrichi nouveau pipeline `sel-events-pipeline.ts` consume Kafka topics SEL (`skalean.events.exchange.audit_logged`, `endpoint_health_changed`, `cert_issued`, `cert_revoked`, `cert_renewed`, `circuit_breaker_state_changed`) + sync ClickHouse pour analytics historiques, dashboards SEL admin Sprint 26 (premier draft data display ready integration future) : Partner health overview matrix grille 3x3 colors red/yellow/green per partner, latency heatmap per service avec gradient colors P95 thresholds, audit log volume trend timeline 30 derniers jours, circuit breaker states list visible quick incident detection, certificate expiration alerts widget critical/warning items, telemetry Prometheus exporters per composant SEL (CA `ca_certs_*`, Security Server `security_server_*`, Hub `hub_routing_*`, Audit Hub `audit_chain_*`), alerting rules `prometheus-alerts-sel.yaml` strict (P95 latency > 1s = warning, P95 > 3s = critical, error rate > 5% per partner over 5min = warning, error rate > 15% = critical, certificate expire < 7j = warning, expire < 1j = critical, circuit breaker open > 60min = warning, chain verification failed = critical immediate page-out), integration Sprint 9 NotificationRouter pour alertes SEL ops team (Email + Push + SMS pour critical avec runbook URL inclus auto), 12+ tests integration monitoring + alerting + dashboard data accuracy.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Metrics cardinality explosion : per partner per service per tenant labels combinent millions metrics distincts. Solution : Prometheus best practice (limit labels cardinality, avoid tenant_id label sur metrics global), aggregated per service uniquement, tenant_id only audit log query.
- Alerting fatigue : trop alertes degradees critical 10x/jour ignore par ops. Solution : tiered alerting (info -> warning 5min -> critical 15min sustained), groupings per partner pour eviter spam, mute periodic incidents recurrents identifies non-critical.
- Dashboard latency partial data : ETL Sprint 13 lag 5min affiche stale data. Solution : header `X-Data-Updated-At` explicite (Sprint 13 4.3.4 pattern), refresh interval dashboards 60s acceptable.
- Certificate expiration alerts noise : 100 tenants * 365j cycle annuel = 365 expirations distribuees. Solution : groupage alerts daily digest 08h UTC (consolide all expirations next 30j single notification), exceptionnels critical < 7j alertes immediate individual.
- Circuit breaker state changes spammy : flapping circuit between open/closed. Solution : hysteresis threshold (open requires 5 failures, closed requires 10 success consecutive), alerting only state changes lasting > 5min.
- Audit log volume trend baseline : volume baseline depends usage Skalean ecosystem grow. Solution : moving average 30j baseline + alert si volume > 2x baseline (anomaly potential incident) + alert si volume < 0.5x baseline (potential outage silent).
- ClickHouse SEL events ETL lag : real-time monitoring inutile si lag 5min. Solution : Sprint 13 ETL polling 5min acceptable historique trends, real-time monitoring via Prometheus directement Hub + Security Servers metrics endpoint.
- Alerting runbook absent : critical alert reveille SRE 03h, pas de procedure. Solution : runbook URL obligatoire dans alert payload (`runbook_url=https://docs.skalean.internal/runbooks/sel-{alert_type}`), runbooks rediges per alert type.

### Task 4.1.11 -- Endpoints REST Admin SEL

**Densite cible** : 100-140 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 4h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.10 | **Dependances sortantes** : 4.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task expose les endpoints REST admin SEL accessibles via Sprint 26 Admin UI super-admin Skalean. Les endpoints permettent supervision SEL en production : list partners + status, list services + endpoints health, query audit log, verify chain integrity, test connection ad-hoc partner. Permissions strictes super-admin Skalean only (pas tenant operationnel access -- SEL admin = Skalean platform team).

Livrables principaux : controller `admin-exchange-layer.controller.ts` dans `repo/apps/api/src/modules/exchange-layer/controllers/` avec 9 endpoints : `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/partners` (list partners avec status synthetique + count services + total requests last 24h), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/partners/{id}` (details partner + services exposed + endpoints health + rate limits configured + cert expiration), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/services` (list all services across partners avec filters partner_id + status), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/endpoints` (list all endpoints avec filters health_status + service_id + circuit_breaker_state), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/endpoints/{id}/health` (health check ad-hoc trigger heartbeat immediate + return result), `POST /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/partners/{id}/test-connection` (trigger test connection ad-hoc end-to-end via SEL pour valider partner reachable + auth working + return latency), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/audit-log` (query audit log paginated cursor-based avec filters tenant_id + partner_id + service_name + period_from + period_to + correlation_id + max 100 entries per request), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/audit-log/{id}` (details audit log entry single avec full chain context prev_hash + next_hash + sequence_number), `GET /api/v1/admin/exchange-layer/audit-log/chain-verification` (status verification chain integrity last run + last successful verification + alerts tampering detected), OpenAPI 3.1 schemas complets via decorateurs NestJS, permissions Sprint 7.5a strictes super-admin Skalean only (`exchange_layer.admin.read` pour partners/services/endpoints/health, `exchange_layer.audit.read` pour audit-log queries, `exchange_layer.test.execute` pour test-connection, `exchange_layer.chain.verify` pour chain-verification access -- super-admin Skalean exclusif via permission `skalean.super_admin` requirement composite), validation Zod entree+sortie strict, rate limit 20 req/min/admin (data sensible critique securite limit), audit log access dans `00-pilotage/audit-trails/sel-admin-access.audit` (every admin endpoint call logged who/what/when), 12+ tests E2E couvrant happy path + permissions strict super-admin + multi-tenant audit log query + chain verification trigger.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Super-admin permission strict cross-tenant : Skalean super-admin doit voir all tenants audit log mais tenant operationnel jamais. Solution : permission composite `skalean.super_admin AND exchange_layer.admin.read` enforce strict, controller verifie `request.user.scope == 'platform'` not `'tenant'`.
- Audit log query performance large datasets : 100M entries scan slow sans indexes proper. Solution : indexes obligatoires Sprint 14 4.1.6 (timestamp_utc DESC, tenant_id+timestamp_utc, partner_id+service_name, correlation_id), partition pruning enforced via filter period strict required.
- Test connection ad-hoc cost : trigger end-to-end test cumulative bandwidth + cost partenaire. Solution : rate limit 5 tests/min/partner enforce, audit log dedicated `test_connection_triggered`, prevent abuse.
- Chain verification trigger cost : full verification 1M entries takes 30-60min CPU intensive. Solution : endpoint trigger uniquement queue le verification (async), return job_id immediate, polling status via separate endpoint, full sync trigger reserved emergency cases (post-tampering suspicion).
- Multi-tenant audit log filter bypass : super-admin can see all tenants but filter accidently leaks data inappropriately. Solution : explicit query parameter `?tenant_id` REQUIRED unless `?all_tenants=true` opt-in with audit log critical, default scoping safer.
- OpenAPI schemas large endpoints : audit log endpoint with all filters = big schema. Solution : schemas modulaires reuse common types (`PartnerFilter`, `PeriodFilter`, `PaginationParam`), Zod auto-generate OpenAPI minimize duplication.
- Permission denied error info leak : if permission missing, error must not reveal SEL existence to tenant operationnel. Solution : 404 Not Found return (route not exposed), pas 403 Forbidden (reveals existence), enforce same pattern tous endpoints SEL admin.
- Audit log immutability admin endpoint : super-admin must NOT be able to delete audit entries. Solution : controllers GET-only sur audit log (pas DELETE), append-only pattern strict, immutability guarantee chain hashes.

### Task 4.1.12 -- Tests E2E 30+ + Sprint 14 Closure

**Densite cible** : 200-280 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 12h
**Dependances entrantes** : 4.1.1 a 4.1.11 | **Dependances sortantes** : V-14 verification finale + Sprint 15 demarrage

**Scope precis** :

Cette task finalise le sprint 14 avec la suite de tests E2E exhaustive et marque la cloture du Sprint 14 (premier de Phase 3 Skalean Exchange Layer). 30+ scenarios E2E organises en 5 suites thematiques (Security mTLS + signing, Service Registry + routing, Audit Log + hash chain, Migration adapters Sprint 12, Disaster Recovery) + benchmarks performance critiques + DR runbook execute reel. Rapport closure Sprint 14 documente SEL operational + migration Sprint 12 complete + pre-requis Sprint 15 valides + risques residuels identifies.

Livrables principaux : fixtures realistes complets dans `tests/fixtures/sprint-14-fixtures.ts` (5 tenants test avec certs emis Skalean CA, 6 partenaires registry 3 gov + 3 fictional insurers, 20 services exposes total, 50+ endpoints health-checked, 1000+ audit log entries simulees pour stress chain verification, signatures JWS fixtures valid + invalid pour test verification), 30+ tests E2E organises (`Suite Security mTLS + signing` 8 tests : handshake mTLS happy path successful, handshake invalid cert rejected with proper error, JWS signature verified valid happy, JWS tampered modified payload rejected, certificate revoked CRL checked rejected, CRL verification published endpoint, certificate expired rejected with clear error, signature replay attack same correlation_id detected rejected), (`Suite Service Registry + routing` 6 tests : routing happy path 1 endpoint, load balancing round-robin verified across 3 endpoints sequence verifiable, circuit breaker triggered after 5 consecutive failures opens state, circuit breaker recovery half-open state after timeout 30s, health-check rejection unhealthy endpoint excluded routing, rate limit enforcement 429 status returned after threshold), (`Suite Audit Log + hash chain` 6 tests : append entry chain valid hash computed correctly, chain verification genesis-to-tip 1000 entries succeeds, tampering detection alerte declenche modify single entry hash mismatch detected, concurrent appends serialised correct order sequence_numbers strict, retention 10 ans automatic archive partition drop after verification archive WORM, query audit log paginated avec filters all combinations functional), (`Suite Migration adapters Sprint 12` 5 tests : DGI submission via SEL non-breaking E2E end-to-end functional, ACAPS reports via SEL non-breaking, AMC declarations via SEL non-breaking, regression Sprint 12 E2E tests pass complete suite, telemetry transitions tracked compteurs incremental), (`Suite Disaster Recovery` 5 tests : Hub DC1 failure failover DC2 < 30s automatic switchover validated, CA recovery from Vault < 5min if root key compromise simulated, audit log chain recovery from backup post-disaster verifies chain integrity, certificate emergency emission via super-admin endpoint bypass standard workflow, full DR runbook execution end-to-end documented + executed reel by SRE team), benchmarks performance executes (`bench-sel-routing.sh` P95 overhead routing < 100ms target verifie sur 1000 req sample, `bench-hub-throughput.sh` 10k req/min sustained 60min Hub no degradation, `bench-audit-append.sh` P99 < 50ms verifie 10k entries append sequential, `bench-chain-verification.sh` 1M entries verification chain integrity < 60s objectif), coverage globale Sprint 14 >= 90% lignes + 85% branches sur les 5 packages SEL, documentation `tests/e2e/SCENARIOS-SEL.md` exhaustive avec chaque scenario explique + assertions criticite, **rapport closure Sprint 14** `00-pilotage/reports/sprint-14-closure-report.md` documenter (SEL operational verifie 4 composants up + sante, migration Sprint 12 complete non-breaking verified Sprint 12 E2E pass, 6 partenaires connected fonctionnels 3 governmental migrated + 3 fictional insurers, decisions 015 ratifiee + appliquee + audit, pre-requis Sprint 15 Resolution Framework valides liste + checklist, risques residuels identifies CA chain Phase 7+ requires + service mesh production-grade Phase 7+ + integrations BAM/CNIE futures, recommandation GO Sprint 15 si score >=95% GO + < 3 FAIL critique).

**Pieges specifiques minimum 10** :

- DR runbook execution reel obligatoire : "documente runbook" sans test reel = inutile en crise. Solution : execution reel par SRE team monthly DR test + audit log dedicated, Sprint 14 closure requires test executed at least once successful.
- Tests E2E volume 30+ takes time : 30 tests * 30s avg = 15min + setup = potentially 30min CI. Solution : parallelization vitest workers, isolation per package (SEL packages can test parallel), CI optimization total ~15min target acceptable.
- Fixtures certs realistic : test certs vs prod certs diff. Solution : test fixtures generated via real Skalean CA 4.1.3 issueCert function (real PKI integration tested), test tenants have real certs.
- Mock insurers signing : fictional insurers must sign responses JWS pour pattern SEL complete. Solution : each fictional insurer has CA-issued cert + signs responses standard JWS RS256, tests verifie signature validation.
- Cross-suite test isolation : Security suite tests affect Routing suite state. Solution : transaction rollback per test BDD Postgres + Redis FLUSHDB per test setup + Kafka topics dedicated test prefix `test_` + drop after suite.
- Sprint 12 E2E regression suite : must verifie tests E2E Sprint 12 pass post-migration. Solution : explicit step in Sprint 14 closure `pnpm --filter @insurtech/books-compliance vitest run tests/e2e/ all pass` verified + counted, no test deletion or skip allowed.
- Benchmark P95 overhead measurement noise : single run variance high. Solution : 3 runs median, baseline updated quarterly, marge 20% acceptable, alert if degradation > 30%.
- Coverage 90% threshold strict : missing test branches difficult final 5%. Solution : focus essential paths first, edge cases secondary, coverage report exclude generated migrations + Dockerfile + config files.
- Closure report exhaustivite : 12 tasks documenter. Solution : template structure (1 section per task + bilan global + decisions appliquees + technical debt + recommandation GO), 5-8 pages length acceptable.
- Phase 3 Sprint 15 prerequisites validation : Sprint 15 Resolution Framework requires SEL operational. Solution : checklist explicit `[ ] SEL Hub up DC1+DC2, [ ] Skalean CA operational, [ ] Audit Hub chain verification daily ok, [ ] Migration Sprint 12 non-breaking verified, [ ] 6 partners registered + functional` dans closure report.
- Risques residuels documentation : "risques identifies" trop vague. Solution : matrix risks structure (risk_id + description + impact + mitigation + owner + target_phase), traceable issues backlog future sprints.

---

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Tout fichier task respecte un format strict : headers Markdown ATX (`# ## ###`) uniquement, code blocks avec language tag explicite (`bash`, `sql`, `typescript`, `xml`, `yaml`, `json`), tableaux Markdown formates avec pipes alignes (`|`), listes a puces avec `- ` (jamais `* `), listes numerotees `1. 2. 3.` (jamais `1) 2)`), liens hyperliens toujours formates `[text](path)` (jamais URLs raw), code blocks pour configurations longues + commentaires inline pour explications, sequence diagrams Mermaid markdown rendus natif GitHub.

### Style narratif

Le style est dense et professionnel : prose dense privilegiee aux bullet points dans sections explicatives (sections 3, 4, 8), paragraphes longs 6-12 lignes justifies (sauf code blocks et tableaux), voix active preferee passive, niveau technique senior (assumer competence NestJS/TypeScript/PostgreSQL/Kafka/Redis/PKI/JWS/cryptography), terminologie metier consistante (Exchange Layer = SEL, Skalean Exchange Hub = Hub, Security Server = sidecar tenant, Service Registry = catalog services, Audit Log Hub = audit centralise, X-Road pattern = pattern inspire Estonie), references cryptographic standards explicit (RFC 7515 JWS, RFC 5280 X.509, FIPS 186 SHA-256).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task doit mentionner explicitement minimum 4 decisions strategiques (001-015), **decision-015 obligatoire toutes tasks 4.1.X** car ratifiee dans 4.1.1 et application directe SEL architecture, minimum 1 reference au B-14 sprint specification, minimum 1 reference a une task voisine (4.1.X), minimum 1 reference au V-14 verification criteria correspondant, minimum 1 reference aux sprints precedents (1-13) pertinents.

### Tailles cibles synthese

| Task | Min KB | Max KB | Densite |
|------|--------|--------|---------|
| 4.1.1 | 100 | 140 | Moyenne (architecture + decision + design doc) |
| 4.1.2 | 130 | 170 | Forte (Hub deployment + 4 entites + Terraform) |
| 4.1.3 | 130 | 170 | Forte (PKI CA + crypto operations critique) |
| 4.1.4 | 130 | 170 | Forte (Service Registry + routing engine intelligent) |
| 4.1.5 | 150 | 200 | Tres forte (CRITIQUE Security Server sidecar tenant) |
| 4.1.6 | 130 | 170 | Forte (Audit Log + hash chain + WORM) |
| 4.1.7 | 130 | 170 | Forte (Migration Sprint 12 non-breaking critique) |
| 4.1.8 | 120 | 160 | Forte (Fictional insurers + Service Registry update) |
| 4.1.9 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (Cross-tenant preparation Sprint 21-22) |
| 4.1.10 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (Monitoring SEL + extension Sprint 13) |
| 4.1.11 | 100 | 140 | Moyenne (Admin REST endpoints) |
| 4.1.12 | 200 | 280 | Tres forte (30+ E2E + DR runbook + closure Sprint 14) |

**Total Sprint 14 attendu : 1.74 - 2.30 MB sur les 12 tasks.**

## WORKFLOW D'EXECUTION RECOMMANDE

### Pour chaque task

```
1. Lire B-14 -- section "Tache 4.1.{Z}" (scope) et "Frontiere du sprint" (limites)
2. Lire V-14 -- criteres V{N} attribues a la task
3. Lire C-14 -- extrait de la task dans orchestrateur (dependances pratiques)
4. Lire 2-3 tasks deja generees voisines pour calibrer style et profondeur
5. Lire decisions/001-015 -- selectionner 4-6 decisions applicables (decision-015 SEL X-Road OBLIGATOIRE)
6. Lire template 03-template-task.md -- valider 11 sections obligatoires
7. Rediger fichier task-4.1.{Z}-{slug}.md dans `prompts-taches/sprint-14-skalean-exchange-layer/`
8. Verifier taille finale dans la fourchette cible
9. Verifier aucune emoji presente
10. Commit Conventional Commits : `feat(prompts-taches): add task-4.1.{Z} prompt for Sprint 14`
```

### Si une task fail la verification format

Identifier la section manquante ou non conforme, lire la task voisine deja generee qui contient cette section bien faite, reecrire la section en alignant style et profondeur, re-verifier toutes les contraintes, re-commit.

### Si task taille hors fourchette

Trop courte : approfondir Sections 4 (Contexte etendu) et 8 (Implementation), pas Section 5 (Objectif). Trop longue : reduire exemples redondants dans Section 8 (garder un exemple par concept), pas reduire pieges Section 4.5.

### Sequencement critique 4.1.1 avant tout

Task 4.1.1 (Architecture + decision-015 + design doc) **doit etre peer-reviewed Saad CTO + Abla CEO** avant generation tasks 4.1.2+. Sans ratification formelle decision-015, l'investissement 75h des tasks suivantes risque mauvaise direction.

## VERIFICATION FINALE APRES GENERATION 12 TASKS

```bash
cd 00-pilotage/prompts-taches/sprint-14-skalean-exchange-layer/

# Verifier presence et taille
ls -la task-4.1.*.md
wc -c task-4.1.*.md

# Verifier total (~1.74 - 2.30 MB)
TOTAL_BYTES=$(wc -c task-4.1.*.md | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}')
TOTAL_MB=$(echo "scale=2; $TOTAL_BYTES / 1048576" | bc)
echo "Total : $TOTAL_MB MB (cible 1.74 - 2.30 MB)"

# Aucune emoji
grep -P "[\x{1F300}-\x{1F9FF}]|[\x{2600}-\x{27BF}]" task-4.1.*.md && echo "EMOJI DETECTED -- FAIL" || echo "No emoji -- OK"

# Sections obligatoires
for task in task-4.1.*.md; do
  for section in "TACHE 4.1." "Sprint :" "## 1. But" "## 2. Contexte" "## 3. Objectif" "## 4. Etat" "## 5. References" "## 6. Implementation" "## 7. Tests" "## 8. Criteres" "## 9. Commandes"; do
    grep -q "$section" "$task" || echo "$task: MISSING $section"
  done
done

# References B-14
for task in task-4.1.*.md; do
  grep -q "B-14-sprint-14" "$task" || echo "$task : MISSING reference B-14"
done

# Decisions strategiques (minimum 4 par task)
for task in task-4.1.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "decision-00[1-9]|decision-010|decision-011|decision-012|decision-013|decision-014|decision-015" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 4 ]; then
    echo "$task : Only $COUNT decisions (min 4)"
  fi
done

# Decision-015 SEL X-Road obligatoire toutes tasks 4.1.X
for task in task-4.1.*.md; do
  if ! grep -q "decision-015" "$task"; then
    echo "$task : MISSING decision-015 SEL X-Road (OBLIGATOIRE)"
  fi
done

# Pieges techniques minimum 8 par task
for task in task-4.1.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "Piege [0-9]+ :|piege technique" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 8 ]; then
    echo "$task : Only $COUNT pieges (min 8)"
  fi
done

# X-Road reference dans tasks architecturales 4.1.1
if ! grep -q "X-Road\|Estonie" task-4.1.1*.md; then
  echo "task-4.1.1 : MISSING reference X-Road / Estonia pattern"
fi

# JWS RS256 / mTLS dans tasks securite 4.1.3, 4.1.5
for task in task-4.1.3*.md task-4.1.5*.md; do
  if ! grep -qE "JWS|RS256|mTLS|X\.509" "$task"; then
    echo "$task : MISSING reference crypto JWS/mTLS/X.509"
  fi
done

# Hash chain dans task audit 4.1.6
if ! grep -qE "hash chain|SHA-256|chain integrity" task-4.1.6*.md; then
  echo "task-4.1.6 : MISSING reference hash chain"
fi

# Migration non-breaking dans task 4.1.7
if ! grep -qE "non-breaking|regression|Sprint 12 E2E" task-4.1.7*.md; then
  echo "task-4.1.7 : MISSING reference non-breaking migration Sprint 12"
fi

# Fictional disclaimers dans task 4.1.8
if ! grep -qE "fictif|fictional|disclaimers" task-4.1.8*.md; then
  echo "task-4.1.8 : MISSING reference fictional disclaimers"
fi

# DR failover < 30s dans task 4.1.12
if ! grep -qE "DR|failover|disaster recovery|< 30s" task-4.1.12*.md; then
  echo "task-4.1.12 : MISSING reference DR failover testing"
fi
```

Si tous checks passent : Sprint 14 pret pour execution par C-14 orchestrateur.
