## TASKS A GENERER -- SCOPES DETAILLES

### Task 5.1.1 -- Architecture Base + Decision-017 + Design Doc

**Densite cible** : 110-150 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : Phase 3 closure | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.2 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task est le gateway architectural Sprint 16 -- premier sprint metier vertical apres 15 sprints infrastructure. Avant toute implementation, decision-017 doit etre formellement ratifiee + design doc detaillee 20+ pages + peer-review Saad CTO + Abla CEO obligatoire. Sprint 16 inflection point critique : transition "infrastructure technique" -> "produit metier observable". Pour Demo Day 30 juin 2026 Marrakech, ce sprint est le premier visible product capability d'Assurflow.

Livrables principaux : decision-017 ratification dans `decisions/017-sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine.md` 8-12 pages (titre + date 1 juin 2026 + status Accepte + contexte business positioning vertical Sinistre + decision state machine 8 etats avec 12 transitions valides matrix + alternatives evaluees workflow-engine BPMN Camunda / Temporal durable execution / custom state machine / Stripe-pattern lifecycle + consequences + revision schedule), design doc dans `docs/architecture/sinistre-foundation-design.md` 20+ pages (executive summary 2 pages non-technique Abla CEO, state machine diagram Mermaid 8 etats avec 12 transitions + 4+ invalides illustrees, sequence diagrams sagas integration Sprint 15 declare-claim 4 etapes + request-expertise 3 etapes + approve-payout 5 etapes preparation Sprint 17+ ecosystem, ACAPS 4.7.2.2 compliance matrix exhaustive avec champs obligatoires + validations + error codes ACAPS_001-020+, risk analysis multi-dimensional, retention policy 10 ans + 1 jour CGNC fiscal preserve, pseudonymisation flow GDPR/CNDP loi 09-08 reconciliation ACAPS retention, roadmap Phase 7+ evolutions decision-017 v2 si futures circulaires modifie state machine), kickoff report `00-pilotage/reports/phase-4-kickoff-report.md`, peer-review obligatoire Saad CTO + Abla CEO avec signatures audit trail dedicated avant impl 5.1.2+.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- State machine model trop complex impl : devs comprend mal 8 etats + 12 transitions. Solution : design doc executive summary 2 pages debut + diagram Mermaid visuel + tableau matrix transitions per role actor + glossaire termes claim/sinistre/insurer/expert/garage en annexe.
- Alternatives serieuses : Temporal durable execution attractive pour workflows production-grade. Solution : justifier rejet (Temporal hosting US/EU pas MA decision-008, license cost, learning curve equipe, custom state machine simpler Sprint 16 acceptable), inclure roadmap Phase 7+ evaluation.
- ACAPS 4.7.2.2 interpretation incertaine : circulaire courte 2024 ambigue. Solution : design doc section ACAPS interpretation explicite + outreach ACAPS Skalean si questions, validation Abla CEO reseaux.
- Versioning decision-017 v1 -> v2 futures circulaires : breaking changes possible. Solution : design doc section Future Evolution explicit, migration plan coordinated.
- Peer-review skip pour speed : tentation Sprint 16 strict. Solution : audit trail obligatoire 5.1.1 closure peer-review Saad + Abla signatures avant commit 5.1.2.
- Design doc trop technique non-tech : Abla CEO doit comprendre. Solution : executive summary 2 pages + diagrams clair + glossaire annexe.
- Risk analysis surface : "risques identifies" sans matrix. Solution : risk matrix structured (risk_id + description + impact + probability + mitigation + owner + target_phase).
- Roadmap Phase 7+ absent : decision-017 sans vision long terme. Solution : section "Future Evolution" 2-3 pages documente Temporal eval + state machine evolutions.

### Task 5.1.2 -- Entites Sinistre + RLS + Indexes Performance

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 6h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.1 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.3 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente les 6 entites domain Sinistre TypeORM + relations + RLS multi-tenant strict + indexes performance critiques. Foundation entites determinante : si entites mal designed initial, refactor Sprint 17+ costly. Indexes performance dimensionnes pour Sprint 18 Customer Portal queries volumineuses (1000+ sinistres list paginated).

Livrables principaux : creation package `@insurtech/sinistre` v1.0.0 NestJS module, migration `CreateSinistreEntities` avec 5 tables Postgres (`sinistres` aggregate root id+tenant_id+customer_id_sprint8+sinistre_type+sinistre_branch+status+declaration_date+montant_estime+correlation_id+plaque+lieu+description+version, `sinistre_lines` multi-line claims, `sinistre_lifecycle_events` audit append-only partition mensuelle, `sinistre_documents` references Sprint 10), RLS multi-tenant active strict toutes tables, enums TypeScript explicit `SinistreStatus` 8 valeurs + `SinistreType` 8 valeurs + `SinistreBranch` 12 valeurs + `ActorType` 7 valeurs + `DocumentType` 9 valeurs, indexes performance critiques (tenant_id+status partial, tenant_id+customer_id_sprint8, tenant_id+declaration_date DESC, correlation_id, plaque_immatriculation, lifecycle_events sinistre_id+timestamp DESC, partition timestamp), entites TypeORM completes avec relations OneToMany, seeds initial migration `SeedSinistreTaxonomy` 8 SinistreType + 12 SinistreBranch idempotent, 16+ tests unitaires.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Partition mensuelle audit log : queries cross-partition slow. Solution : indexes partition-aware + filter timestamp obligatoire dans queries + partition pruning verified.
- Customer_id_sprint8 type UUID vs Sprint 8 entity : FK strict casse decouplage. Solution : FK reference soft (UUID column sans contrainte explicit), validation runtime via CustomerService.findById.
- Plaque format storage : varchar libre vs strict. Solution : VARCHAR(20) + check constraint regex AAA-NNN-LL ou format MA strict.
- GPS coordinates POINT type Postgres : geo queries future. Solution : PostGIS extension optional Phase 7+, POINT basic Sprint 16.
- Optimistic locking version BIGINT : conflicts concurrent updates. Solution : `@VersionColumn` TypeORM auto-increment + throw OptimisticLockingError + retry pattern.
- Enums Postgres vs TypeScript divergence : ajout enum value Postgres sans TypeScript update. Solution : enums TypeScript source-of-truth + migration generation auto.
- RLS bypass via SUPERUSER role Postgres : developpeur debug bypass. Solution : audit log queries SUPERUSER + alerting strict + production access super-admin only.
- Soft delete documents vs hard delete : audit ACAPS retention. Solution : is_soft_deleted BOOLEAN preserve data + filter queries WHERE is_soft_deleted=false, hard delete uniquement apres 10 ans + 1 jour.

### Task 5.1.3 -- State Machine 8 Etats + 12 Transitions Validees

**Densite cible** : 140-180 KB | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE LIFECYCLE | **Effort** : 7h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.2 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.4 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente le state machine 8 etats avec 12 transitions valides whitelist strict + 4+ transitions invalides explicit blocked + trigger Postgres defense en profondeur + audit log immutable. Pattern industrie standard (Stripe payments, Shopify orders, Salesforce opportunities).

Livrables principaux : module `state-machine/sinistre-state-machine.ts` defines `VALID_TRANSITIONS` matrix readonly TypeScript structure avec 12 transitions explicit (from + to + required_actor_types[] + validator) + `INVALID_TRANSITIONS_EXAMPLES` 4+ entries documented avec error codes, service `SinistreLifecycleService.transition(sinistreId, toStatus, reason, actorId, actorType, metadata?): LifecycleResult` execute transaction atomic (SELECT FOR UPDATE sinistre + lookup current_status + verify transition matrix + verify actor in required types + execute validator + INSERT lifecycle_event audit + UPDATE status atomic + emit Kafka event transactional outbox + COMMIT), service `SinistreLifecycleService.getValidTransitions(currentStatus, actorType?): NextStatus[]`, service `SinistreLifecycleService.getHistory(sinistreId, pagination): PaginatedLifecycleEvents`, helper `TransitionValidators` per transition specific (expertise_requested requires metadata.expert_id valid), trigger Postgres `enforce_status_transition` defense en profondeur (verifie lifecycle_event correspondant < 5s avant UPDATE, throw exception si direct UPDATE), trigger `prevent_lifecycle_event_delete` empeche DELETE (append-only strict), telemetry `sinistre_transitions_total{from_status,to_status,actor_type}`, 18+ tests unitaires.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 10** :

- State machine relachee bypass via direct UPDATE Postgres : devs debug. Solution : trigger Postgres `enforce_status_transition` defense en profondeur, throw exception si direct UPDATE.
- Concurrent transitions same sinistre race conditions : 2 agents simultane = 2 events. Solution : SELECT FOR UPDATE strict pendant transition, lock duration < 500ms, retry pattern client-side.
- VALID_TRANSITIONS matrix evolution : add new transition requires code change. Solution : matrix code source-of-truth (not BDD config), changes via PR review.
- Actor type permission missing : transition allowed by actor not in required_actor_types. Solution : verify actorType in transition.required_actor_types[] strict, throw InvalidActorError.
- Transition validators side effects : validator queries BDD intensive. Solution : validators lightweight (max 1 query Postgres), heavy logic in service layer post-transition.
- Audit log volume explosion : 1000 sinistres * 5 transitions/jour = 1.8M/an. Solution : partition mensuelle + archive ClickHouse Sprint 13 apres 6 mois + drop Postgres apres 1 an.
- Trigger Postgres performance overhead : every status UPDATE triggered check. Solution : trigger lightweight EXISTS query indexed, P95 < 5ms overhead acceptable.
- Compensation transitions ambiguous : under_review -> declaration backward = compensation OR missing info ? Solution : 2 transitions distinctes avec validators diff, metadata.compensation_reason required.
- Append-only delete trigger conflict GDPR pseudonymisation : pseudonymisation update events. Solution : pseudonymisation update only (UPDATE actor_id), pas DELETE events.
- State machine v1 vs v2 futures evolutions ACAPS : breaking changes. Solution : version-aware structure `VALID_TRANSITIONS_V2024` / V2025 future, default current, migration plan.

### Task 5.1.4 -- ACAPS Compliance Validator Cas 4.7.2.2

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE | **Effort** : 6h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.3 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.5 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente le `AcapsComplianceValidatorService` avec validations strict cas usage 4.7.2.2 ACAPS Circulaire 02-AS/2024. Compliance ACAPS non negociable pour Programme Emergence ACAPS Skalean. Sans validator, declarations sinistres peuvent etre rejected par ACAPS lors audits reglementaires + impact reputation Skalean InsurTech MA.

Livrables principaux : constants `acaps-2024.constants.ts` (MIN/MAX description length, MIN/MAX montant MAD, MIN photos required, RETROACTIVE_DAYS_MAX, regex `CIN_MA_REGEX = /^[A-Z]{1,2}[0-9]{6,7}$/` + `PLAQUE_MA_REGEX = /^[0-9]{1,5}-[A-Z]{1,2}-[0-9]{1,3}$/`, REQUIRED_FIELDS_AUTO_TYPE + REQUIRED_FIELDS_ALL_TYPES), service `AcapsComplianceValidatorService.validate(declaration, version='2024'): Promise<ComplianceResult>` execute validations sequentielles (CIN format + numero police + plaque format si auto + date_sinistre range + lieu adresse + description range + montant range + photos minimum 3 + constat auto required + lookup customer Sprint 8 + lookup police Sprint 14 SEL + validate documents Sprint 10), helper `errorCodes` 15+ codes ACAPS_001 a ACAPS_020+ extensible, version-aware structure `validators: Record<string, AcapsValidator>` pour evolution circulaires futures, telemetry `acaps_validation_failures_total{error_code,tenant_id}`, 14+ tests scenarios reels couvrant tous codes + happy path + edge cases multi-erreurs + auto vs non-auto differential.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- ACAPS Circulaire interpretation litigieuses : clauses ambiguous. Solution : design doc 5.1.1 explicit assumptions + Abla CEO validation reseau ACAPS contacts, fallback strict interpretation safest.
- CIN format edge cases : initialles speciaux (P, Z) rare mais valides. Solution : regex tolere format variable, tests exhaustifs samples reels MA.
- Plaque format ancien vs nouveau : MA 2 formats coexistent. Solution : regex tolere variations standards, tests samples reels.
- Description multi-langue : prose francais + arabe. Solution : `description` field UTF-8 strict, longueur calcule en codepoints (pas bytes), tests samples FR + AR.
- Photos validation Sprint 10 dependency : Sprint 10 documents soft-deleted. Solution : query DocumentService.findActiveById, throw ACAPS_016 si document deleted.
- Customer Sprint 8 CIN encrypted lookup performance : decryption overhead. Solution : Sprint 8 CRM batch decryption cache, single lookup acceptable, P95 < 50ms target.
- SEL Sprint 14 police_lookup timeouts : insurer slow. Solution : timeout 5s strict + retry 2x + circuit breaker Sprint 14, warning compliance pas error si insurer offline temporaire.
- Version-aware future ACAPS 2025 : breaking changes possible. Solution : validators map per version + default current + migration plan circulaires futures.

### Task 5.1.5 -- SinistreService CRUD + Validations Schemas Avro

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 7h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.4 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.6 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente le `SinistreService` CRUD complet avec validations Zod canonical schemas Avro sinistre.v1 Sprint 15 SRF + integration Sprint 8 CRM customer lookup + ACAPS validator pre-flight + idempotency Sprint 15.

Livrables principaux : service `SinistreService.create(declaration, idempotencyKey?): Sinistre` (ACAPS pre-flight via 5.1.4 throw AcapsValidationError si invalid + Avro schema validation via Sprint 15 SchemaRegistryService + idempotency Sprint 15 IdempotencyService si key provided + generate correlation_id UUID v7 + INSERT sinistre + lines transaction + INSERT initial lifecycle_event status='declaration' + emit Kafka event declaration_submitted + return entity), `SinistreService.update(id, partial)` (validations Zod + si status change appel SinistreLifecycleService.transition jamais direct UPDATE + audit log Sprint 7), `SinistreService.findById(id, includeHistory)`, `SinistreService.findByCustomer(customerId, filters): PaginatedSinistre[]`, `SinistreService.findByCorrelation(correlationId): Sinistre[]` saga tracking, `SinistreService.findByPlaque(plaque): Sinistre[]` recherche vehicule, validations Zod entree+sortie strict via canonical schemas Sprint 15, integration Sprint 8 CRM CustomerService pour customer lookup avec CIN decryption on-demand audit log, integration Sprint 15 SRF Schema Registry pour Avro validation strict, telemetry `sinistre_created_total{type,branch}`, 16+ tests unitaires.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Direct UPDATE status bypass lifecycle service : SinistreService.update peut accidentellement faire UPDATE sinistres SET status. Solution : SinistreService.update detect status change + delegate `SinistreLifecycleService.transition` strict, code review enforce.
- Avro schema validation runtime cost : every create validates. Solution : pre-compile Avro schemas startup + reuse validators, P95 < 1ms validation overhead.
- Idempotency without TTL : duplicate retries weeks later returns stale. Solution : Sprint 15 IdempotencyService TTL configurable per operation, sinistres claims TTL 7j.
- Customer Sprint 8 CIN encrypted lookup recursive : tenant scoping. Solution : Sprint 8 findByCustomerId tenant-scoped strict via AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6.
- findByPlaque cross-tenant leak : plaque AAA-123-LL exists multiple tenants. Solution : query strict WHERE tenant_id + plaque, RLS enforce, audit log critical si cross-tenant attempt.
- Optimistic locking conflicts SinistreService.update : version mismatch. Solution : retry pattern client-side avec exponential backoff, max 3 retries, throw OptimisticLockingError final si exhausted.
- Schema validation errors generic : "validation failed" peu informatif. Solution : Zod error mapping field-level explicit + ACAPS errorCodes mapping, response structure { error_code, field, expected, received }.
- Pagination cursor vs offset : large list slow. Solution : cursor-based via `last_id` parameter + ORDER BY id ASC, LIMIT 50, telemetry pagination performance.

### Task 5.1.6 -- Integration Saga declare-claim Sprint 15 SRF

**Densite cible** : 140-180 KB | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE | **Effort** : 7h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.5 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.7 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task est le **proof point pratique** de l'investissement Sprint 15 SRF. Si saga declare-claim casse, l'argument "SRF gere workflows complexes" devient vide. Integration sagas saga declare-claim 4 etapes Sprint 15 SRF avec entites Sinistre reelles + lifecycle transitions + correlation_id end-to-end + compensations + idempotency.

Livrables principaux : service `SinistreService.declareClaim(declaration, idempotencyKey?): SinistreDeclarationResult` orchestrate (ACAPS validation pre-flight obligatoire + INSERT sinistre status='declaration' via 5.1.5 SinistreService.create + generate correlation_id UUID v7 + call `SagaOrchestratorService.startSaga('declare-claim', { sinistre_id, customer_id, vehicle_info, montant_estime, idempotency_key, correlation_id })` Sprint 15 4.2.8 + return acknowledgement avec saga_id), configuration saga listeners 'declare-claim' Sprint 15 (saga step3 success submit_claim_to_insurer + acknowledgement insurer -> trigger `SinistreLifecycleService.transition(sinistre_id, 'received', 'saga step3 success', system_saga_actor, 'system_saga')`, saga step3 fail -> compensation cancel_claim_at_insurer -> sinistre reste status='declaration' + notification customer Sprint 9, saga step4 notify_tenant_acknowledgement consume customer_id + sinistre_id -> Sprint 9 NotificationRouter), integration SEL Sprint 14 routeRequest dans saga steps (step2 lookup_policy_at_insurer via routeRequest('insurer.policy.lookup', { policy_number }, idempotency_key), step3 submit_claim_to_insurer via routeRequest('insurer.claim.declare', canonical_claim) avec Translation Engine Sprint 15), error handling exhaustive (saga step fail trigger compensation + sinistre transition received->declaration backward + audit log + notification customer), tests integration end-to-end saga + entities + lifecycle (CRITIQUE proof point Phase 3), 14+ tests integration couvrant happy path full saga complete + step failures + compensations + idempotency retries + correlation_id tracking end-to-end.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Saga step3 partial failure : claim submitted but acknowledgement timeout. Solution : timeout 5min strict + compensation cancel_claim_at_insurer + sinistre back to declaration + notification customer explicit "Tentez de nouveau".
- Correlation_id propagation broken end-to-end : Sprint 16 saga -> Sprint 14 SEL -> Sprint 9 notification perd correlation. Solution : header `X-Correlation-Id` propage strict every hop + audit log includes correlation_chain_position.
- Idempotency keys cross-tenant collision : Sprint 15 framework. Solution : Sprint 15 composite key `{tenant_id}:{key}` strict cross-tenant isolation.
- Compensation saga step3 fail too : cancel_claim_at_insurer also fail. Solution : Sprint 15 saga compensation retry exponential max 3 + escalation manual + audit critical.
- Real Sprint 14 SEL vs mocked tests : tests integration require Sprint 14 SEL up. Solution : Sprint 16 tests integration spin Sprint 14 cluster local docker-compose + fictional insurers Sprint 14 4.1.8 mocked endpoints.
- Saga timeout policy aggressive : insurer slow legitimately. Solution : timeouts configurable per partner Service Registry Sprint 14, governmental 30min insurer 15min adaptive.
- Sprint 15 SagaOrchestratorService import : circular dependency Sprint 15 -> Sprint 16. Solution : Sprint 16 depend Sprint 15 (one-way), Sprint 15 sagas don't import Sprint 16 entities (use interfaces).
- Saga test fixtures complex : 4 etapes + compensations + multiple sprints. Solution : test fixtures dedicated `saga-declare-claim-fixtures.ts` complete scenarios + helpers setup/teardown.

### Task 5.1.7 -- SinistreEventEmitter + Kafka Events Lifecycle

**Densite cible** : 110-150 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.6 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.8 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente `SinistreEventEmitter` publish Kafka events sur chaque transition lifecycle consume Sprint 9 + Sprint 13 + Sprint 15 sagas. 8 topics distincts per status + transactional outbox pattern strict + listeners 3 sprints downstream.

Livrables principaux : service `SinistreEventEmitter.emit(event: SinistreLifecycleKafkaEvent): void` publish Kafka topic specific per status, 8 topics distincts (`insurtech.events.sinistre.declaration_submitted`, `.received_by_insurer`, `.under_review`, `.expertise_requested`, `.payout_decided`, `.payment_executing`, `.repair_completed`, `.finalized`), event schema canonical Avro saga_state.v1 Sprint 15 (id + sinistre_id + tenant_id + from_status + to_status + actor + timestamp + correlation_id + metadata), transactional outbox pattern strict (INSERT outbox_kafka_events dans transaction Postgres lifecycle Sprint 16 5.1.3 + INSERT outbox + Publisher worker Kafka async consume outbox + retry exponential backoff sur fail + idempotent via event_id UUID v7), Sprint 9 NotificationRouter listeners 8 topics avec templates per transition (declaration_submitted -> Email customer, received_by_insurer -> Push customer + Email tenant agent, payout_decided approved -> Email customer urgent + agent tenant), Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline `sinistre-pipeline.ts` consume 8 topics + sync ClickHouse pour dashboards Sprint 28, Sprint 15 sagas listeners specifiques (approve-payout saga listen payout_decided -> auto-start saga si approved, finalize-claim saga listen repair_completed -> auto-start finalize), telemetry `sinistre_events_published_total{event_type,status}`, 12+ tests integration.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Transactional outbox publisher lag : Kafka events delayed. Solution : publisher worker max polling 1s + alert si lag > 30s sustained.
- Kafka event ordering critical : payout_decided BEFORE payment_executing. Solution : Kafka topic partition by sinistre_id strict, events ordered per sinistre.
- Listeners failure silent : Sprint 9 down = notifications lost. Solution : retry exponential + dead letter queue + alert ops si dead letter > 10/min.
- Event schema evolution : add field sinistre_state.v2 breaking. Solution : Sprint 15 SRF compatibility checker backward compat enforced.
- Cross-tenant event leak : tenant A event consume by tenant B listener. Solution : event payload include tenant_id + listener filter explicit, audit log cross-tenant attempts.
- Event volume explosion : 1000 sinistres * 5 transitions * 3 listeners = 15000 events/jour. Solution : Kafka throughput sized + telemetry monitoring, Sprint 13 ETL batch consume optimized.
- Outbox table volume : 1M events accumulated. Solution : cleanup published events apres 7j (audit Postgres preserve via Sprint 7 audit log separate).
- Event deserialization failures consumer : Avro schema mismatch. Solution : Sprint 15 SchemaRegistry consumer verify schema version + fallback to compatible.

### Task 5.1.8 -- Documents Sprint 10 References + Photos

**Densite cible** : 110-150 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.7 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.9 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente `SinistreDocument` references Sprint 10 documents (constat amiable + photos + expertises + factures garage). Photos validation cle ACAPS 4.7.2.2 minimum 3 photos required.

Livrables principaux : entite `SinistreDocument` (id + sinistre_id FK + document_id_sprint10 reference + document_type enum constat_amiable/photo_dommage/photo_lieu/expertise_rapport/facture_garage/expertise_complementaire/photo_carte_grise/photo_permis/autre + uploaded_at + uploaded_by_id + ordre_affichage INT + is_soft_deleted), migration table `sinistre_documents` + RLS + indexes, service `SinistreDocumentService.attachDocument(sinistreId, documentId, type, actorId): SinistreDocument` (Sprint 10 DocumentService lookup verify document exists + tenant scope verified + type-specific validation constat_amiable accept PDF + photos accept JPG/PNG/HEIC max 20 MB + factures PDF + INSERT reference avec audit log Sprint 7 + emit Kafka event sinistre.document_attached + verify minimum 3 photos required ACAPS si transition next status), service `SinistreDocumentService.listDocuments(sinistreId, type?): SinistreDocument[]` paginated, service `SinistreDocumentService.detachDocument(id, actorId)` soft delete avec audit log critical, permission Sprint 7.5a `sinistre.document.attach` + `sinistre.document.detach`, helper `validateDocumentType(documentId, expectedType)` type-specific MIME validation, 12+ tests integration.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Document Sprint 10 deleted but reference exists : soft delete sync. Solution : query Sprint 10 DocumentService.findActiveById strict + return error si deleted + audit log critical.
- Photos validation MIME bypass : extension change. Solution : Sprint 10 DocumentService MIME detection magic bytes (not extension), validate match expected types.
- Constat amiable not PDF : photo uploaded as constat. Solution : type-specific MIME validation strict + reject 422 if mismatch.
- Tenant scope leak documents : tenant A attaches tenant B document. Solution : Sprint 10 findActiveById tenant-scoped via AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6.
- Soft delete vs hard delete : audit ACAPS retention. Solution : soft delete preserve data 10 ans + 1 jour + hard delete uniquement apres retention period CRON.
- Ordre_affichage user-controlled : reordering API. Solution : endpoint reorder dedicated PUT /sinistres/{id}/documents/reorder accept array document_ids dans nouvel ordre.
- Max 20 photos enforced : customer uploads 21. Solution : check count before INSERT + throw error 422 + clear message.
- Detach last constat amiable auto : violates ACAPS. Solution : pre-detach check : si type=constat_amiable et sinistre_type=auto et autres constats=0, refuse detach.

### Task 5.1.9 -- Endpoints REST Sinistres

**Densite cible** : 130-170 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 6h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.8 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.10 a 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task expose endpoints REST `/api/v1/sinistres` complets consumables par Sprint 18 Customer Portal + Sprint 19 Mobile + Sprint 26 Admin UI + Sprint 28 Reports.

Livrables principaux : controller `sinistre.controller.ts` avec 12 endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/sinistres` create declaration declenche saga + return acknowledgement
- `GET /api/v1/sinistres` list paginated filterable status/type/customer/period/plaque
- `GET /api/v1/sinistres/{id}` details complets + lifecycle events history optional via `?include_history=true`
- `GET /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/lifecycle-events` history paginated
- `POST /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/transition` manual transition specific status + reason + actorId
- `POST /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/documents` attach document Sprint 10 reference
- `GET /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/documents` list documents attached
- `POST /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/acaps-compliance-check` pre-flight validation cas 4.7.2.2
- `GET /api/v1/sinistres/by-correlation/{correlationId}` saga tracking
- `GET /api/v1/sinistres/statistics` summary metrics tenant
- `POST /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/manual-compensate` super-admin force compensation
- `DELETE /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/pseudonymize` super-admin GDPR/CNDP

OpenAPI 3.1 schemas via decorateurs NestJS, validation Zod via canonical schemas Sprint 15, permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires (`sinistre.read/create/update/transition`, `sinistre.document.attach`, `sinistre.admin.compensate`, `sinistre.admin.pseudonymize`), rate limit 100 req/min/tenant, idempotency-Key header support via Sprint 15 IdempotencyInterceptor, 16+ tests E2E.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Permission granularity confusion : sinistre.read vs sinistre.admin.read cross-tenant. Solution : permissions distinctes documented + RBAC matrix actor_role x permissions strict.
- Rate limit 100 req/min/tenant trop bas Customer Portal : Sprint 18 polling 5s = 12/min OK, 1s = 60/min limit. Solution : Sprint 18 documentation refresh >= 5s.
- Pagination cursor-based vs offset : large list. Solution : cursor-based via `?cursor={last_id}` + LIMIT 50 default + max 100.
- Idempotency-Key auto vs explicit : POST sinistres optional. Solution : Sprint 15 IdempotencyInterceptor decorateur `@Idempotent('sinistre.create')` extracts X-Idempotency-Key header + applies framework.
- Statistics endpoint expensive : aggregation 100k sinistres. Solution : queries pre-aggregated via Sprint 13 ClickHouse + cache Redis 5min.
- Manual compensate endpoint dangerous : super-admin force state change. Solution : confirmation_code required + audit log critical + notification ops team.
- OpenAPI schemas vs Zod source-of-truth : drift. Solution : `zod-to-openapi` lib auto-generate OpenAPI from Zod, single source truth.
- Mobile vs web differential response : Sprint 19 mobile prefers compact. Solution : `?compact=true` query parameter retourne version compact (no description full, no lifecycle history).

### Task 5.1.10 -- Pseudonymisation GDPR/CNDP

**Densite cible** : 110-150 KB | **Priorite** : P1 COMPLIANCE | **Effort** : 5h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.9 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.11, 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task implemente pseudonymisation customer apres customer_deletion_request loi 09-08 CNDP preservant retention 10 ans + 1 jour ACAPS (decision-008). Conflict apparent : loi CNDP permet customer demander suppression PII mais ACAPS Circulaire requires 10 ans retention. Solution : pseudonymisation customer (PII -> hash) avec sinistre data preserve.

Livrables principaux : service `SinistrePseudonymizationService.pseudonymizeCustomer(customerId, requesterId, reason): PseudonymizationResult` (verify Sprint 8 CustomerService.findById deletion_request_pending=true + hash CIN -> sha256 unique pseudonymous_id 32 chars hex + UPDATE sinistres SET customer_id_sprint8 = pseudonymous_id WHERE customer_id_sprint8 = customerId + UPDATE sinistre_lifecycle_events SET actor_id = pseudonymous_id WHERE actor_id = customerId AND actor_type='customer' + UPDATE sinistre_documents SET uploaded_by_id = pseudonymous_id + audit log GDPR-event dedicated table `gdpr_pseudonymization_events` event_id + customer_id_original_hashed + pseudonymous_id + requester_id + reason + timestamp + sinistres_affected_count + Sprint 8 CRM CustomerService soft delete + replace CIN avec sha256 + preserve data structure operational pour ACAPS audit 10 ans + 1 jour + notification customer email confirmation), endpoint admin `DELETE /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/pseudonymize` super-admin Skalean only permission `sinistre.admin.pseudonymize` + audit critical + co-signature Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow 2 personnes DPO + super-admin, idempotent (re-run safe), telemetry `pseudonymization_events_total`, documentation `docs/gdpr-cndp-pseudonymization.md`, 10+ tests integration.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- ACAPS retention conflict GDPR : tension reglementaire. Solution : pseudonymisation preserve data structure + PII pseudonymized, audit log GDPR + ACAPS reconciliation documented.
- Co-signature DPO unavailable : process blocked. Solution : DPO backup designated + escalation hierarchie + audit log critical si bypass.
- Idempotent re-run pseudonymisation : double-apply sha256(sha256). Solution : skip si customer_id_sprint8 deja format pseudonymous (length 32 hex), check pattern.
- Audit GDPR event vs ACAPS retention : audit deleted accidentellement. Solution : table `gdpr_pseudonymization_events` retention 10 ans + 1 jour aligned ACAPS.
- Pseudonymous_id collision : sha256(CIN) collision astronomical low mais theorique. Solution : `pseudonymous_id = sha256(tenant_id + customer_cin + salt)`, salt per tenant evite collision.
- Right erasure customer reconnect : meme CIN reapparait Sprint 8. Solution : Sprint 8 customer reconnect = nouveau customer_id (pas reuse pseudonymous_id), sinistres anciens reste pseudonymized.
- ACAPS audit demand post-pseudonymisation : data preserved pseudonymous OK ? Solution : ACAPS accept pseudonymous (no PII required for audit, structural integrity sufficient), document audit doc 5.1.1.
- Notification customer pseudonymisation completed : sensitive email content. Solution : email template generic "Votre demande de suppression a ete traitee" sans details, audit log dedicated.

### Task 5.1.11 -- Public Interfaces Sprint 17-20 Preparation

**Densite cible** : 100-140 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 4h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.10 | **Dependances sortantes** : 5.1.12

**Scope precis** :

Cette task expose interfaces TypeScript publiques consume par sprints Phase 4 downstream (Sprint 17 Police Lifecycle + Sprint 18 Customer Portal + Sprint 19 Mobile + Sprint 20 IA Estimation). Decouplage clean : sprints downstream depend interfaces stables, pas implementations internes Sprint 16.

Livrables principaux : interface publique `@insurtech/sinistre/src/public.ts` exporte 4 interfaces stables :
- `SinistreReader` methodes findById, findByCustomer, listByStatus pour Sprint 17+18+19
- `SinistreLifecycleObserver` subscribe events lifecycle pour Sprint 18 Customer Portal real-time updates
- `SinistreEstimationProvider` preparation Sprint 20 IA methods getCurrentEstimate, updateEstimate, attachPhotoEstimate
- `PoliceLinkInterface` preparation Sprint 17 methods linkPolice, validatePoliceCoverage

Documentation `docs/sinistre-public-api.md` usage patterns + sequence diagrams Sprint 17/18/19/20 consumers, tests verification stability interfaces (any change interface signature fail tests = breaking change detected automatically), 8+ tests verification.

**Pieges specifiques minimum 8** :

- Interface evolution v1 -> v2 breaking : Sprint 17+ broken. Solution : interfaces versioning explicit `SinistreReaderV1` / V2, deprecation gracieuse 2 versions max.
- Implementation leaks public interface : exposing internal entities. Solution : DTOs explicit pour public interface (`SinistreDto` distinct `Sinistre` entity), serialization clean.
- Subscription leaks memory : observer subscriptions not unsubscribed. Solution : Subscription pattern RxJS-style returned + auto-unsubscribe on disconnect.
- Public interface tests skip during refactor : "fix later" attitude. Solution : CI hook block PR merge si interface tests fail, peer-review required signature changes.
- Cross-tenant access via public interfaces : Sprint 17 tenant_id different. Solution : public interfaces tenant-scoped via AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6, throw cross-tenant if mismatch.
- Async vs sync interface signatures : Promise<T> vs T confusion. Solution : all public interfaces async (Promise<T>) default, sync only for pure helpers.
- Documentation drift : code change but docs not updated. Solution : doc generation auto via TSDoc + CI verify doc coverage 100% public exports.
- Sprint 20 IA estimation interface design uncertain : Sprint 20 IA scope evolving. Solution : `SinistreEstimationProvider` minimal viable interface Sprint 16 + extensible Sprint 20.

### Task 5.1.12 -- Tests E2E 30+ + Sprint 16 Closure

**Densite cible** : 200-280 KB | **Priorite** : P0 | **Effort** : 12h
**Dependances entrantes** : 5.1.1 a 5.1.11 | **Dependances sortantes** : V-16 verification finale + Sprint 17 demarrage

**Scope precis** :

Cette task finalise sprint 16 avec tests E2E exhaustifs + rapport closure + Phase 4 Sprint 1 milestone. 30+ scenarios E2E organises en 6 suites + benchmarks performance critiques + rapport closure documente exhaustivement.

Livrables principaux : fixtures realistic complets (50 customers Sprint 8 + 100 sinistres divers statuses repartition lifecycle 8 etats + lifecycle events history + 200 documents references Sprint 10 + 6 partners Service Registry Sprint 14 + signed certs Sprint 14 4.1.3), 30+ tests E2E organises :

- **Suite lifecycle complete happy path** 6 tests : declaration full path 8 etats sequential + transition each step verified audit log + alternative paths (sans repair, with rejected payout, dispute path)
- **Suite state machine validations** 6 tests : 12 transitions valides verified, 4+ transitions invalides rejected, concurrent transitions serialised via SELECT FOR UPDATE, backward compensation under_review->declaration, trigger Postgres bypass direct UPDATE blocked, append-only DELETE blocked
- **Suite ACAPS compliance 4.7.2.2** 6 tests : 10+ scenarios reels valides+invalides cas 4.7.2.2 (CIN format valid, CIN 7 chars invalide rejected, plaque format valid, plaque format invalide rejected, montant range, photos minimum 3 enforced, constat auto required, date retroactive max 1 an, description range)
- **Suite integration sagas Sprint 15 proof point** 6 tests : declare-claim saga end-to-end success full 4 etapes, step3 failure compensation cancel_claim_at_insurer + sinistre back to declaration, idempotency retries same key returns cached, correlation_id tracking end-to-end Sprint 16 + Sprint 14 + Sprint 15 + Sprint 9 notifications
- **Suite integration Sprint 8+9+10+13+14+15** 4 tests : customer Sprint 8 lookup CIN encrypted decryption audit, notification Sprint 9 lifecycle email + push templates per transition, documents Sprint 10 attach + ACAPS validation min 3 photos, analytics Sprint 13 events ETL ClickHouse
- **Suite Pseudonymisation GDPR/CNDP** 2 tests : customer deletion request pseudonymize sinistres preserve ACAPS retention 10 ans + 1 jour, audit log GDPR event dedicated, idempotent re-run safe

Benchmarks performance : `bench-sinistre-create.sh` P95 < 500ms inclus saga launch, `bench-lifecycle-transition.sh` P95 < 200ms, `bench-list-paginated.sh` P95 < 300ms sur 100k sinistres fixtures, `bench-acaps-validation.sh` P99 < 50ms. Coverage globale Sprint 16 >= 90% lignes + 85% branches. Documentation `tests/e2e/SCENARIOS-SINISTRE.md` exhaustive.

Rapport closure Sprint 16 `00-pilotage/reports/sprint-16-closure-report.md` documenter (Sinistre domain model operational 6 entites + RLS + indexes, state machine 8 etats + 12 transitions valides operational, ACAPS 4.7.2.2 compliance verified, integration sagas Sprint 15 proof point reussi, documents references Sprint 10 attach + types validation, Kafka events 8 topics + consumers Sprint 9+13+15, pseudonymisation GDPR/CNDP + audit preserve ACAPS retention, public interfaces Sprint 17-20 stables, pre-requis Sprint 17 Police Lifecycle valides, risques residuels documentes (vraies compagnies Sprint 32 + IA Sprint 20 + UX Sprint 18-19), recommandation GO Sprint 17 si score V-16 >= 95%).

**Pieges specifiques minimum 10** :

- 30+ tests E2E volume time consuming : 30 tests * 30s avg = 15min CI. Solution : parallelization vitest workers + isolation per suite via transaction rollback + Kafka topics dedicated test prefix.
- Saga integration tests require Sprint 14 + 15 cluster up : complex local setup. Solution : docker-compose local dev avec full stack Sprint 14 SEL + Sprint 15 SRF + tests integration setup helpers.
- Fixtures customers Sprint 8 CIN encrypted : encryption keys management tests. Solution : Sprint 8 test fixtures generate via real CRM encryption pattern + test keys distinct prod.
- Concurrent transitions test fragile : timing race conditions test flaky. Solution : tests serialize via Postgres transactions + retry patterns + test stability metrics.
- ACAPS validation tests cover all 15+ error codes : tedious. Solution : parametrized tests via vitest test.each + matrix scenarios + maintainable.
- Coverage 90% threshold strict last 5% : difficult final branches. Solution : focus essential paths + edge cases secondary + coverage exclude generated migrations.
- Closure report exhaustivite : 12 tasks + 8 integrations + 17 decisions documenter. Solution : template structure exhaustive (section per task + integrations + decisions + technical debt + recommendation GO), 8-12 pages length acceptable.
- Sprint 17 prerequisites validation : Sprint 17 Police Lifecycle requires Sinistre foundation. Solution : checklist explicit dans closure report (`[ ] Sinistre entities operational, [ ] State machine 12 transitions valides, [ ] ACAPS validator 4.7.2.2, [ ] Saga integration proof point, [ ] Public interfaces stable, [ ] Tests E2E 30+ PASS`).
- Risques residuels documentation trop vague : "risques identifies" insuffisant. Solution : matrix risks structure (risk_id + description + impact + mitigation + owner + target_phase) traceable.
- Demo Day 30 juin 2026 deadline tight : Sprint 16 cloture critique. Solution : closure report inclut Demo Day readiness assessment + recommendation Sprint 17-20 acceleration si needed.

---

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Tout fichier task respecte format strict : headers Markdown ATX (`# ## ###`) uniquement, code blocks avec language tag explicite (`bash`, `sql`, `typescript`, `xml`, `yaml`, `json`, `avro`, `mermaid`), tableaux Markdown formates pipes alignes (`|`), listes a puces avec `- ` (jamais `* `), listes numerotees `1. 2. 3.` (jamais `1) 2)`), liens hyperliens toujours formates `[text](path)` (jamais URLs raw), code blocks pour configurations longues + commentaires inline, sequence diagrams Mermaid markdown pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Le style est dense et professionnel : prose dense privilegiee aux bullet points dans sections explicatives (sections 3, 4, 8), paragraphes longs 6-12 lignes justifies (sauf code blocks et tableaux), voix active preferee passive, niveau technique senior (assumer competence NestJS/TypeScript/PostgreSQL/Kafka/Redis/Avro/ACAPS/state machines), terminologie metier consistante (Sinistre = claim, declaration = initial submission, lifecycle = workflow lifecycle, transitions = state changes, ACAPS = Autorite Controle Assurances Prevoyance Sociale MA, CNDP = Commission Nationale Donnees Personnelles, decision-017 = state machine 8 etats), references industry standards explicit (state machine pattern, Stripe payments lifecycle).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task doit mentionner explicitement minimum 4 decisions strategiques (001-017), **decision-017 obligatoire toutes tasks 5.1.X** car ratifiee dans 5.1.1 et application directe Sinistre, **decisions-015 SEL + 016 SRF obligatoires** sur tasks integration (5.1.6 saga critique), minimum 1 reference au B-16 sprint specification, minimum 1 reference a une task voisine (5.1.X), minimum 1 reference au V-16 verification criteria correspondant, minimum 1 reference aux sprints precedents (1-15) pertinents.

### Tailles cibles synthese

| Task | Min KB | Max KB | Densite |
|------|--------|--------|---------|
| 5.1.1 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (architecture + decision + design doc) |
| 5.1.2 | 130 | 170 | Forte (5 tables + RLS + indexes + entites) |
| 5.1.3 | 140 | 180 | Tres forte (CRITIQUE state machine 8 etats + triggers) |
| 5.1.4 | 130 | 170 | Forte (ACAPS compliance critique) |
| 5.1.5 | 130 | 170 | Forte (SinistreService CRUD + Avro) |
| 5.1.6 | 140 | 180 | Tres forte (CRITIQUE proof point Phase 3 saga integration) |
| 5.1.7 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (event emitter + 8 topics) |
| 5.1.8 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (documents Sprint 10) |
| 5.1.9 | 130 | 170 | Forte (12 REST endpoints + permissions) |
| 5.1.10 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (Pseudonymisation GDPR/CNDP) |
| 5.1.11 | 100 | 140 | Moyenne (Public interfaces Sprint 17-20) |
| 5.1.12 | 200 | 280 | Tres forte (30+ E2E + Sprint 16 closure) |

**Total Sprint 16 attendu : 1.54 - 2.08 MB sur les 12 tasks.**

---

## WORKFLOW D'EXECUTION RECOMMANDE

### Pour chaque task

```
1. Lire B-16 -- section "Tache 5.1.{Z}" (scope) et "Frontiere du sprint" (limites)
2. Lire V-16 -- criteres V{N} attribues a la task
3. Lire C-16 -- extrait de la task dans orchestrateur (dependances pratiques)
4. Lire 2-3 tasks deja generees voisines pour calibrer style et profondeur
5. Lire decisions/001-017 -- decision-017 OBLIGATOIRE toutes tasks 5.1.X, decisions 015+016 obligatoires tasks integration
6. Lire template 03-template-task.md -- valider 11 sections obligatoires
7. Rediger fichier task-5.1.{Z}-{slug}.md dans `prompts-taches/sprint-16-sinistre-foundation/`
8. Verifier taille finale dans la fourchette cible
9. Verifier aucune emoji presente
10. Commit Conventional Commits : `feat(prompts-taches): add task-5.1.{Z} prompt for Sprint 16`
```

### Si une task fail la verification format

Identifier la section manquante ou non conforme, lire la task voisine deja generee qui contient cette section bien faite, reecrire la section en alignant style et profondeur, re-verifier toutes les contraintes, re-commit.

### Si task taille hors fourchette

Trop courte : approfondir Sections 4 (Contexte etendu) et 8 (Implementation), pas Section 5 (Objectif). Trop longue : reduire exemples redondants dans Section 8 (garder un exemple par concept), pas reduire pieges Section 4.5.

### Sequencement critique 5.1.1 avant tout

Task 5.1.1 (Architecture + decision-017 + design doc) **doit etre peer-reviewed Saad CTO + Abla CEO** avant generation tasks 5.1.2+. Sans ratification formelle decision-017, l'investissement 70h des tasks suivantes risque mauvaise direction.

### Integration saga 5.1.6 CRITIQUE proof point

Task 5.1.6 est le proof point pratique de l'investissement Phase 3 (Sprint 14 SEL + Sprint 15 SRF). Generation prompt insiste sur tests integration sagas + correlation_id end-to-end + compensations idempotent. Si task 5.1.6 prompt ne couvre pas exhaustivement, regenerer prompt.

## VERIFICATION FINALE APRES GENERATION 12 TASKS

```bash
cd 00-pilotage/prompts-taches/sprint-16-sinistre-foundation/

# Verifier presence et taille
ls -la task-5.1.*.md
wc -c task-5.1.*.md

# Verifier total (~1.54 - 2.08 MB)
TOTAL_BYTES=$(wc -c task-5.1.*.md | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}')
TOTAL_MB=$(echo "scale=2; $TOTAL_BYTES / 1048576" | bc)
echo "Total : $TOTAL_MB MB (cible 1.54 - 2.08 MB)"

# Aucune emoji
grep -P "[\x{1F300}-\x{1F9FF}]|[\x{2600}-\x{27BF}]" task-5.1.*.md && echo "EMOJI DETECTED -- FAIL" || echo "No emoji -- OK"

# Sections obligatoires
for task in task-5.1.*.md; do
  for section in "TACHE 5.1." "Sprint :" "## 1. But" "## 2. Contexte" "## 3. Objectif" "## 4. Etat" "## 5. References" "## 6. Implementation" "## 7. Tests" "## 8. Criteres" "## 9. Commandes"; do
    grep -q "$section" "$task" || echo "$task: MISSING $section"
  done
done

# References B-16
for task in task-5.1.*.md; do
  grep -q "B-16-sprint-16" "$task" || echo "$task : MISSING reference B-16"
done

# Decisions strategiques minimum 4 par task
for task in task-5.1.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "decision-00[1-9]|decision-01[0-7]" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 4 ]; then
    echo "$task : Only $COUNT decisions (min 4)"
  fi
done

# Decision-017 obligatoire toutes tasks
for task in task-5.1.*.md; do
  if ! grep -q "decision-017" "$task"; then
    echo "$task : MISSING decision-017 (OBLIGATOIRE)"
  fi
done

# Decisions-015 SEL + 016 SRF obligatoires task 5.1.6 (integration saga)
if ! grep -q "decision-015" task-5.1.6*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.6 : MISSING decision-015 SEL"
fi
if ! grep -q "decision-016" task-5.1.6*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.6 : MISSING decision-016 SRF"
fi

# Pieges techniques minimum 8 par task
for task in task-5.1.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "Piege [0-9]+ :|piege technique" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 8 ]; then
    echo "$task : Only $COUNT pieges (min 8)"
  fi
done

# State machine reference dans task 5.1.3
if ! grep -qE "state machine|VALID_TRANSITIONS|enforce_status_transition" task-5.1.3*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.3 : MISSING reference state machine"
fi

# ACAPS reference dans task 5.1.4
if ! grep -qE "ACAPS|4\.7\.2\.2|CIN_MA_REGEX" task-5.1.4*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.4 : MISSING reference ACAPS"
fi

# Saga integration dans task 5.1.6
if ! grep -qE "saga.*declare-claim|SagaOrchestratorService\.startSaga|correlation_id" task-5.1.6*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.6 : MISSING reference saga integration"
fi

# Kafka events dans task 5.1.7
if ! grep -qE "Kafka|8 topics|transactional outbox" task-5.1.7*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.7 : MISSING reference Kafka events"
fi

# Pseudonymisation dans task 5.1.10
if ! grep -qE "pseudonymisation|GDPR|CNDP|sha256" task-5.1.10*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.10 : MISSING reference pseudonymisation"
fi

# Sprint 16 closure dans task 5.1.12
if ! grep -qE "Sprint 16 closure|Phase 4 Sprint 1|sprint-16-closure-report" task-5.1.12*.md; then
  echo "task-5.1.12 : MISSING reference Sprint 16 closure"
fi
```

Si tous checks passent : Sprint 16 pret pour execution par C-16 orchestrateur.

### Lectures obligatoires AVANT generation

2. **decisions/001-018** -- 015 SEL + 016 SRF + 017 Sinistre + 018 Police obligatoires
3. **templates/03-template-task.md** -- 11 sections obligatoires
4. **ACAPS Circulaire 02-AS/2024** -- cas 4.7.2.1 + 4.7.2.3

### Coherence sprints 1-16 implementes : `@insurtech/{auth,multi-tenant,rbac,crm,comm,docs-signature,pay-ma-multi,books-compliance,analytics}` + `@insurtech/skalean-{ca,security-server,exchange-hub,audit-hub}` + `@insurtech/skalean-srf-*` + `@insurtech/sinistre` (PoliceLinkInterface defined).

## STRUCTURE OBLIGATOIRE DE CHAQUE TASK -- 11 sections

1. TITRE / 2. METADONNEES (decisions 015+016+017+018 obligatoires) / 3. BUT 3 paragraphes prose dense / 4. CONTEXTE ETENDU (4.1 pourquoi, 4.2 alternatives, 4.3 trade-offs, 4.4 decisions, 4.5 pieges min 8) / 5. OBJECTIF liste 12+ items / 6. ETAT REPO / 7. REFERENCES CONFIG / 8. IMPLEMENTATION REQUISE 50-70% taille / 9. TESTS 12+ unitaires + 5+ integration + 3+ E2E / 10. CRITERES VALIDATION / 11. COMMANDES + NOTES

## TASKS A GENERER -- SCOPES

### Task 5.2.1 -- Architecture Base + Decision-018 + Design Doc

**Densite** : 110-150 KB | **P0** | **5h** | Dependances : Sprint 16 closure

**Scope precis** : Gateway architectural Sprint 17. Decision-018 ratifiee + design doc 20+ pages + peer-review Saad+Abla obligatoire. Sprint 17 complete Phase 4 foundation (Sinistre + Police = domain model assurance complet).

**Livrables** : `decisions/018-police-lifecycle-state-machine.md` 8-12 pages (titre + date 2 juin 2026 + status Accepte + contexte + decision state machine 5 etats + 8 transitions + timer-driven workflows + coverage validation + alternatives Temporal/BPMN/custom + consequences), `docs/architecture/police-foundation-design.md` 20+ pages (executive summary + state machine diagram + sequence diagrams sagas + timer cron flows + coverage validation algorithm + ACAPS matrix + integration Sprint 16 PoliceLinkInterface + cross-tenant SEL endpoint + roadmap Phase 7+), `00-pilotage/reports/sprint-17-kickoff-report.md`, peer-review signatures audit trail.

**Pieges minimum 8** :
- State machine Police vs Sinistre confusion (event vs time-driven) -> design doc tableau comparatif
- Alternatives Temporal attractive -> justifier rejet hosting US/EU + license + roadmap Phase 7+
- ACAPS 4.7.2.1 vs 4.7.2.3 confusion -> 2 matrices distinctes
- Coverage validation algorithm complexity 5 checks + aggregation -> design doc detaille + cache strategy
- Timer cron @nestjs/schedule choix -> alternatives evaluees documentees
- Cross-tenant SEL security threat model -> threat model documented endpoint
- Peer-review skip tentation -> audit trail signatures obligatoire
- Roadmap Phase 7+ absent -> section Future Evolution explicit

### Task 5.2.2 -- Entites Police + RLS + Indexes Performance

**Densite** : 130-170 KB | **P0** | **6h** | Dependances : 5.2.1

**Scope precis** : 6 entites domain Police TypeORM + relations + RLS strict + indexes performance critiques. Foundation refactor costly si mal designed.

**Livrables** : Package `@insurtech/police` v1.0.0, migration `CreatePoliceEntities` 6 tables (`polices` aggregate root avec UNIQUE composite insurer_partner_id+police_number + renewal_date_target GENERATED column, `police_adhesions` multi-personnes, `police_coverages` garanties + plafonds + franchises + exclusions JSONB, `police_branches` association table, `police_lifecycle_events` audit append-only partition mensuelle, `police_renewal_requests` historique), RLS active 6 tables, enums TypeScript PoliceStatus 5 + PoliceType 7 + GaranteeCode 7 + AdhesionRole 5, indexes critiques (tenant_id+status partial, tenant_id+expiration_date pour cron, UNIQUE police_number per insurer, previous_police_id renewal chains, lifecycle_events partition timestamp), seeds 7+7+5, 16+ tests.

**Pieges minimum 8** :
- renewal_date_target GENERATED column STORED syntax Postgres
- UNIQUE constraint composite police_number cross-insurer collision
- Partition mensuelle queries cross-partition slow -> filter timestamp obligatoire
- Customer_id_sprint8 soft FK couplage
- Optimistic locking @VersionColumn retry pattern
- Police_branches association table queries multiples
- Adhesions cin_pseudonymous nullable initial NULL
- Branches_changes JSONB structure standardisee

### Task 5.2.3 -- State Machine 5 Etats + 8 Transitions + 3 Cron Jobs

**Densite** : 150-200 KB | **P0 CRITIQUE** | **8h** | Dependances : 5.2.2

**Scope precis** : State machine 5 etats avec 8 transitions valides + 4+ invalides + trigger Postgres defense + audit append-only + **3 cron jobs daily timer-driven** (distinguant Sprint 16 event-driven).

**Livrables** : Module `state-machine/police-state-machine.ts` VALID_TRANSITIONS matrix 8 entries + INVALID_EXAMPLES 4+ entries, service `PoliceLifecycleService.transition()` execute transaction atomic (SELECT FOR UPDATE + verify matrix + actor + validator + INSERT lifecycle_event + UPDATE status + emit Kafka outbox + COMMIT), service `getValidTransitions` + `getHistory`, helper TransitionValidators per transition, trigger Postgres `enforce_police_status_transition` + `prevent_police_lifecycle_event_delete` append-only, service `PoliceRenewalCronService` avec 3 scheduled jobs @nestjs/schedule timezone 'Africa/Casablanca' :
- `initiateRenewals()` daily 02h00 MA : query polices status='active' AND expiration_date BETWEEN now AND now + INTERVAL '30 days' -> batch process 10/sec rate limit -> trigger renewal saga Sprint 15 -> transition active -> renewal + Sprint 9 notification
- `expirePolices()` daily 02h30 MA : query polices status='active' AND expiration_date < now AND NOT IN renewal -> transition active -> expired + notification
- `expireAbandonedRenewals()` daily 03h00 MA : query polices status='renewal' AND renewal_initiated_at > now - INTERVAL '30 days' AND new_police_id IS NULL -> transition renewal -> expired + notification "abandonne"

Tests time-travel @nestjs/schedule mocking, telemetry, 18+ tests.

**Pieges minimum 10** :
- Direct UPDATE status bypass -> trigger Postgres enforce
- Cron jobs concurrent overlap -> distributed lock Redis cron_lock TTL 1h
- Cron timezone UTC vs MA -> timeZone 'Africa/Casablanca' explicit
- Batch processing rate limit Postgres lock contention -> 10 sagas/sec
- Cron time-travel testing delicate -> SchedulerRegistry mocking
- Saga renewal trigger async vs await -> startSaga retourne id, cron pas await completion
- Daily cron failures silent -> telemetry + alert Sprint 9 si pas execute > 26h
- Renewal initiation duplicate multiple instances -> distributed lock + idempotency
- Expiration vs renewal race condition -> check expiration_date - 30j precisely
- Append-only DELETE trigger conflict pseudonymisation -> UPDATE only

### Task 5.2.4 -- ACAPS Souscription 4.7.2.1 + Renouvellement 4.7.2.3

**Densite** : 140-180 KB | **P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE** | **7h** | Dependances : 5.2.3

**Scope precis** : `AcapsPoliceComplianceValidatorService` validations strict cas 4.7.2.1 souscription + 4.7.2.3 renouvellement. Compliance non negociable Programme Emergence ACAPS Skalean (apres Sprint 16 4.7.2.2 sinistre, Sprint 17 complete les 3 cas).

**Livrables** : Constants `acaps-police-2024.constants.ts` (MIN/MAX prime + duree + prime evolution % + continuity gap days), service `validateSouscription(policeData, version='2024')` (champs obligatoires + customer Sprint 8 lookup + insurer Sprint 14 lookup + dates coherentes + duree standard + branches + coverages + prime range), service `validateRenewal(previousPoliceId, newPoliceData)` (previous police status valid + continuity check date_fin previous - 1j == date_debut new + prime evolution check <= 20% else warning + notification + branches preserved or notify), errorCodes ACAPS_POL_001-016+ extensible, version-aware, telemetry, 14+ tests scenarios reels.

**Pieges minimum 8** :
- Continuity check gap 0 leap year edge cases -> date-fns library strict
- Prime evolution > 20% warning vs error ambiguity -> warning permissive + notification, > 50% error blocking
- Branches preserved vs removed nuance -> superset OK, removed requires notification
- Souscription customer KYC incomplete Sprint 8 -> verify kyc_status='verified' strict
- Insurer Service Registry lookup Sprint 14 down -> timeout + circuit breaker
- Date_debut today or future strict -> exception backdated emergencies admin audit
- Adhesions optional vs required selon type -> type famille requires
- Coverages plafond cumulative sanity check -> sum(plafonds) >= prime * 10 typical

- Direct UPDATE status bypass -> update delegate transition strict
- Avro schema validation runtime cost -> pre-compile schemas startup
- Idempotency TTL polices 30j -> Sprint 15 configurable
- Customer Sprint 8 lookup multi-tenant -> AsyncLocalStorage propagated
- findByPoliceNumber cross-tenant leak -> query strict tenant_id + insurer_id

### Task 5.2.6 -- PoliceLinkInterface Sprint 16 Dependency

**Densite** : 140-180 KB | **P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION** | **7h** | Dependances : 5.2.5

**Scope precis** : **Pont critique** entre Sinistre Sprint 16 et Police Sprint 17. Implementation `PoliceLinkInterfaceImpl implements PoliceLinkInterface` defined Sprint 16 5.1.11. Sans implementation, Sprint 16 fonctionne polices mockees uniquement -- pas viable Demo Day.

**Livrables** : Service `PoliceLinkInterfaceImpl` implements Sprint 16 interface, methode `linkPolice(sinistreId, policeId)` (validate police exists + tenant scope + status not cancelled + INSERT sinistre.police_id reference + emit event), methode `validatePoliceCoverage(sinistreId, policeId): CoverageValidationResult` orchestrate 5 checks sequentiels (Check 1 police status in active/renewal else POLICE_NOT_ACTIVE, Check 2 declaration_date in souscription_date..expiration_date else DATE_OUT_OF_RANGE, Check 3 sinistre.sinistre_branch in police.branches else BRANCH_NOT_COVERED, Check 4 montant_estime <= plafond_total - previous_claims_this_year else MONTANT_EXCEEDS_PLAFOND, Check 5 franchise_applicable = max franchises), helper `getPreviousClaimsAmount(policeId, sinceDate)` query Sprint 16 aggregation, cache Redis policy details TTL 5min, integration Sprint 16 SinistreService.declareClaim pre-saga, 14+ tests integration end-to-end Sprint 16 + 17.

**Pieges minimum 8** :
- Previous claims aggregation 10000 sinistres slow -> pre-aggregated ClickHouse Sprint 13 ou cache
- Cache Redis stale -> invalidation Kafka police_updated
- Cross-tenant police lookup different tenants -> Sprint 14 SEL si cross
- Numeric types Postgres NUMERIC vs JS precision -> Big.js library
- Applicable coverages logic business -> helper appliesTo + audit decisions
- Plafond annuel reset date_souscription year 2 -> previous_claims since souscription_date strict
- Franchise max vs sum ambiguous -> max worst case customer one franchise
- Coverage validation race status change -> check return immediate, retry client

- Mobile vs web differential response -> ?compact=true query param

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers Markdown ATX uniquement, code blocks language tag explicite (bash, sql, typescript, xml, yaml, json, avro, mermaid), tableaux Markdown pipes alignes, listes a puces `- `, listes numerotees `1. 2. 3.`, liens `[text](path)`, sequence diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense et professionnel : prose privilegiee bullets sections explicatives (3, 4, 8), paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, voix active, niveau technique senior NestJS/TypeScript/PostgreSQL/Kafka/Redis/Avro/ACAPS/state machines/timer-driven workflows, terminologie metier consistante (Police = policy d'assurance, souscription = initial creation, renouvellement = annual renewal, lifecycle = workflow lifecycle, transitions = state changes, ACAPS = Autorite Controle Assurances Prevoyance Sociale MA, decision-018 = state machine 5 etats Police), references industry standards explicit (Stripe subscriptions, Salesforce renewals).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 4 decisions strategiques (001-018), **decision-018 obligatoire toutes tasks 5.2.X**, **decisions-015 SEL + 016 SRF + 017 Sinistre obligatoires** tasks integration (5.2.6 PoliceLinkInterface + 5.2.8 sagas + 5.2.9 SEL), minimum 1 reference B-17 + 1 task voisine + 1 V-17 + 1 sprint precedent (1-16) pertinent.

### Tailles cibles synthese

| Task | Min KB | Max KB | Densite |
|------|--------|--------|---------|
| 5.2.1 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (architecture + decision + design doc) |
| 5.2.2 | 130 | 170 | Forte (6 entites + RLS + indexes) |
| 5.2.3 | 150 | 200 | Tres forte (CRITIQUE state machine 5 etats + 3 cron jobs) |
| 5.2.4 | 140 | 180 | Forte (ACAPS souscription + renouvellement) |
| 5.2.5 | 130 | 170 | Forte (PoliceService CRUD + Avro) |
| 5.2.6 | 140 | 180 | Tres forte (CRITIQUE PoliceLinkInterface Sprint 16) |
| 5.2.7 | 110 | 150 | Moyenne (event emitter + 6 topics) |
| 5.2.8 | 150 | 200 | Tres forte (CRITIQUE sagas souscription + renouvellement) |
| 5.2.9 | 130 | 170 | Forte (CRITIQUE cross-tenant SEL proof point) |
| 5.2.10 | 130 | 170 | Forte (12 REST endpoints + permissions) |
| 5.2.11 | 100 | 140 | Moyenne (Public interfaces Sprint 18-20) |

## WORKFLOW D'EXECUTION RECOMMANDE

### Pour chaque task

```
1. Lire B-17 -- section "Tache 5.2.{Z}" (scope) et "Frontiere du sprint" (limites)
2. Lire V-17 -- criteres V{N} attribues a la task
3. Lire C-17 -- extrait de la task dans orchestrateur (dependances pratiques)
4. Lire 2-3 tasks deja generees voisines pour calibrer style et profondeur
5. Lire decisions/001-018 -- decision-018 OBLIGATOIRE toutes tasks 5.2.X, decisions 015+016+017 obligatoires tasks integration
6. Lire template 03-template-task.md -- valider 11 sections obligatoires
7. Rediger fichier task-5.2.{Z}-{slug}.md dans `prompts-taches/sprint-17-police-lifecycle/`
8. Verifier taille finale dans la fourchette cible
9. Verifier aucune emoji presente
10. Commit Conventional Commits : `feat(prompts-taches): add task-5.2.{Z} prompt for Sprint 17`
```

### Sequencement critique 5.2.1 avant tout

Task 5.2.1 (Architecture + decision-018 + design doc) **doit etre peer-reviewed Saad CTO + Abla CEO** avant generation tasks 5.2.2+.

### Integration Sprint 16 PoliceLinkInterface 5.2.6 CRITIQUE

Task 5.2.6 est le pont critique entre Sinistre Sprint 16 et Police Sprint 17. Generation prompt insiste sur 5 checks coverage validation + tests integration end-to-end Sprint 16 + 17. Si task 5.2.6 prompt ne couvre pas exhaustivement, regenerer prompt.

### Cross-tenant SEL 5.2.9 PROOF POINT critique Phase 3

Task 5.2.9 est le second proof point Phase 3 apres Sprint 16 saga integration. Generation prompt insiste sur endpoint mTLS-only + canonical schemas + idempotency + audit log Sprint 14 + tests integration end-to-end.

## VERIFICATION FINALE APRES GENERATION 12 TASKS

```bash
cd 00-pilotage/prompts-taches/sprint-17-police-lifecycle/

# Verifier presence et taille
ls -la task-5.2.*.md
wc -c task-5.2.*.md

# Verifier total (~1.61 - 2.18 MB)
TOTAL_BYTES=$(wc -c task-5.2.*.md | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}')
TOTAL_MB=$(echo "scale=2; $TOTAL_BYTES / 1048576" | bc)
echo "Total : $TOTAL_MB MB (cible 1.61 - 2.18 MB)"

# Aucune emoji
grep -P "[\x{1F300}-\x{1F9FF}]|[\x{2600}-\x{27BF}]" task-5.2.*.md && echo "EMOJI DETECTED -- FAIL" || echo "No emoji -- OK"

# Sections obligatoires
for task in task-5.2.*.md; do
  for section in "TACHE 5.2." "Sprint :" "## 1. But" "## 2. Contexte" "## 3. Objectif" "## 4. Etat" "## 5. References" "## 6. Implementation" "## 7. Tests" "## 8. Criteres" "## 9. Commandes"; do
    grep -q "$section" "$task" || echo "$task: MISSING $section"
  done
done

# References B-17
for task in task-5.2.*.md; do
  grep -q "B-17-sprint-17" "$task" || echo "$task : MISSING reference B-17"
done

# Decisions strategiques minimum 4 par task
for task in task-5.2.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "decision-00[1-9]|decision-01[0-8]" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 4 ]; then
    echo "$task : Only $COUNT decisions (min 4)"
  fi
done

# Decision-018 obligatoire toutes tasks
for task in task-5.2.*.md; do
  if ! grep -q "decision-018" "$task"; then
    echo "$task : MISSING decision-018 (OBLIGATOIRE)"
  fi
done

# Decisions-015+016+017 obligatoires tasks integration 5.2.6/5.2.8/5.2.9
for task in task-5.2.6*.md task-5.2.8*.md task-5.2.9*.md; do
  for dec in "decision-015" "decision-016" "decision-017"; do
    if ! grep -q "$dec" "$task"; then
      echo "$task : MISSING $dec (OBLIGATOIRE integration)"
    fi
  done
done

# Pieges techniques minimum 8 par task
for task in task-5.2.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "Piege [0-9]+ :|piege technique" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 8 ]; then
    echo "$task : Only $COUNT pieges (min 8)"
  fi
done

# Specific references obligatoires
if ! grep -qE "state machine|VALID_TRANSITIONS|cron job" task-5.2.3*.md; then
  echo "task-5.2.3 : MISSING references state machine + cron"
fi
if ! grep -qE "ACAPS|4\.7\.2\.1|4\.7\.2\.3|validateSouscription|validateRenewal" task-5.2.4*.md; then
  echo "task-5.2.4 : MISSING ACAPS references"
fi
if ! grep -qE "PoliceLinkInterface|validatePoliceCoverage|Sprint 16" task-5.2.6*.md; then
  echo "task-5.2.6 : MISSING PoliceLinkInterface integration"
fi
if ! grep -qE "souscription-police.saga|renouvellement-police.saga|SagaOrchestrator" task-5.2.8*.md; then
  echo "task-5.2.8 : MISSING sagas references"
fi
if ! grep -qE "cross-tenant|insurer.policy.lookup|SEL Sprint 14" task-5.2.9*.md; then
  echo "task-5.2.9 : MISSING cross-tenant SEL references"
fi
if ! grep -qE "Sprint 17 closure|Phase 4 foundation|sprint-17-closure-report" task-5.2.12*.md; then
  echo "task-5.2.12 : MISSING closure references"
fi
```

### Task 5.3.3 -- Authentication NextAuth.js v5

**140-180 KB | P0 | 7h** | Dependances : 5.3.2

**Scope** : Authentication JWT integration Sprint 5 backend via NextAuth.js v5 (auth.js).

**Livrables** : Installation `pnpm add next-auth@beta` + `@auth/core`, configuration `auth.config.ts` + `auth.ts` (custom credentials provider POST `/api/v1/auth/login` Sprint 5 + JWT strategy + session callback include tenant_id + roles + permissions Sprint 7), refresh token rotation automatic NextAuth.js v5 built-in + secure httpOnly cookies SameSite=Strict + CSRF protection, middleware `middleware.ts` matcher protected routes (`/[locale]/dashboard/*`, `/[locale]/polices/*`, `/[locale]/sinistres/*`, `/[locale]/profile/*` redirect `/[locale]/login` si pas authentifie), pages `/[locale]/login` (form email + password + remember me + locale-aware error messages), `/[locale]/forgot-password` + `/[locale]/reset-password` (integration Sprint 5 endpoints), hooks `useSession` + `useAuthGuard`, helpers `getServerSession` server components, interceptor axios 401 -> refresh token automatic -> retry + logout si refresh fail, session 7j absolu + 30min idle timeout, 14+ tests integration.

**Pieges minimum 8** :
- NextAuth.js v5 beta vs v4 stable -> v5 production-ready Sprint 18, surveiller breaking changes
- JWT vs database sessions -> JWT (stateless, scale), database Phase 7+ si needed
- Refresh token rotation race conditions -> NextAuth v5 handles automatic + queue
- CSRF token vs sameSite -> SameSite=Strict + CSRF double-submit cookie defense
- Middleware Edge runtime limitations -> Node.js APIs unavailable, use middleware light
- Session callback heavy -> minimize data session (id + tenant_id + roles + permissions only)
- Logout cleanup -> revoke refresh token Sprint 5 backend + clear cookies + redirect
- 401 retry loop infinite -> max 1 retry then logout strict

### Task 5.3.11 -- Accessibility WCAG 2.1 AA Audit

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE | 7h** | Dependances : 5.3.10

**Scope** : WCAG 2.1 AA audit complete + axe-core + Lighthouse CI + manual screen readers.

**Livrables** : Semantic HTML strict (header, nav, main, section, article, footer + headings hierarchy h1 unique per page + h2-h6 sequential + ul/ol/li lists + table headers + form labels), ARIA labels obligatoires interactive elements (aria-label/aria-labelledby/aria-describedby + role explicit si non-standard + aria-current pour nav active + aria-expanded pour collapse + aria-live regions toast notifications), keyboard navigation full audit (Tab + Shift+Tab + Enter + Escape + Space + Arrow keys menus + Home/End lists) + focus visible all interactives custom ring + skip links debut page "Aller au contenu principal", color contrast 4.5:1 minimum text + 3:1 large text (>=18px ou bold >=14px) via Lighthouse + manual chromatic, screen reader testing NVDA Windows + JAWS Windows + VoiceOver macOS + TalkBack Android (suites tests dedicated screen reader scripts), alt text images obligatoire descriptive + decorative empty alt="" + functional images describe action, form labels associated (label htmlFor matching id ou aria-labelledby) + error messages aria-describedby + required indicator aria-required, axe-core tests automated integration CI `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts` zero violations critical, Lighthouse CI configuration `.lighthouserc.json` threshold Accessibility >= 95 strict + Performance >= 90 + Best Practices >= 95, Playwright accessibility tests via `@axe-core/playwright`, audit manual rapport `docs/accessibility-audit-sprint-18.md` WCAG 2.1 AA checklist exhaustive (perceivable + operable + understandable + robust principles), 12+ tests.

**Pieges minimum 10** :
- Semantic HTML vs divs everywhere -> audit migrate generic divs to semantic elements
- ARIA misuse vs HTML semantic -> "ARIA inutile" rule, semantic HTML preferred + ARIA si necessaire
- Focus management modals -> trap focus inside + restore previous focus on close (focus-trap-react)
- Skip links visible only on focus -> sr-only + focus:not-sr-only pattern
- Color contrast complex backgrounds -> manual check + tools (Stark, axe DevTools)
- Screen reader testing time-consuming -> prioritize critical flows (login, dashboard, declare sinistre)
- Alt text generation tedious -> brainstorm content team + AI-assisted Phase 7+
- Lighthouse CI false positives -> manual review + suppress only validated
- Audit manual exhaustive -> WCAG checklist tool + iterative improvements
- a11y testing automated coverage -> axe-core catches 30-40%, manual essential

**Livrables** : Setup Playwright config `playwright.config.ts` multi-browser (Chromium + Firefox + WebKit) + multi-viewport (mobile 375x667 + tablet 768x1024 + desktop 1280x800), 30+ tests E2E organises :

- **Suite authentication** 4 tests : login success credentials Sprint 5, login failure invalid creds, logout cleanup session, refresh token automatic + retry, session expired redirect /login
- **Suite dashboard** 4 tests : data display polices+sinistres counts, drill-down cards navigation, responsive layouts breakpoints, empty states first customer
- **Suite polices** 6 tests : list pagination cursor, filters status/type/date, details page coverage visualization, renewal one-click full flow, cancellation modal + confirm, PDF download
- **Suite sinistres** 8 tests : declaration multi-step 5 etapes complete, lifecycle tracker visual 8 etats display, documents upload constat + photos min 3, list filters status, details page lifecycle history timeline, ACAPS validation pre-flight errors display, saga trigger toast confirmation, real-time SSE update lifecycle
- **Suite real-time SSE** 4 tests : events lifecycle dispatched per tenant, reconnect exponential backoff strategy, toast notifications per event type, cache invalidation React Query auto-refresh
- **Suite accessibility** 2 tests : axe-core zero violations critical all pages, keyboard navigation full audit Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Escape
- **Suite i18n** 2 tests : FR layouts default locale, AR RTL layouts visual regression chromatic strict

Lighthouse CI integration `.github/workflows/lighthouse.yml` :
- LCP < 2.5s strict (image optimization + SSR + code splitting)
- FID < 100ms strict (lazy loading + bundle size)
- CLS < 0.1 strict (skeleton loaders + reserved space images)
- Accessibility >= 95 strict
- Performance >= 90 strict
- Best Practices >= 95
- SEO >= 90

Responsive tests breakpoints sm/md/lg/xl/2xl (375 / 640 / 768 / 1024 / 1280 / 1536), documentation `tests/e2e/SCENARIOS-CUSTOMER-PORTAL.md` exhaustive, rapport closure Sprint 18 `00-pilotage/reports/sprint-18-closure-report.md` documenter (premier frontend operational, dashboard + polices + sinistres + real-time + accessibility + i18n verified, Core Web Vitals strict pass, integration Sprint 16+17 APIs end-to-end, coverage >= 85% lignes, tests E2E 30+ PASS, pre-requis Sprint 19 Mobile valides, pre-requis Sprint 20 IA valides, risques residuels documentes, recommandation GO Sprint 19 si V-18 >= 95%).

**Pieges minimum 10** :
- Tests E2E volume 30+ time CI > 30min -> parallelization workers max + shards
- Playwright fixtures complex auth + db state -> beforeAll setup database + JWT tokens generation
- SSE tests timing fragiles -> waitForResponse + assertions immediate via mock SSE backend tests
- Visual regression chromatic noise -> tolerance threshold acceptable + manual review
- Lighthouse CI thresholds strict failure CI -> incremental improvements + override critical violations
- Responsive tests cross-browser -> Chromium minimum + Firefox + WebKit nice-to-have
- Documentation drift code change -> auto-generation Playwright tests doc + manual SCENARIOS.md
- Closure report exhaustivite -> template structure sections per area + checklist GO
- Sprint 19 prerequisites Mobile -> backend APIs validated + design system reusable
- Demo Day perception qualite frontend -> live demo rehearsal + fallback scenarios

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers Markdown ATX uniquement, code blocks language tag (bash/typescript/tsx/json/css/html), tableaux pipes alignes, listes `- `, listes numerotees `1.`, liens `[text](path)`, diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense + professionnel : prose privilegiee bullets sections explicatives, paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, voix active, niveau technique senior Next.js 14/React 18/TypeScript/Tailwind/shadcn/NextAuth/next-intl/Playwright/axe-core, terminologie metier consistante (Customer Portal = portail client web, Lifecycle Tracker = composant visuel etats, SSE = Server-Sent Events, WCAG = Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, Core Web Vitals = LCP/FID/CLS), references industry standards explicit (Next.js docs, MDN, W3C WCAG).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 4 decisions (001-019), **decision-019 obligatoire toutes tasks 5.3.X**, **decisions-017+018 obligatoires** tasks consume Sprint 16/17 (5.3.6/5.3.7/5.3.8/5.3.9), minimum 1 reference B-18 + 1 task voisine + 1 V-18 + 1 sprint precedent (1-17).

## VERIFICATION FINALE APRES GENERATION 12 TASKS

```bash
cd 00-pilotage/prompts-taches/sprint-18-web-customer-portal/

# Presence et taille
ls -la task-5.3.*.md
wc -c task-5.3.*.md

# Total ~1.63-2.18 MB
TOTAL_MB=$(wc -c task-5.3.*.md | tail -1 | awk '{printf "%.2f", $1/1048576}')
echo "Total : $TOTAL_MB MB"

# Aucune emoji
grep -P "[\x{1F300}-\x{1F9FF}]|[\x{2600}-\x{27BF}]" task-5.3.*.md && echo "EMOJI FAIL" || echo "No emoji OK"

# Sections obligatoires
for task in task-5.3.*.md; do
  for section in "TACHE 5.3." "## 1. But" "## 2. Contexte" "## 3. Objectif" "## 4. Etat" "## 5. References" "## 6. Implementation" "## 7. Tests" "## 8. Criteres" "## 9. Commandes"; do
    grep -q "$section" "$task" || echo "$task: MISSING $section"
  done
done

# References B-18
for task in task-5.3.*.md; do
  grep -q "B-18-sprint-18" "$task" || echo "$task : MISSING B-18"
done

# Decision-019 obligatoire toutes
for task in task-5.3.*.md; do
  if ! grep -q "decision-019" "$task"; then echo "$task : MISSING decision-019"; fi
done

# Decisions 017+018 obligatoires tasks integration 5.3.6/5.3.7/5.3.8/5.3.9
for task in task-5.3.6*.md task-5.3.7*.md task-5.3.8*.md task-5.3.9*.md; do
  for dec in "decision-017" "decision-018"; do
    if ! grep -q "$dec" "$task"; then echo "$task : MISSING $dec"; fi
  done
done

# Pieges minimum 8
for task in task-5.3.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "Piege [0-9]+ :|piege technique" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 8 ]; then echo "$task : Only $COUNT pieges"; fi
done

# Specific references obligatoires
if ! grep -qE "Next.js 14|App Router|shadcn|Tailwind" task-5.3.2*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.2 : MISSING Next.js references"
fi
if ! grep -qE "NextAuth v5|JWT|Sprint 5" task-5.3.3*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.3 : MISSING NextAuth Sprint 5"
fi
if ! grep -qE "next-intl|FR|AR|RTL|logical properties" task-5.3.4*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.4 : MISSING i18n RTL"
fi
if ! grep -qE "PoliceService|renewal|one-click|Sprint 17" task-5.3.7*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.7 : MISSING polices integration"
fi
if ! grep -qE "Stepper|5 etapes|ACAPS|lifecycle tracker.*8" task-5.3.8*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.8 : MISSING sinistres multi-step"
fi
if ! grep -qE "SSE|Server-Sent Events|EventSource" task-5.3.9*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.9 : MISSING SSE references"
fi
if ! grep -qE "WCAG 2.1 AA|axe-core|Lighthouse|accessibility" task-5.3.11*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.11 : MISSING accessibility references"
fi
if ! grep -qE "Sprint 18 closure|Core Web Vitals|Sprint 19 Mobile" task-5.3.12*.md; then
  echo "task-5.3.12 : MISSING closure references"
fi
```

**Livrables** : `decisions/020-mobile-stack-react-native-expo.md` 8-12 pages (date 4 juin 2026 + contexte 75%+ MA mobile-first + decision RN 0.74 + Expo SDK 51 + biometric + push + offline + alternatives evaluees Flutter/Native iOS+Android/Capacitor + consequences + roadmap Phase 7+ AR/wearables/payments natives), design doc `docs/architecture/mobile-portal-design.md` 20+ pages (architecture + navigation Expo Router + auth biometric + camera + GPS + push + offline SQLite + app stores + integration Sprint 16+17+18 + performance targets), kickoff report, peer-review.

- Flutter alternative attractive performance -> justifier RN ecosystem JS shared web/mobile + Expo abstraction modules
- Native iOS+Android codebase double effort -> RN 70% code reuse + Expo native modules abstraction
- Capacitor web-wrap alternative -> rejected (perception "web app" Apple/Google reject potential)
- Decision-020 vs Sprint 18 web continuity -> design system shared via packages monorepo
- App stores costs Apple Developer $99/year + Google Play $25 -> budget allocated Phase 4
- Performance targets strict Demo Day -> startup < 2s + bundle < 50MB explicit
- Offline mode MA reseau critique justification -> rapport ANRT MA 4G inegale rural

**130-170 KB | P0 | 7h** | Dependances : 5.4.1
