**Livrables** : `pnpm create expo@latest mobile-portal --template tabs`, Expo SDK 51 + RN 0.74+ + TypeScript strict (exactOptionalPropertyTypes), NativeWind v4 Tailwind RN config, Expo Router v3 file-based navigation, EAS Build `eas.json` profiles (development apk + preview internal testing + production stores), Fastlane `fastlane/Fastfile` setup basique (build_ios + build_android + screenshots), monorepo pnpm workspaces, scripts (start + ios + android + build:ios + build:android + test:e2e + lint + typecheck), `.env.example` documenter EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL + IOS_BUNDLE_ID + ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME + EAS_PROJECT_ID, README.md installation + Expo SDK 51 requirements (Node 18+ + Xcode 15+ macOS + Android Studio Hedgehog+).

**Pieges 8** :
- Expo SDK 51 vs SDK 50 stability -> SDK 51 production-ready Sprint 19, Expo updates frequent
- RN 0.74 New Architecture Fabric/TurboModules -> opt-in incrementally, RN 0.74 default OK
- NativeWind v4 vs v2 Tailwind RN -> v4 latest (avec react-native-css-interop)
- Expo Router v3 vs React Navigation v7 -> Expo Router file-based recommanded Expo
- EAS Build cloud vs local -> EAS Build cloud (no local Xcode requirement)
- Fastlane setup complexity -> minimal Fastfile + EAS automate most
- Monorepo Metro bundler resolution -> watchFolders + nohoist edge cases
- TypeScript strict noUncheckedIndexedAccess too strict -> incrementally enabled

### Task 5.4.3 -- Design System Shared + 25+ Composants UI Mobile

**140-180 KB | P0 | 8h** | Dependances : 5.4.2

**Scope** : Design tokens shared Sprint 18 + composants UI mobile-specific.

**Livrables** : Package `repo/packages/design-tokens/` (colors brand + typography + spacing + shadows shared web/mobile via package.json exports), composants `src/components/ui/` 25+ mobile-specific (Button, Input, Card, Modal, Toast, Form, List, Tabs, Pagination, Badge, Avatar, Skeleton, Spinner, Calendar, Stepper, LifecycleTrackerMobile, DocumentUploadMobile, PoliceCardMobile, SinistreCardMobile, NotificationItemMobile, LanguageSwitcher, ThemeProvider, ErrorBoundary, EmptyState, SignaturePad), NativeWind config Tailwind RN strict, theme provider React Context, accessibility props mobile (accessibilityLabel + accessibilityRole + accessibilityHint), Storybook React Native optional documentation, 20+ tests Jest.

- NativeWind v4 className prop conflicts -> use cssInterop wrapper
- StyleSheet.create vs inline styles -> StyleSheet better performance
- Theme provider React Context re-renders -> memoization strict
- Custom composants LifecycleTrackerMobile horizontal vs Sprint 18 vertical -> mobile vertical scrollable
- SignaturePad react-native-signature-canvas integration -> WebView-based limitations
- Accessibility props verbose -> helper hooks createAccessibilityProps
- Storybook React Native setup complexity -> optional Sprint 19, mandatory Phase 7+

### Task 5.4.4 -- Authentication Biometric

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 7h** | Dependances : 5.4.3

**Scope** : Biometric auth FaceID/TouchID/Fingerprint + secure storage + Sprint 5 JWT.

**Livrables** : `pnpm add expo-local-authentication expo-secure-store`, service `src/services/auth.service.ts` (FaceID iOS via LAContext + TouchID iOS + Android BiometricPrompt fingerprint + Android face unlock), screen `(auth)/login.tsx` email + password + remember me + locale-aware error messages, screen `(auth)/biometric.tsx` apres login first time -> proposer enroll biometric optional, secure storage tokens via expo-secure-store (Keychain iOS + EncryptedSharedPreferences Android), refresh token automatic background via task queue + listener app foreground, fallback password si biometric unavailable ou device pas configure + UX clear messaging, max 5 biometric attempts then password required strict, session 30j absolu + 7j idle timeout, integration Sprint 5 JWT backend, 14+ tests integration.

**Pieges 8** :
- LAContext iOS vs BiometricPrompt Android API differences -> abstraction layer service
- Keychain iOS vs EncryptedSharedPreferences Android -> expo-secure-store unified
- Biometric scan false negatives -> max 5 attempts + fallback password strict
- Refresh token race condition app launch -> mutex lock + queue
- Session 30j absolute vs 7j idle timeout -> AbsoluteSession + IdleSessionTimer two-layer
- Device biometric not enrolled -> hasHardwareAsync + isEnrolledAsync check first
- Biometric prompt UX timing -> trigger on app foreground + sensitive actions
- Sprint 5 backend JWT format compatibility -> exact same payload claims as Sprint 18

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE MA | 6h** | Dependances : 5.4.4

**Livrables** : `pnpm add expo-localization i18n-js`, configuration i18n loader messages partages Sprint 18 (`@insurtech/i18n/messages/fr.json` + `ar.json` 800+ keys), auto-detect locale device via expo-localization + fallback FR, RTL support via React Native I18nManager.forceRTL(true) si locale AR (require app restart first time + modal explication customer), FlexDirection logical direction (start/end) via NativeWind utilities + StyleSheet logical, LanguageSwitcher component restart hint, tests RTL layouts mobile Detox, 12+ tests.

- I18nManager.forceRTL require app restart first time -> Updates.reloadAsync programmatic restart
- FlexDirection RTL row vs row-reverse -> useColorScheme + I18nManager.isRTL helper
- ScrollView horizontal RTL inversion -> handled automatic if FlexDirection logical
- Text component textAlign 'auto' vs explicit -> 'auto' respects layout direction
- Arabic font rendering iOS vs Android -> system fonts default OK
- Date/time formatting MA timezone -> Intl + expo-localization timezone Africa/Casablanca
- Number formatting MAD currency -> Intl.NumberFormat('fr-MA', {style: 'currency'})

- GPS denial -> fallback manual address input

- Min 3 photos enforce -> validation strict before next step

**Livrables** : `pnpm add expo-notifications`, configuration FCM Android (`google-services.json` placement + EAS Build config), Apple Push Notification certificate iOS + provisioning profile + EAS Submit config, permission handling first launch + explanation modal, token registration backend Sprint 9 chaque app launch + cleanup stale tokens > 90j, notifications categories (sinistre_status_change + police_renewal_alert + payout_received + expertise_assigned + new_message), deep linking notifications tap -> screens specifiques via Expo Router, notifications local scheduled rappel renewal J-7 + J-1 via expo-notifications scheduling, integration Sprint 9 NotificationRouter.sendPush(deviceToken, payload), 12+ tests integration.

**Pieges 8** :
- FCM token rotation periodic -> register chaque app launch
- APNs certificate Apple Push Notification Service annual renewal -> calendar reminder

### Task 5.4.10 -- Offline Mode SQLite + Sync

**170-220 KB | P0 CRITIQUE MA | 9h** | Dependances : 5.4.9

**Scope** : Offline mode SQLite local + sync queue + reconciliation conflicts.

**Livrables** : `pnpm add expo-sqlite expo-network expo-background-fetch`, schemas SQLite tables miroirs (`polices_offline` + `sinistres_offline` + `lifecycle_events_offline` + `documents_offline` + `notifications_offline` + `sync_queue` operations queued offline avec operation_type/entity_id/payload/timestamp + `last_sync_at` metadata per entity), service `SyncService.syncOnConnect()` (replay sync_queue mutations FIFO + fetch latest polices + sinistres + lifecycle Sprint 16+17 + reconciliation conflicts last-write-wins par defaut + manual review queue critical conflicts), offline indicator UI banner top quand NetInfo offline detection, bypass features online-only (declaration sinistre nouveau saga = online only + show error banner si offline + queue retry on connect), features offline-available (consult polices + sinistres existing + history + documents preview cached), background sync via expo-background-fetch task periodic 15min, migration schema versioning future, 15+ tests integration.

**Pieges 10** :
- SQLite schema migrations versioning -> migration scripts versioned + auto-apply on startup
- Sync queue replay order FIFO vs LIFO -> FIFO strict pour consistency
- Conflict resolution last-write-wins default -> manual review queue pour critical (montant_payout differ > 10%)
- Reconciliation incomplete app killed mid-sync -> resume from last_sync_at offset
- Background sync iOS limitations -> max 30s execution + occasional
- Background sync Android Doze mode -> WorkManager-like via expo-background-fetch
- SQLite WAL mode performance -> enable pragma journal_mode=WAL
- Cached documents large size -> max 50 photos cached + LRU eviction
- Offline indicator UX subtle vs intrusive -> banner top non-blocking
- Sync queue persistence app uninstall -> SQLite preserved expo-sqlite default

### Task 5.4.11 -- App Store Preparation iOS + Android

**130-170 KB | P0 | 6h** | Dependances : 5.4.10

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers Markdown ATX, code blocks language tag (bash/typescript/tsx/json/yaml/objc/java), tableaux, listes, diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense + professionnel : prose privilegiee, paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, niveau technique senior React Native/Expo/iOS/Android/Detox/Fastlane, terminologie mobile (FaceID/TouchID/Fingerprint, FCM/APNs, Deep Linking Universal Links/Intent Filters, Bundle ID/Package Name, IPA/APK, TestFlight/Internal Testing).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 4 decisions (001-020), **decision-020 obligatoire toutes tasks 5.4.X**, **decisions 017+018 obligatoires** tasks integration Sprint 16/17 (5.4.7/5.4.8), **decision-019 obligatoire** tasks reusing Sprint 18 (5.4.3/5.4.5).

cd 00-pilotage/prompts-taches/sprint-19-mobile-portal/

ls -la task-5.4.*.md
wc -c task-5.4.*.md

grep -P "[\x{1F300}-\x{1F9FF}]|[\x{2600}-\x{27BF}]" task-5.4.*.md && echo "EMOJI FAIL" || echo "No emoji OK"

# Decision-020 obligatoire toutes
for task in task-5.4.*.md; do
  if ! grep -q "decision-020" "$task"; then echo "$task : MISSING decision-020"; fi
done

done

  if ! grep -q "decision-019" "$task"; then echo "$task : MISSING decision-019"; fi
done

# Pieges minimum 8
for task in task-5.4.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "Piege [0-9]+ :|piege technique" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 8 ]; then echo "$task : Only $COUNT pieges"; fi
done

# Specific references

grep -qE "FaceID|TouchID|expo-local-authentication" task-5.4.4*.md || echo "task-5.4.4 MISSING biometric"

## TASKS A GENERER -- SCOPES

### Task 5.5.1 -- Architecture + Decision-021 + Design Doc IA

**110-150 KB | P0 | 5h** | Sprint 19 closure

**Scope** : Gateway architectural Sprint 20 + premier IA Skalean. Decision-021 ratifiee + clarification decision-007 cas usage specific autorise + design doc 20+ pages + peer-review obligatoire.

**Livrables** : `decisions/021-ai-vision-estimation-photos.md` 8-12 pages (date 5 juin 2026 + contexte premier IA Skalean + clarification decision-007 cas usage SPECIFIC estimation photos AUTORISE vs general IA Sprint 30+ + AI Vision stack + Atlas Cloud souverain + ethics + recommendation-only design + alternatives Tractable.ai SaaS / custom training / open-source models / cloud APIs Google Vision/AWS Rekognition + consequences + roadmap Phase 7+ federated learning + edge inference), design doc `docs/architecture/ai-estimation-pipeline-design.md` 20+ pages (executive summary + pipeline detaille photos -> bbox -> severity -> montant + models architecture YOLOv8m + ResNet50 + LightGBM + Triton serving + deployment Atlas Cloud + ethics framework recommendation-only + audit Sprint 7 + Grad-CAM + opt-out + integration Sprint 16 PoliceEstimationProvider flow + monitoring + drift + retraining + Phase 7+ roadmap), kickoff report Phase 4 Sprint 5 + ethics review committee, peer-review Saad+Abla.

**Pieges 8** :
- Decision-021 vs decision-007 conflict apparent -> clarification SPECIFIC cas usage autorise documente explicit
- Alternatives SaaS Tractable.ai attractive ($X/inference) -> rejeted (data residency MA + souverainete + cost long-term)
- Open-source models maturity -> YOLOv8 + ResNet matures, custom training MA-specific dataset critical
- Recommendation-only design constraints UX -> Sprint 22+ human reviewer mandatory toujours
- Atlas Cloud GPUs availability MA -> reserve capacity contract + budget allocated
- Datasets bias inherent -> ethics committee review + bias audit quarterly mandatory
- Performance vs accuracy tradeoff -> P95 < 5s + accuracy > targets validation set strict
- Roadmap Phase 7+ general IA -> Future Evolution section explicit (chatbot + tarification + fraude + scoring)

### Task 5.5.2 -- Datasets Training Photos Vehicules MA

**140-180 KB | P0 | 8h** | Dependances : 5.5.1

**Scope** : Datasets curated 10000+ photos vehicules MA + annotations exhaustives + augmentation + DVC versioning.

**Livrables** : Datasets structure `repo/packages/ai-estimation/datasets/` (training 8000 + validation 1000 + test 1000), sources collection (fictional brands synthetic AssurMaghreb 3000 + Atlas 2000 + MediAssur 2000 + partenaires garage NDAs 2000 + Flickr Creative Commons MA-relevant licensed 1000), annotations CVAT.ai tool exhaustives (bbox dommages multi-classes hood/door/bumper/window/wheel/headlight/taillight/side_panel + severity grades 1-5 cosmetique/superficiel/modere/grave/total_loss + montants reference reels MAD + vehicule metadata make/model/year + conditions angle/distance/lighting + demographics anonymized), augmentation pipeline (rotation 0-360 + brightness/contrast + cropping random + JPEG quality variations + horizontal flip) dataset effectively expanded 3x, DVC tracking + S3 Atlas Cloud storage, 8+ tests data quality.

**Pieges 8** :
- Photos generales US/EU dataset bias vs vehicules MA -> curation MA-specific strict (Dacia, Renault, Peugeot, Hyundai dominants)
- Annotations CVAT.ai tool licensing fee -> tool open-source ou enterprise license
- Severity grades 1-5 subjectives -> protocoles annotation strict + inter-annotator agreement > 0.80
- Montants reference reels disponibilite -> partenaires garage reels NDAs critique
- Demographics anonymization GDPR strict -> ages/regions aggregated, jamais individuals identifiables
- Augmentation excessive risk overfitting augmentation patterns -> validation set strict separate
- DVC tracking large files Git LFS alternative -> DVC choice for ML-specific workflow
- Dataset bias audit fairness -> quarterly ethics committee review

### Task 5.5.3 -- Models Training YOLOv8 + ResNet + LightGBM

**200-260 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 12h** | Dependances : 5.5.2

**Scope** : Training 3 modeles pipeline + accuracy targets stricts + reproducibility MLflow.

**Livrables** : Notebooks Jupyter training reproducibles (`01-train-detection.ipynb` Ultralytics YOLOv8m + 200 epochs + AdamW + cosine lr scheduler + mAP > 0.75 target, `02-train-classification.ipynb` ResNet50 backbone PyTorch + 5 classes severity + cross-entropy + accuracy > 0.85 target, `03-train-regression.ipynb` LightGBM features bbox_count + severity_grades + vehicule metadata one-hot + montants_reference target + MAE < 20% Pearson > 0.80), hyperparameter tuning Optuna 50 trials per model (learning rate + batch size + augmentation strength), MLflow tracking experiments + DVC dataset versioning + reproducibility random seeds, evaluation comprehensive (mAP + accuracy + MAE/RMSE + cross-validation 5-fold + confusion matrices + ROC curves + calibration plots + Platt scaling), models export ONNX format pour Triton serving, 16+ tests evaluation.

**Pieges 10** :
- Overfitting training set -> validation set 20% strict + early stopping patience 20 epochs
- Hyperparameter tuning Optuna 50 trials cout compute -> distribute multi-GPU si available
- LightGBM features engineering manual vs auto -> manual MA-specific features superieur
- Platt scaling calibration validation set distinct -> separate calibration set 10%
- Inter-model dependencies (regression uses detection+classification outputs) -> careful pipeline order
- ONNX export ResNet special ops -> simpler architecture si export fail
- Random seeds reproducibility CUDA non-deterministic -> torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic=True
- MLflow tracking remote vs local -> remote tracking server Atlas Cloud
- DVC vs Git LFS alternative -> DVC choice ML workflow integration
- Class imbalance critical claims rare -> stratified sampling + weighted loss

### Task 5.5.4 -- Deployment Atlas Cloud Souverain + Triton

**170-220 KB | P0 CRITIQUE SOUVERAINETE | 9h** | Dependances : 5.5.3

**Scope** : Deployment Atlas Cloud souverain MA + Triton Inference Server + Kubernetes + monitoring.

**Livrables** : Terraform `repo/infrastructure/ai-estimation/terraform/` (Atlas Cloud region MA-1 Benguerir + VPC + Kubernetes cluster ai-estimation-cluster + 3 nodes worker + GPUs NVIDIA T4 dedicated + node pools labels + load balancer + S3 storage models + IAM roles + monitoring stack), Kubernetes manifests `k8s/triton-server.yaml` (Deployment Triton Inference Server NVIDIA 24.x + replicas 1 + nodeAffinity GPU nodes + resources requests/limits GPU memory + persistent volumes models PVC + readiness/liveness probes + service ClusterIP) + `k8s/autoscaling.yaml` HPA min 1 max 5 replicas based GPU/CPU utilization > 70% + `k8s/monitoring.yaml` Prometheus ServiceMonitor + Grafana dashboards + AlertManager rules, CI/CD pipeline GitOps ArgoCD apply manifests, certificates mTLS strict + secrets Sprint 5 Vault, model warm-up startup script, 12+ tests deployment validation (smoke + load + chaos).

**Pieges 10** :
- Atlas Cloud Terraform provider maturity -> provider custom developpe Sprint 14 reuse
- GPU NVIDIA T4 availability MA limited -> reserve capacity contract OR fallback CPU temporary
- Triton Inference Server version compatibility ONNX models -> Triton 24.x recent versions
- Persistent volumes Atlas Cloud CSI driver -> CSI compatible verify
- Kubernetes 1.28+ GPU support -> device plugin nvidia-device-plugin
- HPA GPU metrics non-standard -> custom-metrics-api OR Prometheus adapter
- ArgoCD multi-cluster -> single cluster Atlas Cloud Sprint 20
- mTLS certificates Sprint 14 CA -> reuse intermediate CA
- Model warm-up cold start latency -> startup probe wait warm-up complete
- Cost optimization GPUs idle nights -> autoscale min 1 (always warm) ou min 0 (cold but cheaper)

### Task 5.5.5 -- API IA Estimation Endpoint

**140-180 KB | P0 | 7h** | Dependances : 5.5.4

**Scope** : Endpoint REST consumable Sprint 16 + retours structured + timeout + circuit breaker.

**Livrables** : Controller `ai-estimation.controller.ts` endpoint `POST /api/v1/ai/estimation/sinistre`, validation Zod request `{sinistre_id, photo_urls[], vehicule_metadata?: {make, model, year}}`, service `AIEstimationService.estimate(request)` orchestrate (download photos S3 Sprint 10 via signed URLs parallel + preprocess resize 1280x720 + normalize mean/std + invoke Triton ensemble inference YOLOv8 + ResNet + LightGBM + postprocess aggregate + business rules confidence), response structured `{estimation_montant_mad, confidence_score, bbox_detections[], severity_grade, severity_breakdown, human_review_required, processing_time_ms, model_versions: {detection, classification, regression}}`, timeout 30s strict + circuit breaker Sprint 14 pattern + idempotency Sprint 15 via sinistre_id 24h cache + rate limit 100 req/min/tenant + permissions Sprint 7.5a `ai.estimation.request` granular, OpenAPI 3.1 schemas zod-to-openapi, 14+ tests E2E.

**Pieges 8** :
- Photos download S3 timeout slow customer mobile -> parallel download + timeout 10s each
- Photos invalid format/corrupted -> validation magic bytes + skip + report error
- Preprocess resize aspect ratio preservation -> pad black bars short side
- Triton inference batching efficiency -> batch all photos same sinistre single call
- Idempotency key sinistre_id sufficient unique -> 24h TTL retries
- Rate limit 100/min per tenant -> burst tolerance 150/min
- Permissions cross-tenant access -> tenant_id strict in request scope
- OpenAPI schema generation zod-to-openapi maturity -> alternative manual specs

- Async invocation listener vs await -> fire-and-forget pattern + retry queue si failure
- Sinistre metadata JSONB structure schema -> Sprint 16 schema defined explicit
- Event sinistre.ai_estimation_completed payload -> Avro schema Sprint 15 SchemaRegistry
- Error handling fallback graceful -> human_review_required=true + reason in metadata, never throw

### Task 5.5.7 -- Confidence Scoring + Human Review Fallback

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 6h** | Dependances : 5.5.6

**Scope** : Algorithm aggregate per-model confidence + thresholds configurables + Platt scaling.

**Livrables** : Service `ConfidenceScoringService.calculate(detection, classification, regression): ConfidenceScore` aggregate per-model confidence (detection IOU mean across bboxes + classification softmax max prob + regression prediction interval narrowness via quantile predictions 5th/95th percentile narrow = confident) -> overall confidence 0-1 weighted average configurable weights, helper `determineHumanReviewRequired(confidence, claim_amount_estimated)` thresholds configurable (default 0.80 + strict 0.90 si claim > 100000 MAD + permissive 0.70 si claim < 5000 MAD + critical > 500000 MAD always human review regardless confidence), Platt scaling calibration models trained validation set + Expected Calibration Error metrics monitored + recalibration periodic, audit log decisions IA Sprint 7 (sinistre_id + raw_confidence + calibrated_confidence + threshold_applied + decision + reasons explanatory), 12+ tests scenarios.

**Pieges 8** :
- Per-model confidence aggregation weights subjective -> Optuna optimize weights validation set
- Detection IOU mean vs max -> mean smoother + max sensitive outliers, mean preferred
- Classification softmax max prob over-confident DNN -> Platt scaling calibrate
- Regression prediction interval bootstrap -> quantile regression LightGBM built-in
- Thresholds tuning validation set fragile -> stratified sampling claim amounts + cross-validation
- Critical claims always human review override confidence -> business rule strict explicit
- ECE monitoring weekly -> recalibration trigger si ECE > 0.05 sustained
- Audit log Sprint 7 persistence performance -> async batched insert + queue

### Task 5.5.8 -- Integration Sprint 16 Sinistre Lifecycle

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 6h** | Dependances : 5.5.7

**Scope** : Listener Sprint 16 received event -> invoke IA -> store metadata + emit completed event.

**Livrables** : Listener `SinistreReceivedListener` Sprint 16 Kafka topic `insurtech.events.sinistre.received` async invoke `AIEstimationService.estimateClaimAmount(sinistre_id, photo_urls)` (max 30s timeout via Sprint 14 circuit breaker pattern), INSERT result sinistre.metadata.ai_estimation JSONB structure via Sprint 16 SinistreService.updateMetadata, emit event `sinistre.ai_estimation_completed` payload `{sinistre_id, ai_estimation_result, processing_time_ms, model_versions}` Kafka topic `insurtech.events.sinistre.ai_estimation_completed` consume Sprint 9 NotificationRouter (notification reviewer Sprint 22+ + customer optional opt-in) + Sprint 22+ workflow expertise pre-fill `montant_estime_ia` field + flag `human_review_required` boolean, error handling (timeout -> graceful degradation human_review_required=true + audit log error + retry queue exponential backoff), tests integration end-to-end Sprint 16 sinistre.received -> Sprint 20 IA -> metadata + event -> Sprint 22+ future workflow expertise, 12+ tests.

**Pieges 8** :
- Listener Kafka consumer group offset -> per-instance consumer group + max.poll.records configured
- Async invoke vs await blocking lifecycle -> async fire-and-forget + retry queue persistent
- Metadata update concurrency -> UPDATE sinistre.metadata = jsonb_set strict + optimistic locking version
- Event emission ordering -> Kafka topic partition by sinistre_id
- Error handling timeout vs model error vs photos invalid -> per-error-type fallback strategy
- Tests integration require Sprint 16 + Sprint 20 cluster -> docker-compose full stack
- Sprint 22+ future workflow not yet implemented -> mock consumer for tests
- Audit log error scenarios persist -> Sprint 7 critical error path

### Task 5.5.9 -- Performance Optimization

**130-170 KB | P0 | 6h** | Dependances : 5.5.8

**Scope** : Latency optimization + caching + batching + benchmarks production-grade.

**Livrables** : Service `AIEstimationService` parallelization photos preprocessing via Promise.all + inference batching Triton multi-photos same sinistre batch single inference call, cache Redis predictions same photos signature MD5 hash TTL 7 days (key `ai-estimation:photo:{md5hash}`) avoid re-inference identical photos (customer re-uploads OR retry scenarios), GPU memory optimization batch sizes optimal validation experimentation (batch 1, 4, 8, 16, 32 -> select best throughput/memory), warm-up requests startup Triton, telemetry Prometheus metrics (`ai_estimation_latency_seconds{percentile=0.50,0.95,0.99,phase=preprocess|inference|postprocess}` + `ai_estimation_throughput_req_per_min` + `ai_estimation_cache_hit_ratio` + `ai_estimation_gpu_memory_used_bytes`), Grafana dashboards business + technical, benchmarks P95 < 5s 3 photos input + < 10s 10 photos input + throughput 100 req/min sustained, 10+ tests performance load/stress.

**Pieges 8** :
- Photos preprocessing parallel Promise.all photos large -> memory pressure CPU
- Inference batching variable photos count per sinistre -> dynamic batching Triton
- Cache Redis MD5 hash collision low -> SHA256 alternatively, MD5 sufficient practice
- GPU memory batch sizes large OOM -> validate batch size production node
- Warm-up requests startup Triton -> sentinel requests health check
- Throughput 100/min sustained vs burst -> burst tolerance 150/min vs sustained 100/min
- Cache invalidation policy -> TTL 7d sufficient, no manual invalidation
- Benchmarks production-realistic load patterns -> simulated traffic actual customer behavior

### Task 5.5.10 -- Privacy + Ethics Safeguards

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE | 7h** | Dependances : 5.5.9

**Scope** : Audit log Sprint 7 persistent + Grad-CAM heatmaps + opt-out customer + recommendation-only strict + ethics committee.

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers ATX, code blocks tag (bash/typescript/python/yaml/json/sql), tableaux, listes, diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense + professionnel : prose privilegiee, paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, niveau technique senior PyTorch + computer vision + MLOps + Kubernetes + Atlas Cloud + ethics + decision-021, terminologie metier consistante (Vision Computer = computer vision photos analysis, mAP = mean Average Precision detection metric, ECE = Expected Calibration Error, Triton Inference Server = NVIDIA serving, ONNX = Open Neural Network Exchange format, MLflow = ML lifecycle tracking, DVC = Data Version Control), references industry standards (Tractable.ai / Solera / Lemonade industry IA estimation, Grad-CAM paper Selvaraju 2017, Platt scaling paper 1999, KS test Kolmogorov-Smirnov drift detection).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 5 decisions (001-021), **decision-021 obligatoire toutes tasks 5.5.X**, **decision-007 clarification + decision-010 Atlas Cloud + decisions 016+017+018 integration obligatoires** tasks integration Sprint 16/17/18.

## TASKS A GENERER -- SCOPES

### Task 6.1.1 -- Architecture + Decision-022 + Design Doc Dispatch

**110-150 KB | P0 | 5h** | Sprint 20 closure

**Scope** : Gateway architectural Sprint 21 Phase 5 premier marketplace. Decision-022 ratifiee + design doc 20+ pages + peer-review.

**Livrables** : `decisions/022-dispatch-matching-real-time-tracking.md` 8-12 pages (date 6 juin 2026 + contexte premier marketplace Phase 5 + decision dispatch Uber-style + matching weighted + PostGIS+Redis tracking + multi-channel notifications + acceptance optimistic + fallback 3 rounds + alternatives evaluees WebSocket vs SSE+push / Mapbox vs OpenStreetMap / Twilio vs Sprint 9 / Uber API integration vs custom + consequences + roadmap Phase 7+ AI optimization matching + multi-garage parallel dispatch + dispute resolution), design doc `docs/architecture/dispatch-platform-design.md` 20+ pages (architecture marketplace 3-sided + matching algorithm detaille + tracking real-time + notifications fanout + acceptance workflow + Uber/Doordash patterns reference + integration Sprint 16 + Sprint 22+ roadmap), kickoff Phase 5 Sprint 1, peer-review.

**Pieges 8** :
- Marketplace 3-sided vs 2-sided platform complexity -> design careful (insurer + customer + garage)
- Pattern Uber assurance specifique vs ride-sharing -> assurance time-sensitive less than rides + acceptance window wider 60s
- PostGIS vs MongoDB Geo alternative -> PostGIS chosen (decision-003 TypeORM + Postgres)
- Redis Geo Redis 6.2+ requirement -> production verify version
- Multi-channel notifications cost -> push priority + SMS fallback + email always
- Acceptance race conditions multi-instance API -> SELECT FOR UPDATE distributed lock
- Fallback infinite loop risk -> max 3 strict + escalation reviewer Sprint 22 explicit
- Garage onboarding fraud -> KYC + admin approval Sprint 22+

### Task 6.1.2 -- Domain Entities Garage + Dispatch + PostGIS

**130-170 KB | P0 | 6h** | Dependances : 6.1.1

**Scope** : 5 entites domain dispatch + PostGIS spatial + RLS multi-tenant.

**Livrables** : Package `@insurtech/dispatch` v1.0.0, migration `CreateDispatchEntities` :
- `garages` (id + tenant_id + name + email + phone + location GEOMETRY(POINT, 4326) + radius_service_km + capacity_concurrent + rating_average + rating_count + verified + active + status enum pending_verification/verified/active/suspended + signup_date + verified_date + activated_date + tenant_id RLS + version optimistic)
- `dispatch_offers` (id + sinistre_id FK + garage_id FK + tenant_id + status enum proposed/accepted/rejected/timeout/superseded/expired + score_matching NUMERIC + score_proximity NUMERIC + score_capacity + score_rating + score_preference + proposed_at + responded_at nullable + rejected_reason nullable + round_number INT 1-3 + correlation_id, append-only audit no UPDATE/DELETE except status enum permitted)
- `assignments` (id + sinistre_id + garage_id + tenant_id + status enum assigned/in_progress/completed/cancelled + assigned_at + estimated_arrival_at + actual_arrival_at nullable + completion_at nullable + completion_notes + version)
- `garage_ratings` (id + sinistre_id + garage_id + customer_id + tenant_id + rating_1_5 INT CHECK + comment TEXT + timestamp, append-only)
- `garage_preferences` (customer_id + garage_id + tenant_id + is_favorite BOOLEAN + last_used_at + PRIMARY KEY composite)

RLS active 5 tables, indexes (PostGIS GIST garages.location + tenant_id+status+capacity partial + customer_id+garage_id + sinistre_id offers + assignments.sinistre_id), enums TypeScript (GarageStatus, OfferStatus, AssignmentStatus), seeds initial 50 garages MA 6 villes (Casablanca 15 + Rabat 10 + Marrakech 10 + Fes 5 + Tanger 5 + Agadir 5 + ratings synthetic), 16+ tests unitaires.

**Pieges 8** :
- PostGIS extension production prod migration -> staging test + idempotent + 0 downtime
- GEOMETRY vs GEOGRAPHY type -> GEOMETRY POINT 4326 SRID standard
- GIST index spatial vs SP-GiST -> GIST recommended PostGIS standard
- Append-only audit dispatch_offers update status enum allowed strict -> trigger Postgres
- RLS tenant_id propagation -> AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6
- Seeds 50 garages MA-specific -> realistic locations + photos publiques
- Migration scripts idempotent -> safe re-run
- Version optimistic locking -> @VersionColumn TypeORM

### Task 6.1.3 -- Matching Algorithm Weighted

**150-200 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 8h** | Dependances : 6.1.2

**Scope** : Algorithm matching scoring weighted + customer preferences priority.

**Livrables** : Service `MatchingService.findEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, options?): GarageMatch[]` orchestrate :
1. Lookup sinistre Sprint 16 SinistreService.findById + customer_id + location_lat/lng
2. Query PostGIS ST_DWithin garages.location radius 50km default + status='active' + active=true
3. Filter capacity (LEFT JOIN current sinistres count < capacity_concurrent)
4. Apply customer preferences (LEFT JOIN garage_preferences customer_id + IS NULL is_favorite default 0.5)
5. Scoring weighted (proximity 0.40 + capacity 0.20 + rating 0.30 + preference 0.10)
6. Sort score DESC LIMIT 5 top batch initial
7. Return GarageMatch[] {garage_id, score, score_breakdown {proximity, capacity, rating, preference}, distance_km, estimated_arrival_minutes}

Helpers normalized 0-1 :
- `calculateProximityScore(distance_km, max_radius_km)` = max(0, 1 - distance/max_radius)
- `calculateCapacityScore(garage)` = max(0, 1 - garage.current_sinistres/garage.capacity)
- `calculateRatingScore(garage)` = garage.rating_average / 5.0
- `calculatePreferenceScore(customer_id, garage_id)` = 1.0 si favorite sinon 0.5

Configurable poids per tenant via env vars + tenant_config table override Phase 7+, telemetry, 16+ tests scenarios (proximite, capacite full, rating low, preference favorite, no eligible, only 3 eligible < top 5).

**Pieges 10** :
- Performance query 10000 garages -> Redis Geo pre-filter + PostGIS detailed top 100
- Distance calculation Haversine vs Vincenty -> Haversine Earth-flat approximation OK 50km radius
- Capacity current_sinistres count query expensive -> denormalize garage.current_sinistres_count UPDATE on Sprint 16 transitions
- Rating average NULL si garage new -> default 4.0 priority new garages bootstrap
- Customer preferences IS NULL -> default 0.5 medium
- Top 5 batch size config -> env var DISPATCH_TOP_N_GARAGES
- Score normalization 0-1 -> tests strict
- Scoring weights validation -> sum = 1.0 strict
- Tenant config override -> Phase 7+ defere
- ScoreBreakdown debug -> expose details API admin

### Task 6.1.4 -- Real-Time Geolocation Tracking PostGIS + Redis Geo

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 7h** | Dependances : 6.1.3

**Scope** : Tracking GPS garages + Redis Geo queries + ETA calculation.

**Livrables** : Service `GarageTrackingService.updateLocation(garage_id, lat, lng, accuracy_meters?)` execute :
- Redis Geo GEOADD `garage:locations:{tenant_id}` longitude latitude member=garage_id
- Postgres UPDATE garage.location PostGIS POINT (denormalize cache + index update)
- Audit log Sprint 7 location_updates table append-only

Service `getNearestGarages(lat, lng, radius_km, limit?: number): GarageWithDistance[]` Redis GEORADIUSBYMEMBER WITHCOORD WITHDIST + filter active+verified + return nearest within radius limit 10000 garages query < 50ms target benchmark.

Service `EtaCalculatorService.calculateEta(garage_location, sinistre_location): number` distance Haversine + speed_avg 40 km/h urbain MA configurable + traffic_multiplier 1.5x peak hours configurable + buffer 5min minimum + return minutes estimated.

Endpoint `PUT /api/v1/garages/{id}/location` (consume Sprint 19 mobile garage app GPS updates) request body `{lat, lng, accuracy_meters?, source: 'gps'|'manual'}` permissions Sprint 7.5a `garage.update.location` (garage owner only), adaptive frequency Sprint 19 (10s active sinistre + 60s idle + 5min background) + battery-aware throttle.

Cleanup stale locations > 24h via cron Sprint 17 pattern (daily 04h00 MA), 14+ tests integration.

**Pieges 8** :
- Redis Geo precision lat/lng floating point -> reasonable precision GEOADD double
- PostGIS POINT vs WKB format -> ST_MakePoint(lng, lat) order matters
- Cache vs Postgres consistency -> denormalize cache write strategy + audit Postgres
- GPS accuracy variance 5-50m -> log accuracy_meters + threshold filter
- Battery optimization mobile -> adaptive frequency strict
- Cleanup stale 24h -> cron job idempotent
- ETA real traffic data -> Phase 7+ Google Maps Traffic API integration
- Tenant isolation Redis Geo key per tenant -> namespace strict

### Task 6.1.5 -- Notification Fanout Multi-Channel

**140-180 KB | P0 | 7h** | Dependances : 6.1.4

**Scope** : Fanout top 5 garages parallel push + SMS + email Sprint 9.

**Livrables** : Service `DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, garage_matches: GarageMatch[])` orchestrate :
1. For each garage_match parallel via Promise.allSettled
2. Sprint 9 NotificationRouter integration :
   - Push native Sprint 19 mobile (garage device tokens registered + payload `dispatch_offer_received` + deep link `skalean://dispatch/offer/{offer_id}`)
   - SMS Sprint 9 (Maroc Telecom + Orange + INWI fallback selon operator + content template translated FR/AR)
   - Email Sprint 9 Brevo (template `dispatch-offer-received` FR/AR + sinistre summary + estimation IA Sprint 20 + customer location + ETA calculated + buttons Accept/Reject)
3. Single attempt par garage par offer (no retry)
4. Telemetry `dispatch_notifications_sent_total{channel,result}` + `dispatch_notification_fanout_duration_ms`

Templates Sprint 9 dedicated dans `repo/packages/comm/src/templates/dispatch/` :
- `dispatch-offer-received-push.json` (title + body + data deep link)
- `dispatch-offer-received-sms.txt` FR + AR (max 160 chars chacun)
- `dispatch-offer-received-email.html` FR + AR (rich HTML + buttons CTA)

12+ tests integration.

**Pieges 8** :
- SMS cost MA Maroc Telecom 0.05 MAD/SMS -> 5 garages * 1 SMS = 0.25 MAD per dispatch
- Push delivery rate variable -> SMS fallback si push fail dans 30s
- Email delivery spam folder -> Brevo whitelisted + sender reputation
- Templates i18n FR/AR translation quality -> reviewer arabophone professional
- Deep linking notification tap -> Sprint 19 Expo Router skalean://
- Fanout parallel Promise.allSettled vs all -> allSettled tolerate failures
- Single attempt par garage par offer no retry -> avoid spam garage
- Operator detection MA phone format -> regex prefix +212 6/7

### Task 6.1.6 -- Acceptance Workflow + Timeout + Fallback 3 Rounds

**150-200 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 8h** | Dependances : 6.1.5

**Scope** : Acceptance optimistic locking + timeout 60s + fallback 3 rounds escalation.

**Livrables** : Service `OfferAcceptanceService.acceptOffer(offer_id, garage_id, eta_estimated_minutes)` :
1. Verify offer status='proposed' + lock optimistic Postgres BEGIN TRANSACTION + SELECT FOR UPDATE
2. Verify timeout proposed_at < now() - INTERVAL '60 seconds' -> throw OfferExpiredError
3. First wins UPDATE offer status='accepted' + assigned_at = now() + eta_estimated_minutes
4. UPDATE all other offers same sinistre status='superseded' + responded_at = now()
5. COMMIT transaction
6. Emit Kafka event `dispatch.offer_accepted` payload `{offer_id, sinistre_id, garage_id, eta_estimated_minutes, accepted_at}`

Service `rejectOffer(offer_id, garage_id, reason)` :
1. Verify offer status='proposed' + atomic UPDATE status='rejected' + rejected_reason
2. Emit event `dispatch.offer_rejected` payload `{offer_id, sinistre_id, garage_id, reason, rejected_at}`
3. Trigger check if all offers same sinistre rejected/timeout -> FallbackOrchestrationService.handleAllOffersFailed

Service `expireOffers()` cron job every 30s (frequent enough to catch 60s timeouts) :
```
UPDATE dispatch_offers SET status='timeout', responded_at=now() 
WHERE proposed_at < now() - INTERVAL '60 seconds' 
AND status='proposed'
RETURNING id, sinistre_id, garage_id
-- For each row emit event dispatch.offer_timeout
```

Service `FallbackOrchestrationService.handleAllOffersFailed(sinistre_id)` :
1. Query current round_number from dispatch_offers WHERE sinistre_id ORDER BY round_number DESC LIMIT 1
2. If current_round < 3 :
   - MatchingService.findEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, {exclude_garage_ids: previously_offered_ids}) -> next batch 5 garages
   - Create new DispatchOffer entries round_number = current_round + 1
   - DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages new batch
   - Emit event `dispatch.fallback_round_triggered` payload `{sinistre_id, round_number, garage_ids}`
3. If current_round >= 3 :
   - Emit event `dispatch.all_offers_failed` payload `{sinistre_id, total_rounds: 3, total_offers_attempted: N}`
   - Sprint 16 listener -> SinistreService.escalateToManualReview + Sprint 22 reviewer notification

Sprint 16 listener `dispatch.all_offers_failed` -> SinistreService.escalateToManualReview, 16+ tests integration scenarios race conditions concurrent acceptance.

**Pieges 10** :
- Race conditions concurrent acceptance multiple garages -> SELECT FOR UPDATE strict
- Distributed lock multi-instance API -> Postgres advisory lock OR Redis SETNX
- Timeout 60s precise vs cron 30s granularity -> sufficient for practical purposes
- Fallback round counter increment race -> atomic UPDATE
- All offers failed detection -> count check status='proposed' = 0 + status='accepted' = 0
- Excluded garages previous rounds -> WHERE NOT IN (previously_offered_garage_ids)
- Sprint 16 escalation listener critical -> retry queue persistent
- Customer notification on round triggered -> Sprint 9 customer notification "Recherche garages..."
- ETA estimation by garage -> garage provides on acceptance vs calculated default
- Audit log all status changes -> dispatch_offers append-only enum strict

### Task 6.1.7 -- Integration Sprint 16 Sinistre Lifecycle

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION | 6h** | Dependances : 6.1.6

**Scope** : Listeners Kafka Sprint 16 + Sprint 21 dispatch + integration end-to-end.

**Livrables** : Listener `SinistreReviewListener` Sprint 16 Kafka topic `insurtech.events.sinistre.review` (triggered when sinistre transitions received -> review post Sprint 20 IA estimation completion) :
1. Async invoke MatchingService.findEligibleGarages(sinistre_id)
2. Create DispatchOffer entries for each top 5 garage (status='proposed' + round_number=1 + score_breakdown)
3. DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages
4. Audit log Sprint 7

Listener `OfferAcceptedListener` event `dispatch.offer_accepted` :
1. Verify sinistre_id status = 'review'
2. Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition(sinistre_id, 'assigned', actor='dispatch_system', metadata={garage_id, eta_estimated})
3. Create Assignment entity status='assigned'
4. Emit event `sinistre.assigned_to_garage` consume :
   - Sprint 9 NotificationRouter customer notification "Garage XYZ a accepte votre demande, ETA 25 min" push native Sprint 19
   - Sprint 18 real-time SSE update + Sprint 19 mobile push
   - Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline

Listener `AllOffersFailedListener` event `dispatch.all_offers_failed` :
1. Sprint 16 SinistreService.escalateToManualReview(sinistre_id, reason='all_dispatch_offers_failed')
2. Sprint 22+ reviewer notification high priority
3. Customer notification Sprint 9 "Recherche garage manuelle en cours..."

Error handling exhaustive + retry queues persistent + audit log all transitions, integration tests end-to-end Sprint 16 sinistre.review -> Sprint 21 matching -> notifications -> acceptance -> Sprint 16 assigned -> Sprint 22+ future workflow expertise, 14+ tests.

**Pieges 8** :
- Listener Kafka consumer group offset -> per-instance OR shared depending pattern
- Sprint 16 transition method signature exact -> verify (sinistre_id, status, options) + actor + metadata
- Async vs await blocking lifecycle -> async fire-and-forget + retry queue
- Tenant_id propagation cross-service -> AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6 strict
- Race conditions multiple listeners same event -> idempotency Sprint 15 strict
- Sprint 22+ future workflow not yet implemented -> mock consumer tests
- Audit log Sprint 7 persistence retry on failure -> dedicated audit retry queue
- Notifications customer multi-step (research -> accepted -> ETA updates) -> stateful logic

### Task 6.1.9 -- Customer Preferences + Ratings

**110-150 KB | P0 | 5h** | Dependances : 6.1.8

**Scope** : Customer favorites + ratings post-assignment + rolling average.

**Livrables** : Service `CustomerPreferenceService.toggleFavorite(customer_id, garage_id)` Sprint 8 customer profile extension :
- INSERT garage_preferences (customer_id, garage_id, is_favorite=true) ON CONFLICT UPDATE
- Audit log Sprint 7
- Update Sprint 8 customer.metadata.favorite_garages_count

Service `applyToMatching(customer_id, garage_matches: GarageMatch[])` :
- LEFT JOIN garage_preferences customer_id
- Set preference_score = 1.0 si favorite sinon 0.5
- Maintain matching algorithm scoring weights

Service `GarageRatingService.submitRating(sinistre_id, customer_id, garage_id, rating_1_5, comment?)` :
1. Verify assignment status='completed' for sinistre_id+garage_id
2. Verify customer_id = sinistre.customer_id (anti-fraud)
3. Check no existing rating same (sinistre_id, customer_id) (one rating per sinistre)
4. INSERT garage_ratings + audit log Sprint 7
5. UPDATE garage.rating_average rolling average via materialized view ou trigger Postgres :
   ```sql
   UPDATE garages SET rating_average = (SELECT AVG(rating_1_5) FROM garage_ratings WHERE garage_id = NEW.garage_id), rating_count = rating_count + 1
   WHERE id = NEW.garage_id;
   ```
6. Emit event `garage.rating_submitted` consume Sprint 13 Analytics

Endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/dispatch/assignments/{id}/rate` (customer rate apres completion)
- `GET /api/v1/garages/{id}/ratings` (history paginated public)
- `POST /api/v1/customers/me/favorites/{garage_id}` toggle favorite
- `GET /api/v1/customers/me/favorites` list favorites

UI Sprint 18 + Sprint 19 modal rating post-assignment completion + star rating 1-5 + comment optional + soumission +" Merci !" toast, 10+ tests.

**Pieges 8** :
- Anti-fraud verification sinistre completed + customer verified strict
- One rating per sinistre constraint UNIQUE (sinistre_id, customer_id, garage_id)
- Rolling average performance trigger vs materialized view -> trigger Postgres simpler
- Rating count fast denormalize -> trigger increment
- Customer favorites max 10 -> business rule optional
- UI Sprint 18 + Sprint 19 modal timing post-completion -> SSE event listener
- Comment moderation -> Phase 7+ moderation queue
- Garage owner reply to ratings -> Phase 7+ reply feature

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers ATX, code blocks tag (bash/typescript/sql/yaml/json), tableaux pipes alignes, listes, diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense + professionnel : prose privilegiee, paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, niveau technique senior PostgreSQL + PostGIS + Redis Geo + Kafka + microservices + marketplace patterns + Uber/Doordash reference, terminologie metier consistante (dispatch = matching sinistre-garage, marketplace = 3-sided platform, matching algorithm = scoring weighted, fallback rounds = retry strategy, optimistic locking = race condition handling).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 5 decisions (001-022), **decision-022 obligatoire toutes tasks 6.1.X**, **decisions-016+017 obligatoires** tasks integration Sprint 16 (6.1.7).

# Decision-022 obligatoire toutes

done

done

- `payout_triggers` orchestration sequenced (id + sinistre_id + expertise_id + customer_payout_id Sprint 11 nullable + garage_payout_id Sprint 11 nullable + triggered_at + customer_payout_completed_at nullable + garage_payout_completed_at nullable + status enum pending/customer_processing/customer_completed/garage_processing/completed/failed)

- PayoutTrigger orchestration sequence customer-first -> status transitions strict
- Disputes escalation_level INT CHECK BETWEEN 1 AND 3
- SLA due_at calculation automatic per level (24h/48h/1week)
- RLS tenant_id propagation strict
- Indexes partial pour performance queries actives uniquement
- Seeds reviewers MA-realistic emails + specialties balanced

### Task 6.2.3 -- Expertise Assignment Routing Automatic

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 7h** | Dependances : 6.2.2

**Scope** : Routing automatic reviewers + load balancing + escalation senior + listener Sprint 21.

**Livrables** : Service `ExpertiseAssignmentService.assignReviewer(sinistre_id): ReviewerAssignment` algorithm orchestre :
1. Lookup sinistre Sprint 16 SinistreService.findById + claim_amount_estimated_mad + branche enum auto/sante/RC
2. Query reviewers (status='active' + specialty matches branche + workload < EXPERTISE_REVIEWER_MAX_IN_REVIEW=10)
3. Sort par historical_accuracy_score DESC + workload ASC (load balancing)
4. Si claim_amount > EXPERTISE_SENIOR_ESCALATION_MAD=100000 -> escalateToSenior strict
5. Assign reviewer + create Expertise status='assigned'
6. Emit event `expertise.assigned` + notification Sprint 9 reviewer "Nouvelle expertise XYZ assignee"

Helper `getReviewerWorkload(reviewer_id): number` count expertises status IN ('assigned', 'in_review'), helper `escalateToSenior(reviewer_id, claim_amount)` (query senior_reviewers role + assign highest priority queue + notification high_priority Sprint 9), endpoint `POST /api/v1/expertise/assignments/{sinistre_id}/assign` manual override admin Sprint 22+ permissions `expertise.admin.assign`, integration listener Sprint 21 `dispatch.assignment_completed` Kafka topic -> trigger assignReviewer automatic + retry queue persistent si error, 14+ tests scenarios (routing happy path + workload max + specialty matching + escalation senior > 100k + manual override + load balancing equality).

**Pieges 10** :
- Workload calculation race conditions multi-instance API -> SELECT FOR UPDATE reviewer record + atomic UPDATE
- Specialty matching strict vs flexible -> exact match priority + fallback flexible si none available
- Historical accuracy score baseline new reviewers -> default 0.5 medium until 10 expertises
- Round-robin weighted vs strict round-robin -> weighted by historical_accuracy + workload combined
- Senior reviewers limited inventory -> queue prioritized + alerting si all senior overloaded
- Listener Sprint 21 idempotency -> Sprint 15 Idempotency-Key sinistre_id strict
- Manual override admin audit obligatory -> Sprint 7 audit log + rationale required
- Tenant_id propagation strict -> AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6
- Notification reviewer push native Sprint 19 future -> Sprint 9 templates
- Reviewer wellbeing analytics -> dashboard Sprint 18 workload + burnout indicators

### Task 6.2.4 -- Expertise Review Service + Checklist 8 Items

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 7h** | Dependances : 6.2.3

**Scope** : Review flow + checklist 8 items mandatory + decision approve/reject/dispute + integration sources.

**Livrables** : Service `ExpertiseReviewService.startReview(expertise_id, reviewer_id): ReviewContext` :
1. Verify expertise.status='assigned' + reviewer_id assigned
2. Transition status='in_review' + audit Sprint 7
3. Load review context (photos before/after Sprint 10 + IA estimation Sprint 20 from sinistre.metadata.ai_estimation + customer signature Sprint 10 + garage details Sprint 21 + sinistre details Sprint 16)
4. Initialize checklist 8 items entities expertise_validations (one per validation_type)
5. Return ReviewContext for Sprint 18 UI render

Service `submitChecklistItem(expertise_id, validation_type, result, reviewer_notes?): ValidationResult` :
1. Verify expertise.status='in_review' + reviewer_id assigned
2. UPDATE expertise_validations result + reviewer_notes + validated_at
3. Audit Sprint 7
4. Si validation_type='montant_coherence' AND result='fail' -> trigger IA override required workflow

Service `approve(expertise_id, final_amount_mad, rationale_text): ApprovalResult` :
1. Verify all 8 expertise_validations completed (count=8 + result IN pass/warning, no fail)
2. UPDATE expertise decision='approved' + final_amount_mad + rationale_text + reviewed_at + status='approved'
3. Transition Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition(sinistre_id, 'validated')
4. Emit event `expertise.approved` payload {expertise_id, sinistre_id, final_amount_mad, reviewer_id}
5. Audit Sprint 7

Service `reject(expertise_id, reason): RejectionResult` UPDATE decision='rejected' + transition Sprint 16 + emit event.

Service `dispute(expertise_id, reason, initiator): DisputeResult` create Dispute entity escalation_level=1 + assign second reviewer + emit event `dispute.raised`.

Checklist 8 items (cf B-22) :
1. photos_before_quality (min 5 photos + eclairage + angles + acceptable)
2. photos_after_quality (min 5 photos + comparable angles + acceptable)
3. photos_coherence (vehicule meme + dommages reparation evident)
4. montant_coherence (deviation < 30% IA Sprint 20 acceptable, > 30% override rationale obligatoire)
5. customer_signature (presence + timestamp valide)
6. garage_compliance (facture detaillee + breakdown pieces + main d'oeuvre)
7. delais_respect (assignment completed < 14j defaut EXPERTISE_DELAIS_MAX_DAYS)
8. quality_reparation (visuelle no defaults evidents)

12+ tests scenarios.

**Pieges 10** :
- All 8 checklist items mandatory enforcement -> CHECK constraint Postgres OR service-level
- IA deviation calculation precision -> abs((final - ia) / ia) * 100 percent
- Checklist UI Sprint 18 fatigue -> visual progress + estimated time + breaks
- Customer signature timestamp validation -> > sinistre.completed_at strict
- Garage compliance documents verification -> Sprint 10 documents check + signed
- Delais respect calculation business days vs calendar days -> business days standard MA
- Quality reparation visuelle subjective -> reviewer training + examples library
- Photos coherence detection -> Sprint 20 IA optional helper detection
- Multiple expertises same sinistre -> UNIQUE constraint (sinistre_id, status='in_review')
- Reviewer changes mid-review -> reassignment workflow + audit

**Livrables** : Service `IAEstimationOverrideService.override(expertise_id, reviewer_final_amount_mad, rationale_text, issue_type): OverrideResult` :

2. Lookup sinistre.metadata.ai_estimation Sprint 20 IA estimation montant
3. Calculate deviation_percent abs((reviewer_final - ia) / ia) * 100

6. UPDATE expertise override_ia=true + override_rationale=rationale_text
7. Audit Sprint 7 (expertise_id + ia_estimation + reviewer_final + deviation_percent + rationale + issue_type)
8. Sprint 20 dataset retraining feedback INSERT `ai_estimation_human_overrides` table (tag='human_override' + ia_estimation + human_correction + issue_type + photo_urls_hash) consume Sprint 20 monthly retraining pipeline
9. UPDATE sinistre.metadata.ai_estimation_override JSONB
10. Monitoring `ia_overrides_rate_weekly` (counter overrides / total expertises) alert > 30% Sprint 20 quality issue trigger

10+ tests scenarios.

### Task 6.2.7 -- Payout Customer Sprint 11 Integration

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE PAYMENTS | 6h** | Dependances : 6.2.6

**Scope** : Trigger payout customer Sprint 11 post-validation + retry + fallback + Sprint 13 analytics.

**Livrables** : Service `PayoutCustomerService.triggerPayout(expertise_id): PayoutTriggerResult` :
1. Verify expertise.status='approved' + decision='approved'
2. Verify customer signature acceptance present (sinistre.customer_signature_at IS NOT NULL)
3. Lookup customer_payout_preference Sprint 8 Customer profile (mWallet_bam/bank_transfer/cashplus_pickup)
4. Sprint 11 integration PayoutInstantService.processCustomerPayout(sinistre_id, customer_id, final_amount_mad, payment_method)
5. INSERT PayoutTrigger entity status='pending' + customer_payout_id Sprint 11 reference
6. Audit Sprint 7
7. Notification Sprint 9 customer "Indemnisation X MAD envoyee, methode Y, ref Z" push native Sprint 19 + email Brevo + SMS optional
8. Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline payouts events

Error handling Sprint 11 failure exhaustive :
- BAM mWallet API down -> retry queue persistent exponential backoff (1min, 5min, 15min)
- Circuit breaker Sprint 14 pattern (fail fast after 5 failures dans 1min)
- Fallback bank transfer si mWallet fail definitively (after 3 retries) -> notification customer "Indemnisation envoyee par virement bancaire au lieu de mWallet"
- CashPlus pickup fallback final si bank transfer fail

Listener Sprint 11 event `payout.completed` payload `{payout_id, sinistre_id, status}` -> UPDATE PayoutTrigger.customer_payout_completed_at + emit event `expertise.customer_payout_completed` consume Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService trigger next step.

Listener Sprint 11 event `payout.failed` -> alerting Sprint 9 admin + manual intervention workflow.

14+ tests integration scenarios (happy path + BAM failure retry + fallback bank + CashPlus final + listener completed + Sprint 13 analytics).

**Pieges 10** :
- Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService idempotency -> sinistre_id + customer_id strict
- BAM mWallet API rate limits -> max 100 payouts/min global tenant
- Retry queue persistent vs in-memory -> persistent Redis OR Postgres queue
- Circuit breaker Sprint 14 configuration thresholds -> 5 failures / 60s window
- Fallback bank transfer customer notification -> clear communication different method
- CashPlus pickup customer must travel -> last resort only
- Listener payout.completed timing -> wait max 24h then escalate manual
- Sprint 13 Analytics ETL latency -> async non-blocking
- Customer payout_preference change mid-workflow -> snapshot at trigger time
- Tenant isolation payouts strict -> tenant_id propagation Sprint 6

### Task 6.2.8 -- Garage Compensation Tiered + Sprint 11

**130-170 KB | P0 | 6h** | Dependances : 6.2.7

**Scope** : Garage compensation calculation tiered rules + Sprint 11 payout post-customer + bonus/penalty.

**Livrables** : Service `GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation(garage_id, sinistre_id, work_type, final_amount_mad): CompensationCalculation` orchestre :
1. Lookup garage Sprint 21 (tier basic/verified/premium/partner determined by status + rating + completed_count + contractual Sprint 23 future)
2. Apply tier base percentage :
   - basic 70% (GARAGE_TIER_BASIC_PERCENT=70)
   - verified 75% (verified status Sprint 21)
   - premium 80% (rating > 4.5 + completed_count > 50)
   - partner 85% (Sprint 23+ contractual insurer-specific)
3. Apply bonus +5% (GARAGE_BONUS_ON_TIME_PERCENT=5) si garage completion_at <= assignment.estimated_arrival_at + delta tolerance
4. Apply penalty -10% (GARAGE_PENALTY_LATE_PERCENT=10) si garage completion_at > 50% over estimated_arrival_at
5. Calculate garage_compensation_amount_mad = final_amount_mad * (base_tier + bonus - penalty) / 100
6. Return CompensationCalculation {base_tier_percent, bonus_percent, penalty_percent, total_percent, garage_compensation_amount_mad, breakdown_explanation}

Service `triggerGaragePayout(payout_trigger_id): GaragePayoutResult` :
1. Verify PayoutTrigger.customer_payout_completed_at IS NOT NULL (sequence customer-first strict)
2. Lookup expertise + garage compensation calculation
3. Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.processGaragePayout(garage_id, sinistre_id, garage_compensation_amount_mad, payment_method='bank_transfer' garage default)
4. UPDATE PayoutTrigger.garage_payout_id + status='garage_processing'
5. Audit Sprint 7
6. Notification Sprint 9 garage "Compensation X MAD versee" + email + Sprint 13 analytics

Listener Sprint 11 `payout.completed` garage payout -> UPDATE PayoutTrigger.garage_payout_completed_at + status='completed' + emit event `expertise.workflow_completed` + Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition(sinistre_id, 'finalized'), 12+ tests scenarios tier calculations + bonus/penalty + sequence enforcement.

**Pieges 10** :
- Tier upgrade pendant calculation race -> SELECT FOR UPDATE garage + cache tier 1h TTL
- On-time tolerance delta configurable -> +/- 30min standard MA
- Penalty -10% over 50% late strict vs graduated -> strict for simplicity
- Custom contracts Sprint 23+ override defaults -> hierarchy contracts > tier > defaults
- Garage payment_method default bank_transfer -> garage profile preference Phase 7+
- Sequence customer-first strict enforce -> reject garage trigger si customer not completed
- Compensation amount precision MAD 2 decimals -> Decimal type strict
- Multiple sinistres same garage parallel -> queue per garage avoid double payouts
- Custom contracts insurer-specific Sprint 23+ -> hooks Phase 5 Sprint 3
- Audit compensation breakdown transparency -> dispute resolution evidence

### Task 6.2.9 -- Dispute Resolution 3 Niveaux Escalation

**150-200 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 8h** | Dependances : 6.2.8

**Scope** : Dispute resolution workflow 3 niveaux + SLA monitoring + auto-escalate + ACAPS reporting.

**Livrables** : Service `DisputeResolutionService.raiseDispute(expertise_id, reason, initiator, evidence_data): DisputeResult` :
1. Verify expertise.status IN ('approved', 'rejected')
2. Verify initiator enum customer/garage/insurer
3. Verify dispute window < 14j post expertise.reviewed_at
4. Create Dispute entity escalation_level=1 + status='open' + sla_due_at=now()+24h
5. Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition(sinistre_id, 'disputed')
6. Assign second reviewer (different from original expertise.reviewer_id + specialty matching)
7. Notification Sprint 9 stakeholders + email + push
8. Audit Sprint 7
9. Emit event `dispute.raised`

Service `escalate(dispute_id, manual_or_auto: 'manual'|'auto'): EscalationResult` :
1. Verify dispute.status='open' + (manual OR sla_due_at < now())
2. Increment escalation_level (max 3)
3. UPDATE sla_due_at according to new level (24h/48h/1week)
4. Assign next-level reviewer/manager/ethics-committee
5. Audit + emit event `dispute.escalated`

Service `resolve(dispute_id, decision, rationale): ResolutionResult` :
1. Verify dispute.status IN ('open', 'escalated')
2. Validate decision enum favor_original_approve/favor_dispute_reject/mediation/partial_payout
3. UPDATE dispute resolution_decision + resolution_rationale + status='resolved' + resolved_at
4. Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition based on decision (favor_approve -> validated, favor_reject -> rejected, mediation -> custom)
5. Sprint 11 payout adjustments si applicable (partial_payout calculate new amount + retrigger payouts)
6. Niveau 3 ACAPS reporting si applicable (article assurance MA reportable)
7. Audit + emit event `dispute.resolved`

Niveaux escalation detail :
- Niveau 1 (24h SLA) : reviewer second opinion (different reviewer assigned + review original expertise + decision : confirme original OR overrules)
- Niveau 2 (48h SLA) : senior reviewer + manager review (decision : confirme niveau 1 OR overrules + mediation customer-garage negotiation possible)
- Niveau 3 (1 week SLA) : ethics committee + legal review (final binding decision + ACAPS reporting if applicable)

SLA monitoring cron `expireSLA()` every 30min :
- UPDATE disputes WHERE sla_due_at < now() AND status='open' -> auto-escalate next level
- Alerts Sprint 9 manager + ethics committee
- Manager visibility dashboard

14+ tests scenarios (raise + escalate niveau 1 + niveau 2 + niveau 3 + auto-escalate SLA breach + resolve favor_original + resolve favor_dispute + mediation + partial_payout + ACAPS reporting).

**Pieges 10** :
- Dispute window 14j strict post expertise.reviewed_at -> CHECK constraint
- Second reviewer assignment specialty matching -> reuse ExpertiseAssignmentService routing
- SLA cron auto-escalate atomicity -> SELECT FOR UPDATE
- Niveau 3 ethics committee inventory limited -> dedicated members + on-call rotation
- ACAPS reporting trigger conditions -> documented decision matrix
- Sprint 11 payout adjustments partial_payout -> reverse + new payout (NOT modify completed)
- Mediation customer-garage negotiation manual workflow -> external coordination
- Evidence_data storage Sprint 10 S3 + audit -> documents reusable disputes
- Notification stakeholders selective -> roles per level (customer/garage/insurer/reviewer/manager/ethics)
- Resolution decision impact Sprint 16 lifecycle -> transitions matrix documented

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers ATX, code blocks tag (bash/typescript/sql/yaml/json), tableaux pipes alignes, listes, diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense + professionnel : prose privilegiee, paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, niveau technique senior NestJS + TypeScript + Postgres + Kafka + payments BAM + workflow orchestration + dispute resolution legal MA + ACAPS compliance + ethics human-in-the-loop, terminologie metier consistante (expertise = reviewer human validation, assignment = routing reviewer, checklist 8 items = verification mandatory, IA override = reviewer overrules IA estimation, payout sequencing = customer-first then garage, dispute escalation = 3 niveaux SLA, ACAPS reporting = regulator notification).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 6 decisions (001-023), **decision-023 obligatoire toutes tasks 6.2.X**, **decisions 011+012+016+017+020+021+022 obligatoires** tasks integration Sprint 11 + Sprint 16 + Sprint 20 + Sprint 21.

## WORKFLOW D'EXECUTION RECOMMANDE

```
1. Lire B-22 -- section "Tache 6.2.{Z}"
2. Lire V-22 -- criteres V{N}
3. Lire C-22 -- etapes detaillees
4. Lire 2-3 tasks voisines deja generees
5. Lire decisions/001-023 -- decision-023 OBLIGATOIRE toutes
6. Lire decisions 011+012+016+017+020+021+022 si task integration
7. Template 03 -- 11 sections strict
8. Rediger task-6.2.{Z}-{slug}.md
9. Verifier taille fourchette
10. Verifier no emoji
11. Commit Conventional Commits
```

### Sequencement critique

- 6.2.1 peer-reviewed avant 6.2.2+ (decision-023 + design doc)
- 6.2.3 assignment routing critique reviewer wellbeing
- 6.2.4 checklist 8 items mandatory quality
- 6.2.7 Sprint 11 payouts BAM proof point Demo Day
- 6.2.9 dispute resolution 3 niveaux + ACAPS compliance

## VERIFICATION FINALE APRES GENERATION

```bash
cd 00-pilotage/prompts-taches/sprint-22-workflow-expertise/

ls -la task-6.2.*.md
wc -c task-6.2.*.md
TOTAL_MB=$(wc -c task-6.2.*.md | tail -1 | awk '{printf "%.2f", $1/1048576}')
echo "Total : $TOTAL_MB MB"

grep -P "[\x{1F300}-\x{1F9FF}]|[\x{2600}-\x{27BF}]" task-6.2.*.md && echo "EMOJI FAIL" || echo "No emoji OK"

# Decision-023 obligatoire toutes

  if ! grep -q "decision-023" "$task"; then echo "$task : MISSING decision-023"; fi
done

# Decisions integration obligatoires
grep -qE "decision-011|decision-012|PayoutInstantService" task-6.2.7*.md || echo "task-6.2.7 MISSING Sprint 11"

# Pieges minimum 8
for task in task-6.2.*.md; do
  COUNT=$(grep -cE "Piege [0-9]+ :|piege technique" "$task")
  if [ "$COUNT" -lt 8 ]; then echo "$task : Only $COUNT pieges"; fi
done

# Specific references

### Task 6.3.2 -- Entites Networks + Members + Contracts

- `garage_networks` (id + tenant_id (insurer) + name + description + status enum active/suspended + admin_user_id + tier_auto_upgrade_enabled BOOLEAN + total_garages_count INT + metrics JSONB + created_at + updated_at + tenant_id RLS + version optimistic, UNIQUE (tenant_id, name) strict)
- `network_members` (id + network_id FK + garage_id FK + tenant_id + status enum applied/approved/rejected/active/suspended + applied_at + reviewed_at nullable + reviewed_by nullable + custom_tier nullable enum basic/verified/premium/partner + custom_priority_score 0-100 INT + leave_requested_at nullable, UNIQUE (network_id, garage_id) strict)
- `network_contracts` (id + network_id FK + tenant_id + name + description + version INT + custom_compensation_rules JSONB + effective_from TIMESTAMP + effective_to TIMESTAMP nullable + status enum draft/active/expired + audit_trail JSONB + created_at + updated_at, indexes (network_id+status partial + effective_from desc))

RLS strict tenant_id (network belongs to insurer), indexes performance (tenant_id+status partial + network_id+status members + network_id+effective_from contracts + UNIQUE composite (network_id, garage_id) + UNIQUE composite (tenant_id, name) networks), enums TypeScript explicit (NetworkStatus, MemberStatus, ContractStatus, CustomTier), seeds initial 3 fictional insurer networks (AssurMaghreb-network + Atlas-network + MediAssur-network) + 30 garage memberships (10 per network) + 3 active contracts demo + 5 draft contracts, 16+ tests unitaires.

**Pieges 8** :

### Task 6.3.5 -- Network Contract Service

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE | 7h** | Dependances : 6.3.4

**Scope** : Custom compensation contracts override Sprint 22 + versioning + JSONB schema validation Zod strict.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkContractService` :
- `createContract(network_id, name, description, custom_compensation_rules, effective_from, effective_to?)` validate JSONB Zod schema strict :
  ```typescript
  const compensationRulesSchema = z.object({
    base_percent: z.number().min(50).max(100),
    bonus_rules: z.array(z.object({
      condition: z.enum(['on_time', 'rating_above', 'completed_count_above', 'no_disputes']),
      threshold: z.number().optional(),
      percent: z.number().min(0).max(20)
    })),
    penalty_rules: z.array(z.object({
      condition: z.enum(['late_above', 'rating_below', 'dispute_rate_above']),
      threshold: z.number().optional(),
      percent: z.number().min(-50).max(0)
    })),
    custom_clauses: z.array(z.string()).optional()
  });
  ```
  Version increment automatic + status='draft' + audit
- `activateContract(contract_id)` insurer activates (verify status='draft' OR 'active' transition + previous active expires automatic effective_to=now() + status='active' + audit + emit `network.contract_activated`)
- `lookupActiveContract(network_id, garage_id, sinistre_id): NetworkContract | null` core lookup pour Sprint 22 integration (query SELECT * FROM network_contracts WHERE network_id=? AND status='active' AND effective_from <= now() AND (effective_to IS NULL OR effective_to > now()) verify garage membership status='active' return contract OR null)
- Audit trail complete contract changes Sprint 7 JSONB audit_trail field

Schema JSON example documented :
```json
{
  "base_percent": 75,
  "bonus_rules": [
    {"condition": "on_time", "percent": 5},
    {"condition": "rating_above", "threshold": 4.7, "percent": 3}
  ],
  "penalty_rules": [
    {"condition": "late_above", "threshold": 50, "percent": -10}
  ],
  "custom_clauses": ["exclusive_priority", "quarterly_review"]
}
```

14+ tests scenarios.

**Pieges 10** :
- JSONB schema Zod evolution -> versioning strategy
- Multiple active contracts same network -> previous expires automatic on activate
- Effective_from race conditions microseconds precision -> timestamp(6)
- Custom_clauses string list extensibility documented
- Audit trail JSONB append append -> trigger Postgres
- Lookup performance partial index status='active' + effective_from
- Cross-tenant contract access -> RLS strict
- Bonus rules condition enum strict -> avoid free-form
- Penalty percent CHECK between -50 and 0
- Sprint 22 integration via lookupActiveContract called by NetworkAwareCompensationService

### Task 6.3.6 -- Network-Aware Matching Sprint 21 Integration

**130-170 KB | P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION | 6h** | Dependances : 6.3.5

**Scope** : Extension Sprint 21 MatchingService priority boost network members + dependency injection substitution.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkAwareMatchingService` extends MatchingService Sprint 21 (same interface compatible) modifies `findEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, options?)` :
1. Call super.findEligibleGarages first (inherit Sprint 21 logic)
2. Lookup sinistre.tenant_id (insurer)
3. For each eligible garage in result :
   - Query NetworkMember WHERE garage_id = garage.id AND status='active' AND network.tenant_id = sinistre.tenant_id
   - If member of any insurer network -> boost garage_match.score += NETWORK_PRIORITY_WEIGHT=0.20 configurable
4. Re-sort result by updated score DESC
5. Audit Sprint 7 explicit network boost applied (sinistre_id + garage_id + boost_applied)
6. Configurable env var per tenant via `network_priority_weight` (default 0.20)

Integration Sprint 21 :
- Replace MatchingService import in dispatch.module.ts with NetworkAwareMatchingService :
  ```typescript
  // dispatch.module.ts
  import { NetworkAwareMatchingService } from '@insurtech/garage-networks';
  
  @Module({
    providers: [
      { provide: MatchingService, useClass: NetworkAwareMatchingService }
    ]
  })
  ```
- Maintain interface compatibility for backward compatibility
- All existing Sprint 21 tests still pass

12+ tests scenarios (network member boost + non-network garages baseline + multi-network insurer + cross-tenant invisible + configurable weight).

**Pieges 8** :
- Interface compatibility Sprint 21 strict -> extends class same signature
- Performance impact boost calculation per garage -> batch query NetworkMembers
- Tenant_id propagation AsyncLocalStorage Sprint 6
- Re-sort algorithm preserve stable order
- Boost weight 0.20 vs other scores normalization -> verify max score 1.0
- Cross-tenant invisible strict -> tests negative obligatoires
- Audit log volume excessive -> log only boosts > 0
- Module substitution backward compatibility -> all Sprint 21 tests pass

### Task 6.3.7 -- Network-Aware Compensation Sprint 22 Integration

**140-180 KB | P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION | 7h** | Dependances : 6.3.6

**Scope** : Extension Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService custom contracts override + dependency injection substitution.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkAwareCompensationService` extends GarageCompensationService Sprint 22 modifies `calculateCompensation(garage_id, sinistre_id, work_type, final_amount_mad)` :
1. Lookup sinistre Sprint 16 + garage Sprint 21
2. Check active NetworkContract via NetworkContractService.lookupActiveContract(network_id, garage_id, sinistre_id) for sinistre.tenant_id's networks where garage is active member
3. If contract found -> apply custom_compensation_rules JSONB :
   - `base_percent` from contract
   - Iterate `bonus_rules` evaluate each condition vs garage metrics last quarter (`on_time` based assignment.estimated_arrival_at vs actual_arrival_at, `rating_above` based garage.rating_average vs threshold, `completed_count_above` based garage stats, `no_disputes` based dispute_rate metric)
   - Iterate `penalty_rules` evaluate each condition
   - Apply `custom_clauses` as metadata for audit
   - Return CompensationCalculation `{base_percent_from_contract, bonus_total_percent, penalty_total_percent, total_percent, garage_compensation_amount_mad, rules_applied: [...], custom_clauses: [...]}`
4. Else fallback super.calculateCompensation (Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService tiered defaults)

Helpers :
- `evaluateBonusRule(rule, garage_metrics, assignment)` evaluate condition + return percent OR 0 si not matched
- `evaluatePenaltyRule(rule, garage_metrics, assignment)` evaluate condition + return percent OR 0

Audit Sprint 7 explicit (sinistre_id + garage_id + network_contract_id OR null + rules_applied + total_percent + amount) traceability complete.

Integration Sprint 22 :
- Replace GarageCompensationService import in expertise-workflow.module.ts with NetworkAwareCompensationService :
  ```typescript
  // expertise-workflow.module.ts
  import { NetworkAwareCompensationService } from '@insurtech/garage-networks';
  
  @Module({
    providers: [
      { provide: GarageCompensationService, useClass: NetworkAwareCompensationService }
    ]
  })
  ```
- Maintain interface backward compatibility

14+ tests scenarios (contract found + bonus rules matched + penalty applied + multiple bonuses + fallback defaults no contract + custom_clauses metadata + audit explicit).

**Pieges 10** :
- Interface compatibility Sprint 22 strict
- Rule evaluation order bonus + penalty -> sum all applicable
- Conditions vs garage metrics last quarter -> Sprint 13 ClickHouse query
- Threshold parsing dynamic (rating_above 4.7) -> safe conversion
- Custom_clauses metadata applied vs business logic -> documentation
- Audit log explicit which rules applied -> debugging facility
- Performance lookup contract + metrics -> cache short TTL 5min
- Fallback Sprint 22 defaults edge case no contract no membership -> default tier basic
- Module substitution backward compatibility -> Sprint 22 tests pass
- Multi-network garage same insurer multiple contracts -> latest effective_from wins documented

### Task 6.3.8 -- Tier Auto-Upgrade Workflow

**110-150 KB | P0 | 5h** | Dependances : 6.3.7

**Scope** : Tier auto-upgrade quarterly cron + algorithm + notifications.

**Livrables** : Service `TierAutoUpgradeService` :
- `evaluateMember(membership_id): TierEvaluation` (verify network.tier_auto_upgrade_enabled + lookup garage performance metrics last quarter via Sprint 13 ClickHouse aggregate : completed_count + rating_average + dispute_rate + on_time_percentage + recommended_tier algorithm :
  - basic si completed < 10 OR rating < 4.0
  - verified si completed >= 10 AND rating >= 4.0 AND no_serious_disputes
  - premium si completed >= 50 AND rating >= 4.5 AND dispute_rate < 5%
  - partner si completed >= 100 AND rating >= 4.7 AND on_time > 90% AND custom_clause 'partner_contract' present
- `runQuarterlyEvaluation()` cron `0 4 1 */3 *` first day quarter 04h00 MA (iterate active memberships + evaluate + update custom_tier + audit Sprint 7 + emit `network.tier_auto_upgraded` if changed)
- Manual trigger admin endpoint `/api/v1/networks/{id}/members/{member_id}/tier-evaluate` permissions strict
- Notification Sprint 9 garage owner + insurer admin "Tier auto-upgrade X -> Y"
- Tier downgrade allowed rare scenarios (performance degraded significantly) documented

10+ tests scenarios.

**Pieges 8** :
- Cron schedule timezone UTC+1 MA -> explicit conversion
- Cron idempotency duplicate runs -> idempotency key (network_id + period)
- Performance metrics last quarter Sprint 13 query latency -> cache results
- Tier downgrade business rule controversial -> opt-in network setting
- Custom_clause 'partner_contract' check -> verify NetworkContract clauses
- Notifications batch -> 1 per member changed avoid spam
- Audit log volume quarterly -> Sprint 13 archival
- Algorithm threshold tuning Phase 7+ Optuna

## CONTRAINTES UNIVERSELLES TOUS LES 12 TASKS

### Format strict

Headers ATX, code blocks tag (bash/typescript/sql/yaml/json), tableaux pipes alignes, listes, diagrams Mermaid pour design docs.

### Style narratif

Dense + professionnel : prose privilegiee, paragraphes 6-12 lignes justifies, niveau technique senior NestJS + TypeScript + Postgres + Kafka + microservices + multi-tenant SaaS + marketplace patterns + Amazon Flex/Doordash networks reference, terminologie metier consistante (network = closed marketplace insurer-specific, custom contract = compensation rules override defaults, tier auto-upgrade = quarterly performance evaluation, cross-tenant isolation = data residency strict).

### References strategiques obligatoires

Chaque task minimum 5 decisions (001-024), **decision-024 obligatoire toutes tasks 6.3.X**, **decisions 002 + 022 + 023 obligatoires** tasks integration Sprint 21 + Sprint 22.
